脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lud脛聸k Niedermayer (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, President von der Leyen, your Commission is in office only for half a year, so I guess your speech that was rather analysing the situation and showing the way forward was the proper one, but now time is coming when not speeches, but action will decide if we succeed in the battles that you properly describe.

In months to come here in the House, we will have to show who has courage and who has the wisdom to do the right things. Difficult files will be on the table and we should show who is responsible, who is thinking about the future of Europeans and who is not. We will be voting on the EU-Mercosur Agreement and many others.

The Commission should come with important proposals on how to improve our economy. We still don't have them and we badly need them. And again, the time will come and we will show who is caring about the future of Europeans, about their security and living standards, and who does not.

I know that you are on board, most of our colleagues are on the board. Council should be on board. If we make it, we succeed. If not, we fail.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente da Comiss脙拢o, no seu discurso, referiu que precis脙隆vamos de estar unidos, mas temos de estar unidos em torno das prioridades certas, daquelas que respondem 脙聽s ang脙潞stias de milh脙碌es de cidad脙拢os europeus, e eles t脙陋m sido claros em cada Eurobar脙鲁metro, e tamb脙漏m nas ruas, quanto a Gaza.

Por isso, somos muito claros sobre aquilo que esperamos. Primeiro, os nossos cidad脙拢os elegeram a paz como principal valor e priorizam a defesa na a脙搂脙拢o europeia 芒聙聰 da脙颅 a necessidade da nossa autonomia estrat脙漏gica. Precisamos de poder fazer escolhas e, para isso, precisamos de ser livres. E por isso lhe pergunto:脗聽como 脙漏 poss脙颅vel que o acordo comercial com os Estados Unidos da Am脙漏rica inclua os compromissos que inclui em mat脙漏ria de aquisi脙搂脙拢o de equipamentos militares e de g脙隆s?

Segundo, recordamos que melhorar a qualidade de vida pelo resultado do seu trabalho foi sempre a expetativa, a grande promessa que as democracias europeias fizeram 脙聽s suas classes m脙漏dias 芒聙聰 da脙颅 os ganhos que precisamos de alcan脙搂ar em competitividade para refor脙搂ar o Pilar Europeu dos Direitos Sociais e outros.

E por isso a minha pergunta: porqu脙陋 continuarmos a insistir em que o combate 脙聽s migra脙搂脙碌es se faz por muros e n脙拢o pela regula脙搂脙拢o dos mercados de trabalho? 脙聣 a coer脙陋ncia nos valores que nos est脙隆 a falhar.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fran脙搂ois-Xavier Bellamy (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, quel est l'脙漏tat de l'Union? L'Europe est menac脙漏e de sortir de l'histoire. Fragilis脙漏e, divis脙漏e. Tous ceux qui travaillent et qui font vivre nos pays vivent la r脙漏alit脙漏 d'un appauvrissement qui frappe partout, d'un d脙漏crochage qui d脙漏j脙聽 nous fait perdre la ma脙庐trise de notre destin. Il est urgent de r脙漏agir.

Ce matin, dans votre discours, vous avez annonc脙漏 beaucoup de nouveaux plans, d'initiatives, de textes. Mais l'essentiel reste sans aucun doute de commencer par corriger les erreurs et les d脙漏nis qui nous ont conduits jusqu'ici.

Vous avez parl脙漏 de s脙漏curit脙漏. Commen脙搂ons d'abord par cesser de financer massivement des armements achet脙漏s 脙聽 l'脙漏tranger. Ne plus d脙漏pendre militairement des 脙聣tats-Unis sera la condition pour ne plus subir le chantage commercial qu'ils nous imposent aujourd'hui.

Vous parlez des prix de l'脙漏nergie, mais continuer de financer massivement les renouvelables ne fera qu'augmenter nos d脙漏pendances. Cessons enfin d'exclure des budgets europ脙漏ens le nucl脙漏aire, qui, malgr脙漏 ce qu'en ont dit pendant longtemps les Verts, est souverain et d脙漏carbon脙漏. La Cour de justice a confirm脙漏 ce matin m脙陋me le combat que nous menons depuis des ann脙漏es pour cela. Nous n'avons pas besoin d'un plan pour rendre les petites voitures abordables. Nous avons besoin d'abandonner d'abord la r脙篓gle qui impose de n'acheter que des voitures 脙漏lectriques. Et, ensuite, laissons nos constructeurs innover.

Vous voulez faire de la publicit脙漏 pour les agriculteurs europ脙漏ens. Mais commen脙搂ons par abandonner les coupes massives pr脙漏vues par la Commission dans les budgets qui leur sont dus, et par leur garantir qu'ils ne seront pas la variable d'ajustement du Mercosur.

Vous parlez de lutte contre l'immigration ill脙漏gale. Nous ferons bien s脙禄r le travail au Parlement pour faciliter enfin, par le r脙篓glement sur le retour, les expulsions. Mais il faudra aller bien plus loin.

Apr脙篓s des ann脙漏es pass脙漏es 脙聽 alerter, pendant la derni脙篓re l脙漏gislature, sur une politique largement dict脙漏e par la gauche, qui se pr脙漏occupait bien peu 脙聽 l'脙漏poque de ce que pensait la majorit脙漏 centrale dans ce Parlement comme dans nos pays, nous avons fait un choix clair, mais maintenant il faut des r脙漏sultats.

(L'orateur accepte une question carton bleu)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bert-Jan Ruissen (ECR), 芒聙聹blauwe kaart芒聙聺-vraag. 芒聙聯 Ik heb een vraag aan de heer Bellamy. Hij is er ongetwijfeld van op de hoogte dat we nog steeds wachten op de herbenoeming van de speciale EU-gezant voor de godsdienstvrijheid. Mevrouw Von der Leyen talmt en belooft iedere keer dat die speciale EU-gezant voor de godsdienstvrijheid zal worden benoemd, maar er is nog steeds niemand benoemd. Wat vindt u daarvan? Bent u het er mee eens dat het Europees Parlement maximale druk moet uitoefenen op deze Europese Commissie om die speciale EU-gezant voor de godsdienstvrijheid per direct te benoemen?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fran脙搂ois-Xavier Bellamy (PPE), r脙漏ponse carton bleu. 芒聙聯 Cher coll脙篓gue, je partage enti脙篓rement votre point de vue et j'ai 脙漏t脙漏 d'ailleurs marqu脙漏 d'entendre ce matin dans votre discours, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, que l'Europe devait d脙漏fendre la libert脙漏 de prier.

Je crois que la libert脙漏 de conscience est au c脜聯ur de l'h脙漏ritage europ脙漏en et cette libert脙漏-l脙聽, elle suppose d'脙陋tre d脙漏fendue 脙聽 travers la nomination de ce repr脙漏sentant. Nous comptons sur vous pour avancer rapidement sur cette nomination essentielle.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andreas Schieder (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrte Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Eines der gr脙露脙聼ten Probleme, wenn man die Europ脙陇erinnen und Europ脙陇er fragt, sind die Lebenshaltungskosten, und da sind es vor allem die Wohnungskosten.

Wohnen darf kein Luxus werden, Wohnen ist ein Grundrecht脗聽芒聙聯 dieser Satz muss jetzt auch endlich gebaute, erlebbare, bewohnbare Realit脙陇t f脙录r die Europ脙陇er werden. Mit dem Plan f脙录r erschwinglichen Wohnraum haben wir auch die M脙露glichkeit, das Leben von Millionen Menschen in Europa zu verbessern脗聽芒聙聯 n脙陇mlich jenen Menschen wie Familien, die gezwungen werden, ihre Wohnungen zu verlassen, weil sie sie sich nicht mehr leisten k脙露nnen; Mieter und Mieterinnen, die die H脙陇lfte oder mehr ihres verf脙录gbaren Einkommens f脙录r Wohnen ausgeben; junge Menschen, die 30, 35脗聽Jahre alt werden m脙录ssen, bevor sie erst ausziehen k脙露nnen von zu Hause; und die 400脗聽000脗聽Kinder und Minderj脙陇hrigen, die t脙陇glich obdachlos sind.

Wir haben es nicht nur in der Hand脗聽芒聙聯 es ist auch heute angesprochen worden von Ihnen, Frau Pr脙陇sidentin脗聽芒聙聯, es ist ein zentrales Thema. Es ist aber h脙露chste Zeit, dass wir auch ins Tun kommen, dass wir diesen Plan f脙录r erschwinglichen Wohnraum mit all den wichtigen Ma脙聼nahmen ganz rasch umsetzen. Die Europ脙陇er k脙露nnen es sich nicht mehr leisten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Aodh脙隆n 脙聯 R脙颅ord脙隆in (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, like millions of others in our Union, I believe in Europe. Together we believe in a Europe based on social justice, on human rights and on solidarity 芒聙聯 a Europe that is a voice for good in the world.

President von der Leyen, we do not believe in you. You bow to Trump's bullying, you roll back on climate commitments and, for all your rhetoric, you cooperate with the far right.

And while a genocide rages in Gaza and Israel rips up international law, your response is pathetic. The partial suspension of the Association Agreement you announced today is an insult. This is not a partial genocide: 20脗聽000 Palestinian children are not partially dead!

This is not the Europe the people of Ireland believe in. This is not the Europe they love. That Europe is one that houses its people, protects our planet, strengthens workers' rights, stands against genocide and defends international law!

President von der Leyen, Europe deserves so much better than your presence.

(The speaker declined to take a blue-card question from Se脙隆n Kelly)

脗聽脗聽Bart脜聜omiej Sienkiewicz (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Chcia脜聜em bardzo podzi脛聶kowa脛聡 za solidarno脜聸脛聡 z Polsk脛聟 i za to, co te脜录 dzisiaj wyrazi脜聜a Komisja. To s脛聟 bardzo wa脜录ne chwile. Pierwszy raz w historii NATO pa脜聞stwo cz脜聜onkowskie zosta脜聜o zaatakowane na swoim terytorium powietrznym przez Rosj脛聶. Chc脛聶 powiedzie脛聡, 脜录e Polacy si脛聶 nie daj脛聟 zastraszy脛聡. I wobec tego kryzysu jeste脜聸my zjednoczeni. S脜聜yszeli脜聸cie pa脜聞stwo wszyscy o konfliktach wewn脛聟trz Polski. Chc脛聶 zapewni脛聡 na tej sali wszystkich, 脜录e premier i prezydent zgodnie dzia脜聜aj脛聟 wobec tego kryzysu. Koordynuj脛聟 ze sob脛聟 dzia脜聜ania i jeste脜聸my wobec tego nie tylko nie do zastraszenia przez Rosj脛聶, ale jeste脜聸my tak脜录e nie do podzielenia. I tak b脛聶dzie. Ale te脜录 oczekujemy wsparcia i zrozumienia, 脜录e tylko i wy脜聜脛聟cznie nacisk na Rosj脛聶 jest w stanie powstrzyma脛聡 jej agresj脛聶.

脗聽脗聽Thomas Bajada (S&D). 芒聙聯 Sinjura President, "l-istess Ewropa li tatni l-Erasmus biex nikber u no脛搂lom, illum titkellem fuq l-armi milli fuq il-futur ta' binti". Din hija l-vu脛聥i ta' omm 脜录ag脛搂脜录ug脛搂a Maltija li emmnet fil-pro脛隆ett Ewropew. Imma llum x'qed naraw? 脜禄g脛搂a脜录ag脛搂 li j脛搂ossuhom minsija, familji li qed jit脛搂allew we脛搂idhom, anzjani li jridu jag脛搂脜录lu bejn ikla u saqaf fuq rashom u istituzzjonijiet li jdawru wi脛聥脛聥hom quddiem 脛隆eno脛聥idju u in脛隆ustizzja.

Din mhijiex Ewropa li tispira, mhijiex l-Ewropa tal-pa脛聥i, tal-prosperit脙聽 u tal-ugwaljanza. Sinjura President, 脛搂ares madwarek u isma' li脜录-脜录g脛搂a脜录ag脛搂 tag脛搂na li jridu Ewropa li t脛搂addan lill-popli tag脛搂ha u mhux timliehom bil-bi脜录a'. Ewropa fejn 脛搂add ma jit脛搂alla barra, fejn kull tifel ikollu futur u kull anzjan jg脛搂ix 脛搂ajja denja. Din hija l-Ewropa li jien nemmen fiha. Ewropa ta' pa脛聥i, ta' 脛隆ustizzja so脛聥jali u ta' tama g脛搂al kull wie脛搂ed u wa脛搂da minna.

脗聽脗聽Anna Maria Cisint (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, oggi, Presidente von der Leyen, Lei ha dato spettacolo di equilibrismo, arrampicandosi sugli specchi nel tentativo di accontentare tutti. Una zuppa di sole intenzioni, ma da sei anni Lei 脙篓 al timone e le tragiche conseguenze sono sotto gli occhi di tutti.

Parla di conciliare difesa e transizione verde, dovremmo immaginare carri armati a pannelli solari e proiettili biodegradabili? E mentre invoca il riarmo contro la Russia, la minaccia 脙篓 gi脙聽 qui: quinte colonne islamiste che nelle nostre citt脙聽 gridano "morte all'Occidente!".

La sicurezza strategica non 脙篓 solo militare, ma 脙篓 anche industriale: con il meno 90% di emissioni state condannando le nostre imprese e i nostri lavoratori svendendoli alla Cina.

Lo dica chiaramente: vuole difendere l'Europa o farla inginocchiare a Pechino, continuando le folli politiche green solo per tenere in piedi questa sua traballante maggioranza?

脗聽脗聽Helmut Brandst脙陇tter (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Frau Pr脙陇sidentin von der Leyen, als 脙聳sterreicher m脙露chte ich Ihnen sagen: Sie sprechen 脙录ber den Binnenmarkt; der muss endlich ausgebaut werden, ja. Aber da kann es ja nicht m脙露glich sein, dass dieselben Produkte von denselben Ketten in 脙聳sterreich viel teurer sind als in Deutschland! Also bitte, machen Sie endlich den Binnenmarkt脗聽芒聙聯 die Kommission kann das.

Ein wesentliches Thema: Serbien. Ich war 脙露fter in Serbien zuletzt, und ich muss Ihnen sagen: Viele Menschen sind entt脙陇uscht von Ihnen, weil Sie noch immer zu stark bei dem autokratischen Pr脙陇sidenten Vu脛聧i脛聡 sind, weil Sie sich nicht auf die Seite der Menschen stellen. Marta Kos, die zust脙陇ndige Kommissarin, macht das jetzt脗聽芒聙聯 endlich! Aber die Menschen dort wollen auch von Ihnen, dass klar ist, dass ein derart autokratisches System nichts in der EU verloren hat.

Drittens: Russland. Herzlichen Dank, dass Sie jetzt sagen, dass das russische Verm脙露gen in die Ukraine geh脙露rt; als Leihe zun脙陇chst, aber bitte tun Sie es endlich! Endlich tun Sie es脗聽芒聙聯 wir brauchen das, die Menschen dort brauchen das, und es ist auch gut f脙录r Europa.

脗聽脗聽Vicent Marz脙聽 Ib脙隆脙卤ez (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora Von der Leyen, yo le quiero preguntar si usted se ha hecho hoy una moci脙鲁n de censura a s脙颅 misma, porque usted ha anunciado justo lo contrario de lo que ha estado haciendo en muchos 脙隆mbitos en este a脙卤o.

Usted habla de una Europa fuerte y despu脙漏s nos humilla y nos somete a Trump. Usted habla del cambio clim脙隆tico y luego pacta todas las pol脙颅ticas relacionadas con el cambio clim脙隆tico con los negacionistas de la extrema derecha. Usted habla de vivienda y lo que hace es reforzar el presupuesto en armas y no en vivienda. Y lo m脙隆s grave de todo es que usted, hasta ahora, ha estado callada ante un genocidio. Ha estado callada, hasta ahora, diciendo que no ten脙颅a competencias. Y, de repente, hoy ya ten脙颅a competencias para anunciar acciones parciales, detr脙隆s de las movilizaciones de millones de ciudadanos europeos.

Presidir Europa es liderarla, no ir detr脙隆s de sus ciudadanos, como los de la flotilla que debe usted proteger. Presidir Europa es ser coherente y ejercer sus valores, por ejemplo, actuando igual en Ucrania que en Palestina, actuando, defendiendo los derechos universales en toda Europa y ejerciendo la libertad de Europa en todo el mundo.

脗聽脗聽Estrella Gal脙隆n (The Left). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora presidenta Von der Leyen, Europa est脙隆 en la UCI, y usted lo ha demostrado; y lo est脙隆 por sus pol脙颅ticas, que favorecen a los poderosos y hunden y humillan a los m脙隆s vulnerables.

Usted hoy ha hablado de la independencia de Europa, y la hemos visto c脙鲁mo se humillaba en el campo privado de golf del se脙卤or Trump, donde 芒聙聰sin mandato democr脙隆tico芒聙聰 nos ha dejado vendidos. Lo hemos visto con sus planes para destrozar el Fondo Social Europeo, que era fundamental para mejorar la vida de la gente, y, sin embargo, lo quiere vaciar para financiar la guerra. Ya se lo hemos dicho muchas veces: la seguridad de la ciudadan脙颅a no cabe en un misil, cabe en los derechos sociales.

Lo vemos tambi脙漏n con sus necropol脙颅ticas migratorias, con la creaci脙鲁n de Guant脙隆namos fuera de la Uni脙鲁n Europea con nuestros impuestos, persiguiendo a quienes huyen por poner su vida a salvo. Y, sobre todo, lo vemos con Gaza, un genocidio que usted ni siquiera ha sido capaz de reconocer como tal. Ha puesto encima de la mesa...

脗聽脗聽Alexander Jungbluth (ESN). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Mehr Druck auf Russland, Sanktionen gegen Russland: Es ist immer die gleiche Leier, die wir h脙露ren. In diesem Haus, Frau von der Leyen, d脙录rfen Sie immer noch sprechen, im Wei脙聼en Haus darf jemand wie Sie maximal noch an den Katzentisch.

Viel Aufr脙录stung, viel Krieg脗聽芒聙聯 wissen Sie, welches Wort Sie nicht benutzt haben? Diplomatie! Vielleicht liegt es daran, dass Sie mal Verteidigungsministerin gewesen sind, wobei keiner Ihrer eigenen S脙露hne seinem Land gedient hat. 脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 We can hear only you, Mr脗聽Sieper. Go ahead.

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Jungbluth, k脙露nnen Sie jetzt mal aufh脙露ren, bitte? Also wirklich!

Madam President, dear people of Europe, I would like to thank the Commissioners working closely in my field, especially Commissioner 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧, Commissioner Kos and Commissioner Lahbib, for their constructive and professional cooperation.

Frau Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin von der Leyen, vor genau einem Jahr hatte ich zum ersten Mal das Vergn脙录gen, mit Ihnen zu sprechen, und ich sagte Ihnen damals, dass ich Sie aufgrund eines basisdemokratischen Prozesses nicht gew脙陇hlt habe, aber hoffe, dass Sie mich eines Besseren belehren w脙录rden.

Ein Jahr sp脙陇ter ist mein Urteil gespalten. Auf technischer Ebene haben Sie mich 脙录berzeugt. Ich habe so gut wie allen von der Kommission initiierten Gesetzgebungsvorhaben zugestimmt, aber ich m脙露chte Sie auch ermutigen, sich auf der Ebene der politischen 脙聳ffentlichkeitsarbeit noch zu verbessern. Sie m脙录ssen transparenter vorgehen, Deals eingehen, die Europa nicht schwach stehen lassen, Menschlichkeitsverbrechen klar benennen, egal wo sie stattfinden, und einen st脙陇rkeren Auftritt auf Social Media hinlegen. 芒聙娄

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Tmiem tal-pro脛聥edura 芒聙聹catch the eye芒聙聺)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ursula von der Leyen, President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, thank you very much for a very vivid and interesting debate indeed. I was told I spoke for one and a half hours 芒聙聯 sorry, that was long. We had a debate now for two hours, but Europe deserves to have such a debate and, as I said, it was a very interesting and lively debate.

We have heard you loud and clear. The college has heard you loud and clear. Very clear, also, was that the challenges that Europe faces are coming in different forms and that they come from different directions. And there can be no doubt about any of that, as we have seen again in the last 24 hours, both here in Europe and across the world.

But what was also very clear in this debate here is Europe has agency. Europe has agency, what it now needs, in many of the topics, is to develop the necessary urgency to deliver. If we stay united, we're the ones who can create the conditions for a more prosperous and more secure future.

I want to reiterate what I said already in my speech over and over: I will always be ready to work with you to strengthen the pro-European democratic majority. In my view, it's the only majority in this House that can deliver for Europeans. I will always be ready to bridge our differences and find common ground because it was excellent that we had the debate here today but democracy is only strong not only if we have debate, but when it decides and when it delivers.

Therefore we can, on one hand, always choose to focus on what sets us apart but we should make our difference, on the contrary, the foundation for a stronger European home. I think it is easy to say that Europe does not do enough here, and does too much there, but what the majority of speeches from the pro-European democratic forces was, the tone was encouraging, the tone was progress-oriented.

You asked 芒聙聯 rightly so 芒聙聯 for not only words, but deeds. Yes, you are right. The character of a State of the Union is that we do not only look back on what we have done, but we also sketch the way forward and, of course, at the moment, it is words and not yet deeds. But as I know this Parliament, you will hold me accountable. That is good, because this also shows that Europe is not some abstract entity. Europe is you, Europe is us, every one of us and this, therefore, with conviction I say: 'Long live Europe!'

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sarah Knafo, au nom du groupe ESN. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, nous n'attendons d脙漏sormais plus rien de vous et vous trouvez quand m脙陋me le moyen de nous d脙漏cevoir.

Apr脙篓s la crise de l'脙漏nergie et le fiasco du renouvelable, nous attendions que vous remettiez en cause votre politique, mais vous allez encore plus loin. Apr脙篓s l'explosion du co脙禄t de votre grand emprunt, nous attendions que vous reveniez 脙聽 la raison, mais vous proposez 脙聽 l'instant un budget aberrant de 2脗聽000脗聽milliards de d脙漏penses. Apr脙篓s vos le脙搂ons de morale sur l'脙漏tat de droit, nous attendions que vous transmettiez vos SMS avec Pfizer, comme la justice vous l'ordonne, mais l'脙漏tat de droit, ce n'est pas pour vous, c'est uniquement pour les autres. Apr脙篓s l'脙漏lection de Donald脗聽Trump, nous attendions que votre anti-trumpisme vous conduise, pour une fois, 脙聽 d脙漏fendre nos int脙漏r脙陋ts, mais vous avez c脙漏d脙漏 sans combattre et d脙漏sormais vous signez le Mercosur tout en surr脙漏glementant nos propres activit脙漏s.

Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, en vous regardant, je ne sais m脙陋me pas si vous comprenez ce que je suis en train de vous dire. Vous ne changerez jamais. Une id脙漏ologue restera une id脙漏ologue. Une technocrate restera une technocrate. La seule chose que les peuples europ脙漏ens attendent de vous d脙漏sormais, c'est votre d脙漏part.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Manon Aubry, au nom du groupe The Left. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, cela fait d脙漏j脙聽 un an que vous avez 脙漏t脙漏 r脙漏脙漏lue 脙聽 la t脙陋te de la Commission europ脙漏enne. Un an de trop, pendant lequel vous avez commis des d脙漏g脙垄ts irr脙漏parables. Franchement, aujourd'hui, votre discours ne suffit pas 脙聽 masquer la r脙漏alit脙漏.

Oui, vous vous 脙陋tes rendue complice du g脙漏nocide du peuple palestinien en ne prenant aucune r脙漏elle sanction jusqu'alors. Et vos annonces d'aujourd'hui ne changent rien, car, face 脙聽 un g脙漏nocide, il ne peut y avoir de demi-mesure.

Oui, vous avez capitul脙漏 face au chantage commercial de Trump et sign脙漏, avec le Mercosur, le pire accord de libre-脙漏change, le plus n脙漏faste, qui sera un d脙漏sastre pour nos agriculteurs.

Oui, vous avez persist脙漏 avec l'aust脙漏rit脙漏 budg脙漏taire, qui prend aux pauvres pour donner aux riches, et avez multipli脙漏 les renoncements 脙漏cologiques.

Et vous voulez que cela dure encore quatre ans de plus? Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, au terme de cette ann脙漏e, oui, vous devez partir, et vite, avec toute votre Commission. Ce n'est pas seulement notre groupe qui le demande, mais 60脗聽% des d脙漏put脙漏s europ脙漏ens, 60脗聽% des citoyens europ脙漏ens. Et puisque vous refusez, nous d脙漏poserons une motion de censure contre vous pour mettre un terme 脙聽 votre politique de casse sociale et votre inaction coupable dans le g脙漏nocide 脙聽 Gaza. Nous vous censurons...

(La Pr脙漏sidente retire la parole 脙聽 l'oratrice)

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bas Eickhout, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, President von der Leyen, let's just go back one year. The political guidelines that you published in July last year, they were very balanced. But we have to say, over the last year, we have been disappointed in what was implemented of those political guidelines because the focus was very much on defence, Ukraine and deregulation. Especially on the latter one, it was deregulation 芒聙聯 what you do to the due diligence proposal is deregulation; it has nothing to do with simplification.

Your tone now in the State of the Union is different again. You promise differences, but now we really want to see that you deliver on that. It boils down to real implementation, real proposals, and then you can find us because we are very well aware, we want to work on security, on competitiveness, but in a broader definition, and we will work with you.

Thank you for making also very clear that you want to work with the pro-Europeans. But I do think you still have one minute, Manfred Weber: use that wisely with whom you want to work.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Val脙漏rie Hayer, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, je vous ai expos脙漏 tout 脙聽 l'heure les priorit脙漏s de mon groupe politique. Je voudrais vous faire part d'une remarque.

Il y a une chose qui m'a frapp脙漏, moi, dans ces d脙漏bats, c'est l'hypocrisie 脙聽 l'extr脙陋me droite de cet h脙漏micycle, 脙聽 l'extr脙陋me droite, qui nous fait aujourd'hui la le脙搂on sur Donald脗聽Trump, qui nous tord le bras pour nous imposer des tarifs, qui nous oblige 脙聽 acheter am脙漏ricain, qui sape notre 脙漏conomie. Mais de qui se moque-t-on?

Vous avez 脙漏t脙漏 les premiers supporters de Donald脗聽Trump. Vous 脙陋tes les premiers 脙聽 relayer la propagande MAGA ici en Europe. Alors, si vous vous souciez vraiment de vos 脙漏lecteurs et du sort des citoyens europ脙漏ens dans chacun de vos pays, commencez par arr脙陋ter d'脙陋tre les porte-parole de Vladimir脗聽Poutine. Commencez par arr脙陋ter d'脙陋tre les porte-parole de Donald脗聽Trump. Vous pr脙漏tendez d脙漏fendre les peuples, vous les trahissez.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Assita Kanko, on behalf of the ECR Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, President von der Leyen, the truth is that the left killed the drug report before your plane even took off and then blamed you for standing by the corpse.

The public remains anxious all over Europe and angry, we see it today here in France. People worry about transparency, security and respect for the European values, a more powerful voice at the international stage, protection for businesses, justice and law enforcement, a realistic migration policy. To address these concerns, we cannot afford to be blindsided, cowards or hypocrites.

So, was your speech an appropriate answer today? I have some doubts. I heard a lot of new initiatives. Some of it sounded like more bureaucracy to me. You smuggled the deregulation agenda in there, but it seemed like you had to hide it from the Socialists. You are afraid to lose their support. You accept their blackmail. Why? A centre-right majority is possible in this House. A majority that will reduce red tape, a majority that will protect our citizens, a majority that will set the innovative powers of our private sector free. The future of Europe cannot be secured with the left, but with the centre-right.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Iratxe Garc脙颅a P脙漏rez, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, como espero que este debate de alguna manera sea 脙潞til para la ciudadan脙颅a europea, voy a obviar y no hacer ninguna referencia m脙隆s a la figura del se脙卤or Weber y voy a centrarme en lo que han sido las propuestas de la presidenta Von der Leyen.

He de decirle que, desde el Grupo S&D, vamos a estar vigilantes, cr脙颅ticos y constructivos. Usted hoy ha recogido muchas de las propuestas que este Grupo plante脙鲁, pero no pueden quedar simplemente en un anuncio para que hoy este debate termine en un buen clima, sino que tiene que ser la agenda real de la Comisi脙鲁n para los pr脙鲁ximos meses: queremos calendario, queremos propuestas legislativas, queremos presupuesto 芒聙聰cosa de la que hoy poco se ha hablado芒聙聰, queremos realmente voluntad pol脙颅tica para construir.

Esta Europa se ha construido con la cooperaci脙鲁n de las fuerzas proeuropeas y con el protagonismo de la socialdemocracia. No vamos a excusarnos de nuestra responsabilidad, pero lo vamos a hacer sin ser rehenes de aquellos que quieren robarnos nuestros valores, nuestros ideales y nuestras propuestas. Con responsabilidad, seguiremos trabajando.

脗聽脗聽Jeroen Lenaers, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, President von der Leyen, thank you for setting out your clear vision today and also for reminding us that also in this House, sometimes, we are so busy with debating the small print that the world is writing a new chapter without us, and Europe simply cannot afford this. At a time of war on our continent, of increased global competition, we need to respond with unity and with responsibility.

And yes, only the pro-European democratic forces in this House can achieve this together. But for all the attacks from the Socialists, the Liberals and the Greens today on this issue, we are still waiting for a real commitment on delivering in this agenda, and for us, this is the autumn of truth. Do you choose a competitive Europe, cutting red tape and driving innovation, or do you choose a Europe drowning in bureaucracy and trade war, putting at risk the livelihoods of millions of people?

Do you choose a Europe脗聽... (The President cut off the speaker)

Trebuie s脛聝 ne asigur脛聝m c脛聝 aceste politici nu doar continu脛聝 s脛聝 ofere rezultate, ci sunt 脙庐mbun脛聝t脛聝脠聸ite 脠聶i adaptate noilor provoc脛聝ri 芒聙聯 de la tranzi脠聸ia energetic脛聝 脠聶i digital脛聝, p脙垄n脛聝 la competitivitate 脠聶i securitate alimentar脛聝. Politica de Coeziune trebuie s脛聝 r脛聝m脙垄n脛聝 fidel脛聝 principiilor gestiunii partajate, guvernan脠聸ei pe mai multe niveluri 脠聶i abord脛聝rii teritoriale, pentru a r脛聝spunde realist nevoilor locale. 脙聨n acela脠聶i timp, Politica Agricol脛聝 Comun脛聝 trebuie s脛聝 avanseze urgent spre convergen脠聸a pl脛聝脠聸ilor directe, 脙庐ntruc脙垄t fermierii europeni se confrunt脛聝 cu acelea脠聶i costuri pe pia脠聸a unic脛聝 脠聶i au dreptul la o finan脠聸are echitabil脛聝.

脗聽脗聽Miros脜聜awa Nykiel (PPE), na pi脜聸mie. 芒聙聯 Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo! Dzi脜聸 na terytorium Polski wlecia脜聜y rosyjskie drony. To nie jest ju脜录 odleg脜聜a wojna ani ostrze脜录enia bez pokrycia 芒聙聯 to nasza codzienno脜聸脛聡. Jeszcze wczoraj m脙鲁wi脜聜am, 脜录e Rosja nie zatrzyma si脛聶 na granicy z Ukrain脛聟. Dzi脜聸 mamy na to dow脙鲁d.

Wyra脜录am ogromn脛聟 wdzi脛聶czno脜聸脛聡 polskim s脜聜u脜录bom i rz脛聟dowi za szybk脛聟 reakcj脛聶. Dzi脛聶ki ich czujno脜聸ci i odpowiedzialno脜聸ci Polki i Polacy mog脛聟 czu脛聡 si脛聶 bezpieczniej w tych dramatycznych godzinach. Widzimy, jak kluczowe dla pa脜聞stwa jest skuteczne zabezpieczenie infrastruktury krytycznej.

Musz脛聶 jednak powiedzie脛聡 jasno: w tej sytuacji nie ma miejsca na dwuznaczno脜聸脛聡 czy polityczne gry. Zwracam si脛聶 do prawicy, do tych, kt脙鲁rzy wczoraj krzyczeli: 芒聙聻nie pomagajcie Ukrainie, to nie nasza wojna芒聙聺. Je脜聸li b脛聶dziecie to powtarza脛聡, b脛聶dziemy mieli prawo nazywa脛聡 Was zdrajcami Polski i Europy. Ka脜录de dzia脜聜anie przeciwko wsparciu Ukrainy jest dzia脜聜aniem na korzy脜聸脛聡 Putina.

Apeluj脛聶 o natychmiastowe i dotkliwe sankcje wobec Rosji, o wyeliminowanie jej z areny mi脛聶dzynarodowej. Apeluj脛聶 o dozbrojenie Ukrainy i pe脜聜ne wsparcie techniczne. Tylko w ten spos脙鲁b zatrzymamy agresj脛聶 i obronimy bezpiecze脜聞stwo Polski oraz ca脜聜ej Europy.

脗聽脗聽J脙录ri Ratas (PPE), kirjalikult. 芒聙聯 Toetan komisjoni ettepanekut luua rahvusvaheline koalitsioon Venemaa poolt k脙录脙录ditatud Ukraina laste tagasitoomiseks. Samuti toetan k脙碌nes k脙碌lanud vaadet, et Euroopa peab suuremat rolli kandma Gaza 脙录lesehitamisel ja humanitaarabi korraldamisel. Humanitaarabi peab j脙碌udma inimesteni, pantvangid tuleb vabastada ja kahe riigi lahendus on ainus tee p脙录siva rahuni.

Kindlasti vajame otsustavust kaitses. Venemaa raketi- ja droonir脙录nnakud on oht kogu liidule. On h脙陇davajalik, et Balti riikide ja Poola kaitse oleks tugev ning droonim脙录脙录r saaks reaalsuseks. Aga sellest 脙录ksi ei piisa. Euroopa peab kasutama oma t脙露脙露stuslikku v脙碌imekust ja panustama rohkem julgeolekusse 芒聙聯 peame tootma ja ostma relvastust ning tehnoloogiat ka Ukrainalt.

Euroopa tulevik ei s脙碌ltu ainult julgeolekust, vaid ka majandusest. Energiakriis on n脙陇idanud, kui haavatavad oleme. Vajame kiireid investeeringuid energiatootmisesse ja taristusse. Euroopa peab looma energia脙录hendusi, mis tagavad taskukohase ja usaldusv脙陇脙陇rse energia k脙碌igile.

Eluaseme hind on t脙碌usnud viimase k脙录mne aasta jooksul rohkem kui 20 protsenti, ehituslubade arv on aga langenud. Kodust ei tohi saada 脙陇revuse ja v脙碌lgade allikat. Euroopa peab seisma taskukohase eluaseme eest, see on osa inimv脙陇脙陇rikusest.

Euroopa tulevik s脙碌ltub ka meie tehnoloogilisest tugevusest. Investeeringud rohetehnoloogiatesse, kodumaine tootmine ja ringmajanduse arendamine on strateegiline valik, mis tagab meie s脙碌ltumatuse ja konkurentsiv脙碌ime.

Ainult koos, 脙录htselt ja otsustavalt tegutsedes suudame tagada, et Euroopa j脙陇脙陇b vabaduse, 脙碌iguse ja inimv脙陇脙陇rikuse majakaks.

脗聽脗聽Marcos Ros Sempere (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 La Uni脙鲁n Europea se encuentra en un contexto interno lleno de retos pol脙颅ticos y sociales de mucha envergadura, y en un contexto internacional de gigantes desaf脙颅os debidos a las guerras y las crisis en nuestro entorno. La encrucijada para la Presidenta de la Comisi脙鲁n es, o seguir construyendo la Uni脙鲁n en coordinaci脙鲁n con las fuerzas europe脙颅stas que apoyaron su candidatura, o dejarse llevar por quienes quieren desmantelar las pol脙颅ticas que la propia presidenta propuso, y que se han construido por la 芒聙聹gran coalici脙鲁n芒聙聺 europe脙颅sta, como el Pacto Verde Europeo, la Directiva contra la violencia de g脙漏nero, la Ley de Servicios digitales o el Pacto Migratorio. Los socialistas espa脙卤oles pedimos a la Comisi脙鲁n valent脙颅a y voluntad pol脙颅tica para afrontar los retos que la sociedad europea tiene por delante. La ciudadan脙颅a, especialmente los j脙鲁venes, exige que se garanticen sus derechos, como el acceso a una vivienda digna. Necesitamos un pacto europeo por una vivienda asequible. Los ciudadanos queremos y necesitamos la paz. Por eso le pedimos a la Comisi脙鲁n que act脙潞e y se quede en el lado correcto de la Historia, como est脙隆 haciendo el Gobierno de Espa脙卤a ante el genocidio que se est脙隆 cometiendo contra el pueblo palestino. Los socialistas seguimos comprometidos con la construcci脙鲁n de la Uni脙鲁n Europea.

脗聽脗聽Rosa Serrano Sierra (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 La Uni脙鲁n Europea se encuentra en un contexto interno e internacional marcado por los retos pol脙颅ticos, guerras y las crisis en nuestro entorno.

La encrucijada para la Presidenta de la Comisi脙鲁n es, o seguir construyendo la Uni脙鲁n en coordinaci脙鲁n con las fuerzas europe脙颅stas que apoyaron su candidatura, o se deja llevar por quienes quieren desmantelar las pol脙颅ticas que la propia presidenta propuso, y que se han construido por la 芒聙聹gran coalici脙鲁n芒聙聺 europe脙颅sta, como el Pacto Verde Europeo, la Directiva contra la violencia de g脙漏nero, la Ley de Servicios digitales o el Pacto Migratorio.

Los socialistas espa脙卤oles pedimos a la Comisi脙鲁n y especialmente a su Presidenta, valent脙颅a y voluntad pol脙颅tica para afrontar los retos que la sociedad europea tiene por delante.

La ciudadan脙颅a exige que se garanticen sus derechos, como el acceso a una vivienda digna. Necesitamos ya un pacto europeo por una vivienda asequible.

Los ciudadanos queremos y necesitamos la paz. Por eso le pedimos a la Comisi脙鲁n que act脙潞e y se quede en el lado correcto de la Historia, como est脙隆 haciendo el Gobierno de Espa脙卤a, ante el genocidio que se est脙隆 cometiendo contra el pueblo palestino.

脗聽脗聽Georgiana Teodorescu (ECR), 脙庐n scris. 芒聙聯 Politicianul Ursula von der Leyen promite multe, dar cifrele o contrazic:

1. Comisia Europeana vrea sa vina in sprijinul IMM-urilor, sau cel pu脠聸in a脠聶a se laud脛聝. Realitatea se vede clar in modul in care au fost alocate fondurile din viitorul buget multianual. Pactul pentru o industrie curata prevede o alocare de 100 miliarde euro pentru marile corporatii, in timp ce IMM-urile beneficiaza de o alocare de doar 500 milioane de euro. Matematic, asta inseamna 1302 euro pentru un angajat intr-o multinationala si doar 6 euro pentru un angajat intr-o intreprindere mica sau mijlocie.

2. Alt studiu de caz: agricultura europeana. Dupa protestele masive de anul trecut, Comisia a venit cu un set de masuri pentru fermieri si cu o promisiune ferma: ca va transforma agricultura intr-un sector strategic. Cum explica Comisia fermierilor faptul ca in actualul CFM sectorul primeste 0.40% din bugetul UE, iar in propunerea pentru viitorul CFM agriculturii i se aloca doar 0.15%? Romania, de exemplu, ar pierde 17.4 miliarde de euro, asta in timp ce ni se cere dublarea contributiei nationale la cofinantare, de la 15% la 30%, o povara insuportabila pentru un sector agricol deja fragil.

Drag脛聝 Comisie, e timpul pentru fapte, vorbe am auzit destule!

3. Resumption of the sitting
Video of the speeches
脗聽脗聽

(The sitting resumed at 13:11)

脗聽

4. Negotiations ahead of Parliament芒聙聶s first reading (Rule 72) (action taken)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The President has received a request for a vote in Parliament from the EPP Group, reaching at least the medium threshold, on the decision of the AGRI Committee to enter into negotiations on the following file:

'Cooperation among enforcement authorities regarding unfair trading practices in the agri-food supply chain'

The vote will be held tomorrow.

Concerning the decision of the PECH Committee to enter into institutional negotiations pursuant to Rule 72(1), announced at the opening of the session on Monday 8 September, the President has received no request for a vote in Parliament by Members or political groups reaching at least the medium threshold.

The Committee may therefore start negotiations.

I would like to inform the House that I have received a request for a point of order, and will give the floor to Lukas Sieper.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, dear people of Europe, pursuant to Rule 10(3) of our Rules of Procedure, I would like to raise a point of order concerning the behaviour of our colleague Ms Iovanovici 脠聵o脠聶oac脛聝 during the address of the honourable Maia Sandu, President of the Republic of Moldova. This House has already shown its dismay through its vocal reaction in the Chamber, but such behaviour deserves a formal objection.

While our President was ensuring the dignified departure of our guests, Ms 脠聵o脠聶oac脛聝 shouted that Moldova is part of Romania. To make such a pseudo-historic, imperialistic claim in the presence of a head of state of a sovereign European nation, formally recognised as such by all UN member states, especially Romania, is inappropriate behaviour that is unworthy of this Chamber.

Moldova deserves our respect. Moldova is not Romania. Moldova is Europe, just like Romania.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much. The President already took note of this.

I have received another request for a point of order, and will give the floor to Michael Gahler.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, I rise on the basis of Rule脗聽194 of the Rules of Procedure.

During yesterday's vote, a widespread human error and irritation in the voting procedure caused a mistake which leads to the international irritation and questions for clarification addressed to our House by other parliaments and diplomatic missions.

An amendment originally tabled by The Left Group was adopted, accusing the European Union of pursuing a militaristic policy towards Ukraine. This is now paragraph脗聽2 of the adopted Ukraine text. I speak on behalf of the 70脗聽% majority having adopted the Ukraine report yesterday, coming mainly from the political groups of EPP, S&D, ECR, Renew and the Greens, and I declare the language of paragraph脗聽2脗聽does not represent the position of the European Parliament as expressed in all other paragraphs of the Ukraine report.

We have not changed our position on Ukraine. We stand with Ukraine and we will continue to do so. The incoming corrections of the votes from the Members of our political groups will clearly show there is no majority support of paragraph脗聽2脗聽in this House. The Ukraine report should, in future, be considered as having been adopted without paragraph脗聽2.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you, Mr Gahler, for this clarification.

Furthermore, I have received a request for a point of order from Patryk Jaki. You have the floor.

脗聽脗聽Jacek Ozdoba (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca, sytuacja jest nag脜聜a. W Polsce spad脜聜y drony rosyjskie, a Pani m脙鲁wi, 脜录e w poniedzia脜聜ek ustalono temat debaty. 脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much for this. We will take the point, as we already did before, and we will discuss it also with the President.

It's not that we are ignoring what happened. The question is: is it possible at the moment? Under these Rules, we have to make an additional agenda point. That's not possible at the moment under the Rules that are mentioned and cited. So we have to check that with the President.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jean-Paul Garraud, auteur. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Liana. Lisa. Deux pr脙漏noms. Deux destins bris脙漏s. Deux jeunes filles europ脙漏ennes, l'une Ukrainienne, r脙漏fugi脙漏e en Allemagne, l'autre N脙漏erlandaise. Toutes deux victimes d'une barbarie que nos soci脙漏t脙漏s n'auraient jamais d脙禄 laisser s'installer. Deux noms que nous devons refuser d'oublier.

Liana avait 16脗聽ans. Elle avait fui la guerre. Elle cherchait la paix. Ce qu'elle a trouv脙漏, c'est la mort. L'脙漏t脙漏 dernier, un migrant irakien, sous le coup d'un ordre d'expulsion jamais ex脙漏cut脙漏, l'a pouss脙漏e sous un train. Quelques instants plus t脙麓t, elle appelait son grand-p脙篓re, apeur脙漏e. Le dernier son qu'il a entendu, ce sont les cris d脙漏chirants de sa petite-fille.

Lisa avait 17脗聽ans. Il y a quelques jours, elle rentrait d'une soir脙漏e 脙聽 Amsterdam. Inqui脙篓te d'脙陋tre suivie, elle alertait la police. Les forces de l'ordre, arriv脙漏es sur place, d脙漏couvrent le corps sans vie de la jeune fille, gisant dans un foss脙漏, la gorge tranch脙漏e. Le meurtrier pr脙漏sum脙漏? Un pensionnaire d'un centre de r脙漏fugi脙漏s tout proche, d脙漏j脙聽 soup脙搂onn脙漏 de viol quelques jours auparavant.

Ces faits atroces ne sont malheureusement pas isol脙漏s. Ils ne sont pas des faits divers, comme le pr脙漏tendent certains. Ces violences aux femmes, devenues le quotidien de nos filles, de nos m脙篓res, de nos 脙漏pouses, ces violences sont le fruit direct d'une politique migratoire irresponsable impos脙漏e par l'Union europ脙漏enne et certains 脙聣tats membres depuis des d脙漏cennies, et accept脙漏e par une caste d脙漏connect脙漏e qui sacrifie nos enfants sur l'autel de l'id脙漏ologie.

En 2019, en 脙聨le-de-France, 63脗聽% des agressions sexuelles dans les transports ont 脙漏t脙漏 commises par des 脙漏trangers. Ces chiffres n'incluent m脙陋me pas ceux qui ont acquis la nationalit脙漏 fran脙搂aise. 脙聙 Paris, en 2023, sur l'ensemble des viols commis dans la rue, 脙漏lucid脙漏s ou connus, 77脗聽% des personnes interpell脙漏es脗聽sont des 脙漏trangers. En Allemagne, les viols de Cologne en 2016 ont 脙漏t脙漏 perp脙漏tr脙漏s majoritairement par des migrants nord-africains. Au Royaume-Uni, plus de 4脗聽000脗聽enfants ont 脙漏t脙漏 abus脙漏s par des gangs indo-pakistanais pendant plus de 30脗聽ans, avec la complicit脙漏 du silence m脙漏diatique et politique. Un v脙漏ritable scandale que l'on pr脙漏f脙篓re ignorer.

La situation dans nos prisons en France confirme ce d脙漏sastre. En mars 2025, le ministre de l'int脙漏rieur en exercice indiquait que plus de 19脗聽000脗聽d脙漏tenus 脙漏trangers 脙漏taient incarc脙漏r脙漏s en France, ce qui repr脙漏sente 24,5脗聽% de l'ensemble de la population carc脙漏rale, soit une repr脙漏sentation trois fois sup脙漏rieure 脙聽 leur poids d脙漏mographique. Je ne viens de citer que des faits, chiffr脙漏s, sourc脙漏s, v脙漏rifiables.

Le d脙漏ni face 脙聽 cette r脙漏alit脙漏 rel脙篓ve d脙漏sormais de la malhonn脙陋tet脙漏 intellectuelle. Nous focaliser dans ce d脙漏bat sur la question migratoire ne signifie pas que nous nions les violences intrafamiliales et conjugales, qui sont un v脙漏ritable fl脙漏au et un d脙漏chirement pour de nombreuses familles. Mais se focaliser uniquement dessus en ignorant les violences commises dans la sph脙篓re publique est tout autant irresponsable. La situation dans les transports en commun, dans les rues, dans les quartiers entiers sur notre continent, n'est plus tenable. Et pendant ce temps, que fait l'Union europ脙漏enne?

Elle finance des ONG qui aident toujours plus de migrants 脙聽 rejoindre l'Europe, m脙陋me ceux li脙漏s 脙聽 des r脙漏seaux islamistes. Elle applique le pacte sur la migration et l'asile, qui veut r脙漏partir ces migrants sur le sol europ脙漏en. Elle subventionne des projets id脙漏ologiques, comme celui d'un Coran europ脙漏en 脙聽 10脗聽millions d'euros, oubliant sans doute la place d脙漏gradante que les islamistes offrent 脙聽 la femme. La Commission europ脙漏enne va jusqu'脙聽 financer des campagnes de communication o脙鹿 apparaissent des fillettes voil脙漏es, pendant que le r脙漏gime iranien des mollahs met 脙聽 mort des jeunes filles parce qu'elles retirent leur hijab.

Le temps presse. Si nous ne changeons pas de cap, nos soci脙漏t脙漏s basculeront. Ce n'est pas une question de statistiques, mais une urgence vitale pour notre civilisation, nos pays, nos peuples, nos filles. Quelle Europe voulons-nous leur laisser? Une Europe protectrice, s脙禄re, libre, ou bien une Europe soumise au chaos et 脙聽 la charia?

L'immigration massive, l脙漏gale comme ill脙漏gale, en provenance de pays musulmans constitue un danger de mort pour nos libert脙漏s et en premier lieu pour celle des femmes. Du c脙麓t脙漏 des Patriotes, le choix est fait. Nous continuerons tant que n脙漏cessaire 脙聽 agir pour mettre fin 脙聽 cette anarchie migratoire, 脙聽 l'entrisme islamiste au sein de nos institutions et de nos soci脙漏t脙漏s. Nous voulons garantir un avenir impr脙漏gn脙漏 de libert脙漏 et de s脙漏curit脙漏 pour nos concitoyens, et en particulier pour les femmes en Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, Commissioner, Europe has a problem with irregular migration. It is a European challenge that needs a European response. There is a broad agreement in the Council that we must keep building a comprehensive migration policy that protects our borders, gives protection only to those entitled and prevents the loss of migrants' lives.

In recent years, a lot has been done to address these challenges. The Pact on Asylum and Migration was a historic result. It provides clear rules and faster handling of asylum applications and more solidarity between Member States. Thanks to the reformed Eurodac, we can now have the means to better track movements of migrants and asylum seekers, reducing secondary movements between Member States.

The Schengen Borders Code has also been updated. The Schengen area is now more resilient to crises and we have reinforced our external border control. The role of Frontex has been strengthened, especially in the area of returns. This trend will continue.脗聽The screening procedure under the new Pact will ensure that vulnerable third-country nationals are identified early. Also, the necessary data will be registered before asylum or return procedures are started.脗聽All of this said, the success of the reforms will depend on the correct implementation.

Madam President, honourable Members, Commissioner, cooperation with countries outside the EU is also essential. The new Pact on Migration and Asylum focuses on cooperation with origin and transit countries. In order to deal with migratory pressure on the European Union, the Heads of State and Government have called for partnerships with those countries. These partnerships address the root causes of migration and counter irregular departures. They should also make returns and readmissions more efficient. Currently, the EU has concluded comprehensive partnerships with key third countries around and near the Mediterranean Sea, such as Tunisia, Jordan, Syria and Egypt, as well as the Joint Declaration on Migration with Mauritania.

The work at EU level and by Member States has shown results, and the number of irregular border crossings in 2024 decreased notably compared to 2023. A final word on the important role of EU agencies in migration management: the support provided by agencies such as Frontex and the EUAA to Member States is essential to address irregular migration to the EU together.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andrius Kubilius, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Minister, dear colleagues, I would like to present the Commission position, which was supposed to be presented by Commissioner Brunner, but because of agenda issues, I will do that.

Once the migration crisis of 2015 struck, more than 1 million people arrived in the EU seeking our protection. Our immigration and asylum systems were simply not designed to cope with such a large scale crisis. They were fragmented, not modern enough, and lacked a truly European response. We did not have the structures, the coordination or the solidarity mechanisms in place to cope.

Today we are in a very different situation, not because migration pressure has disappeared, but mainly because we have fundamentally changed the way we respond. Thanks to the twin-track approach followed by the Commission combining operational action on the ground with legislative reform, we are seeing results.脗聽The numbers speak for themselves. From January to August this year, illegal border crossings decreased by 23 % compared to the same period last year. Applications for international protection also went down by 23 % in the first half of 2025.

But of course, we cannot control every factor driving migration. But what we can control is our capacity to manage it. And today we are putting in place a stable, predictable and resilient migration management system, one that is able to deal with whatever challenges may come.

Five years ago, the Commission proposed a comprehensive pact on migration and asylum and together with the Council and this Parliament worked to reach a historic agreement. With the pact, we are equipping our Union with a means to act, showing that migration can be properly managed. That we, and not the criminal smuggling networks, decide who enters the EU and on what grounds. Since its adoption last year, we have worked with the Member States for the pact to make a difference on the ground.

Next month, we will launch the first annual migration management cycle.脗聽In parallel, with our proposals on safe countries of origin and the safe country concept, we are accelerating the implementation of some aspects of the pact. And to complete the puzzle, we have proposed a new return regulation for a more coherent and efficient return system that respects fundamental rights.

While the pact brings our European House in order, it is equally important to do better in the external dimension. On 10 December, the Commission will host the second conference on the Global Alliance to Counter Migrant Smuggling. We need to enter into a new era of migration diplomacy where the EU combines visas and economic incentives, but also cooperation partnerships that create win-win situations, like our partnerships with Egypt, Mauritania and Tunisia, which have already impacted migration flows.

The crisis of 2015/16 was a wake up call for Europe. It was the biggest demonstration of the need for a proper and common European migration management system. We have woken up putting in place pragmatic solutions that will make our systems more resilient, not just for today, but for the long run.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomas Tob脙漏, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Europe needs strong borders and a controlled migration policy. This requires a unified approach with clear and strict rules. Ten years ago, Europe failed to manage the migration crisis. Our laws were ineffective and Europe was fragmented. We cannot keep repeating those mistakes. That is why the adoption of the migration pact in the last mandate was such a crucial step forward, breaking years of political deadlock and enabling us to start taking back control of migration.

But colleagues, let's be honest: if the far right or the far left had had their way, we would not have a common approach. We would still be in the same broken system. I think the citizens can rightfully demand that Europe has a stronger response to migration, and that is why the EPP is committed to protecting our borders, returning those who do not have a right to stay in Europe, and strengthening control.

It is easy to criticise from the sidelines. It is harder to deliver real solutions. But that is what the citizens expect and deserve, and that is what we will keep delivering.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Catarina Mendes, em nome do Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, caros colegas, a primeira nota que quero dar 脙漏 que este debate 脙漏 contr脙隆rio 脙聽 constru脙搂脙拢o do projeto europeu, contr脙隆rio aos valores da Uni脙拢o Europeia, porque ele 脙漏, em si mesmo, um t脙颅tulo e um debate 芒聙聰 como aqui ouvimos pela apresenta脙搂脙拢o do proponente 芒聙聰 que instiga 脙聽 divis脙拢o, ao 脙鲁dio e ao preconceito, e isso vai contra todos os valores da Uni脙拢o Europeia.

E contra as perce脙搂脙碌es e perante as perce脙搂脙碌es, aquilo que o proponente aqui veio fazer foi dizer um conjunto de perce脙搂脙碌es, generalizar situa脙搂脙碌es. Primeiro, o Estado de direito existe para todos, e quem comete crimes, sejam nacionais ou sejam imigrantes, deve ser punido perante a lei e perante o sistema judicial.脗聽Segundo, o Eurostat e a OCDE t脙陋m dito, ao longo dos tempos, que n脙拢o h脙隆 uma liga脙搂脙拢o direta entre imigra脙搂脙拢o e criminalidade. Terceiro, est脙隆 provado que, na esmagadora maioria dos Estados, a viol脙陋ncia dom脙漏stica ou as viola脙搂脙碌es s脙拢o praticadas essencialmente por nacionais.

脙職ltima nota, Senhora Presidente, a imigra脙搂脙拢o irregular baixou 38脗聽% em 2024, o que significa que, perante as perce脙搂脙碌es, vamos sempre ter os factos e a verdade.

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio T脙垄nger Corr脙陋a, em nome do Grupo PfE. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, n脙鲁s, em Portugal, sempre fomos a favor da imigra脙搂脙拢o, sempre tivemos imigra脙搂脙拢o desde a funda脙搂脙拢o do pa脙颅s, h脙隆 quase 900 anos.

脗聽脗聽Fabienne Keller, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire脗聽Kubilius, Madame la Ministre des affaires europ脙漏ennes, ch脙篓re Marie脗聽Bjerre, chers coll脙篓gues, je r脙漏p脙篓te ce que j'ai dit lundi lors de mon rappel au r脙篓glement int脙漏rieur: ce d脙漏bat mis 脙聽 l'agenda par l'extr脙陋me droite est un faux d脙漏bat, qui instrumentalise les peurs et manipule les chiffres.

脗聽脗聽M脙漏lissa Camara, au nom du groupe Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, prot脙漏ger les femmes et les enfants? Oui, de l'extr脙陋me droite.

Prot脙漏ger, ce n'est pas exclure. Prot脙漏ger, c'est garantir la sant脙漏, l'脙漏galit脙漏 et la libert脙漏. Quand l'extr脙陋me droite avance, ne soyons pas dupes, nos droits reculent. Et nous, f脙漏ministes, nous serons toujours l脙聽 pour d脙漏fendre nos droits face 脙聽 l'internationale r脙漏actionnaire.

脗聽脗聽Irene Montero, en nombre del Grupo The Left. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, cuando una mujer denuncia una agresi脙鲁n sexual, ustedes, la extrema derecha, nos llaman locas, mentirosas, putas, 脗芦feminazis脗禄.脗聽Dicen que buscamos fama o dinero, que algo habremos hecho, que vamos provocando y as脙颅 ning脙潞n hombre se puede resistir a violarnos, que la culpa es nuestra por ir borrachas.

Ustedes hablan de denuncias falsas, publican nuestras fotos y la direcci脙鲁n de nuestras casas.脗聽Ustedes llaman 脗芦divorcio duro脗禄 a la violencia de g脙漏nero. Defienden a los maltratadores que ejercen violencia econ脙鲁mica, a los que ponen todo su empe脙卤o en hacer da脙卤o a las madres y darles donde m脙隆s les duele. Ustedes defienden poner en riesgo a ni脙卤os y a ni脙卤as oblig脙隆ndoles a vivir con sus padres maltratadores.

Ustedes tapan las agresiones sexuales de miles de curas a ni脙卤os y ni脙卤as en Espa脙卤a.脗聽Ustedes miran para otro lado cuando asesinan a un chico al grito de 脗芦maric脙鲁n脗禄.脗聽Y ustedes dicen que el problema no es la violencia machista, sino los pobres hombres a los que el feminismo est脙隆 destrozando la vida con denuncias falsas.

D脙漏jennos en paz: no somos sus mujeres, no somos su propiedad ni un objeto propiedad de nadie. Precisamente, porque se creen que somos su propiedad, nos agreden y nos asesinan diciendo: 脗芦La mat脙漏 porque era m脙颅a脗禄.

No son las personas migrantes las que nos ponen en peligro. Son ustedes: racistas, machistas y fascistas.

脗聽脗聽Ewa Zaj脛聟czkowska-Hernik, w imieniu grupy ESN. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! W Niemczech ostatnio imigrant wepchn脛聟脜聜 szesnastolatk脛聶 pod poci脛聟g. Nielegalna imigracja na teren pa脜聞stw Unii Europejskiej utrzymuje si脛聶 na bardzo wysokim poziomie. Mamy rocznie kilkaset tysi脛聶cy nielegalnych przekrocze脜聞 granicy. Pa脜聞stwa nie poradzi脜聜y sobie z efektami rekordowego roku 2015, kiedy ponad milion osiemset tysi脛聶cy os脙鲁b nielegalnie przekroczy脜聜o granic脛聶 Unii Europejskiej. Przez ostatnie 10 lat nie ma rozlicze脜聞 politycznych za t脛聶 katastrof脛聶, za rosn脛聟ce zagro脜录enia dla strefy Schengen i istotne powi脛聟zania przemytnik脙鲁w z lewicowymi organizacjami pozarz脛聟dowymi.

Ile 脜录y脛聡 musi jeszcze zosta脛聡 po脜聸wi脛聶conych, 脜录eby lewicowy mainstream nie wspiera脜聜 obecnej patologii? Dlatego czas najwy脜录szy powo脜聜a脛聡 komisj脛聶 脜聸ledcz脛聟 Parlamentu Europejskiego do spraw nielegalnej imigracji w Unii Europejskiej, by wyja脜聸ni脛聡, dlaczego Unia Europejska nie chce poradzi脛聡 sobie z masow脛聟 legaln脛聟 i nielegaln脛聟 imigracj脛聟.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jeroen Lenaers (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, op 19脗聽augustus in Nederland werd Lisa, 17脗聽jaar oud, op gruwelijke wijze vermoord. Een verdachte werd gearresteerd in een asielzoekerscentrum. Een jong leven weggerukt, een gezin gebroken. Dit is meer dan een persoonlijke tragedie. Dit raakt aan ons gevoel van veiligheid en aan de fundamenten van onze samenleving.

Maar juist in zulke momenten van groot verdriet en diepe emotie, moeten we oppassen voor al te politieke reflexen. Van de uiterst rechtse zijde klinkt een framing脗聽die angst zaait en migratie reduceert tot een bedreiging voor onze vrouwen en kinderen. En daarmee speel je mensen tegen elkaar uit zonder echte oplossingen te bieden. Tegelijkertijd wordt aan de linkerkant te vaak gedaan alsof migratie slechts een opvang is uit solidariteit. Alsof er geen grenzen mogen zijn aan opvang, huisvesting of integratie. Alsof de draagkracht van onze samenleving onbeperkt is. Ook daarmee worden de zorgen van veel burgers te gemakkelijk terzijde geschoven.

Wij nemen veiligheid en draagkracht serieus, maar blijven tegelijkertijd trouw aan die menselijke waardigheid. We willen snelle en rechtvaardige procedures en een integratiebeleid dat echt werkt. Wie in Europa veiligheid zoekt, moet die veiligheid ook respecteren. En wie onveiligheid veroorzaakt, moet daarvoor direct de gevolgen dragen. Ja, we hebben een opdracht om onze vrouwen en kinderen beter te beschermen. Als vrouwen zich niet veilig voelen op onze straten, dan verliezen we als samenleving de kern van onze vrijheid. We kunnen migratie niet reduceren tot enkel angst en we kunnen ook niet wegkijken waar veiligheid in het geding is.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lina G脙隆lvez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, la identificaci脙鲁n que hace la extrema derecha de la inmigraci脙鲁n con la violencia contra las mujeres y las ni脙卤as es simplemente falsa. No se basa en ninguna evidencia y hay much脙颅simos estudios que la desmienten. Piensen solo en Pelicot y sus colegas, si vinieron o no vinieron en patera. Pero claro, la ignorancia es la base del racismo.

Su discurso genera desinformaci脙鲁n y es un discurso de odio nauseabundo, que incita al miedo de la violencia contra las mujeres que ustedes mismos niegan. Es que ustedes niegan que exista una violencia estructural contra las mujeres, tapan su propia violencia machista.

Ustedes han sido muy h脙隆biles en atraer una parte del electorado masculino, pero, claro, el femenino se les resiste y de ah脙颅 que nos intenten conquistar con estas cosas. Necesitan meternos miedo con el extranjero, porque nos necesitan para alcanzar su plan racista, su plan xen脙鲁fobo, para agitar la f脙隆bula del 脗芦gran reemplazo脗禄, su plan de ingenier脙颅a socioecon脙鲁mica autoritaria que solo quiere controlar nuestros cuerpos y nuestras vidas.

Dejen de utilizarnos para otros fines y acomp脙隆脙卤ennos para conseguir los derechos de las mujeres en igualdad.

脗聽脗聽Harald Vilimsky (PfE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, meine Damen und Herren! Vor rund zehn Jahren hat Angela Merkel den wahrscheinlich schicksalstr脙陇chtigsten Schlag gegen Europa 脙录berhaupt in der letzten Dekade ausgef脙录hrt, als sie gesagt hat 芒聙聻Wir schaffen das芒聙聹 und 脙录ber 10脗聽Millionen Menschen aus Arabien und Afrika zu uns gestr脙露mt sind脗聽芒聙聯 Menschen, wo zwei Drittel keinerlei Schutzbed脙录rfnis haben, weder Genfer Konvention noch humanit脙陇r, noch subsidi脙陇r irgendeine Schutzberechtigung haben. Und mit dieser Welle an illegalen Migranten sind auch wirtschaftliche Probleme, soziale Probleme, kulturelle Probleme einhergegangen, und auch Terror ist einhergegangen: Bataclan-Theater, Flughafen in Br脙录ssel, in Stra脙聼burg am Weihnachtsmarkt und, und, und.

Und eines der f脙录r mich beeindruckendsten Werke in diesem Zusammenhang ist ein Buch, das jetzt herausgekommen ist, von einem Landsmann von mir, von Gerald Grosz, mit dem Titel 芒聙聻Merkels Werk脗聽芒聙聯脗聽Unser Untergang芒聙聹. Er zitiert viele europ脙陇ische Staatsm脙陇nner, unter anderem den ehemaligen Innenminister und nunmehrigen Parteichef der FP脙聳 in 脙聳sterreich, Herbert Kickl, der das sagt脗聽芒聙聯 und genau das bringt es auf den Punkt: Man schert sich keinen Deut f脙录r die Interessen der Bev脙露lkerung und macht ausschlie脙聼lich, wof脙录r man von der linken Schickeria Applaus erh脙陇lt; das ist das, was stattgefunden hat. Und weitere Analysen von Viktor Orb脙隆n bis Tino Chrupalla: Ich kann nur jedem empfehlen, lesen Sie dieses Buch, das ist die perfekte Contra-Meinung. 芒聙娄

(Die Pr脙陇sidentin entzieht dem Redner das Wort.)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Herr Vilimsky, es ist nach unseren Regeln nicht erlaubt, hier B脙录cher zu zeigen, genauso wenig wie T-Shirts zu tragen mit politischen Aufdrucken. Ich bitte Sie, das Buch fortzutun, und ich bitte Sie jetzt auch, Ihre Redezeit zu beenden, weil die ist schon lange vorbei. Danke sch脙露n!

Herr Vilimsky, wir haben Regeln. Bitte respektieren Sie auch Ihre Redezeit! Sie waren schon eine halbe Minute dr脙录ber. Vielen Dank! Sie k脙露nnen gerne zu Ihrem Platz gehen und der Debatte weiter folgen.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alessandro Ciriani (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, per anni questo Parlamento ha preferito chiudere gli occhi di fronte alla realt脙聽. Si 脙篓 parlato di accoglienza illimitata, di porte aperte, come se i nostri Stati fossero in grado di assorbire senza conseguenze un flusso continuo di arrivi.

Oggi, per脙虏, anche chi negava l'evidenza 脙篓 costretto a riconoscere la realt脙聽: quel modello 脙篓 fallito. La migrazione non 脙篓 pi脙鹿 un terreno di scontro ideologico. Con realismo dobbiamo ammettere che i nostri sistemi sociali di accoglienza non possono reggere una pressione di queste dimensioni e che cos脙卢 nessuna integrazione 脙篓 possibile.

Il risultato 脙篓 che i diritti di chi fugge davvero da guerre e persecuzioni vengono calpestati, perch脙漏 il sistema collassa sotto il peso di chi entra senza alcun titolo. E, ovviamente, i risvolti sulla sicurezza dei nostri cittadini sono sotto gli occhi di tutti. I fatti di cronaca parlano da soli: interi quartieri diventati zone rosse, dove degrado e violenze sono purtroppo all'ordine del giorno.

Per questo si apre oggi una delle stagioni pi脙鹿 decisive; in quest'Aula discutiamo strumenti concreti come il rafforzamento dei rimpatri, l'applicazione coerente del principio di "paese terzo sicuro" e la lista comune dei paesi sicuri.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Abir Al-Sahlani (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, this debate is both factually and morally wrong. Wrong, wrong, wrong. And two factual wrongs you will find already in the name of this debate. There is no mass migration to Europe, but in fact, there is a 66脗聽% drop in the applications of asylum. The second wrong is the EU-wide data shows that sexual violence is committed by EU nationals, by men who are known to the women in their own homes.脗聽The common denominator here is spelled M-E-N, men. That is where the wrong is.

The far right are twisting facts into an ignorant, hate-fuelled portrayal of migrants. Correlation does not equal causality. Women's rights organisations are rejecting your cynical weaponisation of our pain for your racist agenda. I have my voice. Don't you dare claim to represent it.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anna Strolenberg (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, ending mass migration to protect our women and children is the title of this debate, chosen by my colleagues on the far right.

Colleagues, you can do better than this: every opportunity you get to dehumanise migrants, you take it again and again and again, and now you're even exploiting the murder of a Dutch woman for your own political agenda.

It is true: women do get murdered, but not by migrants 芒聙聯 mostly by their partners, ex-partners or men close to them. The only thing you do is to spread fear. I have a different fear. In my country, the Netherlands, calls to domestic violence hotlines are at a record high, and every eight days a woman does get murdered.

So if you are truly worried about women, then do what you have failed to do before: fight with us. I would say: build shelters for women, educate men, invest in prevention, train agents at recognising abuse and call all countries to sign the Istanbul Convention.

Colleagues, we will not dehumanise. We will organise to end femicide.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

PREDSED脙聛: MARTIN HOJS脙聧K

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脙聳zlem Demirel (The Left). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Unverfroren streuen hier die Rechtspopulisten Hass und Hetze. Sie spr脙录hen ihr Gift und wollen Menschen gegeneinander ausspielen. Das Problem ist aber auch, dass die Politik der Kommission, der Regierenden, und der Kniefall der Konservativen den Rechten immer weiter die T脙录r 脙露ffnen, um weitergehen zu k脙露nnen.

Sie sagen, Sie wollen 脙录ber Frauenrechte und Kinderrechte reden? Dann lassen Sie uns doch 脙录ber Femizide reden! Lassen Sie uns doch 脙录ber h脙陇usliche Gewalt reden! Lassen Sie uns doch dar脙录ber reden, wie wir Frauenh脙陇user erweitern k脙露nnen! Das wollen Sie nicht, Sie wollen keine L脙露sung.

Sie wollen 脙录ber Kinderrechte reden? Lassen Sie uns 脙录ber Familienarmut reden! Lassen Sie uns dar脙录ber reden, dass wir keine Chancengerechtigkeit in der Bildung haben! Lassen Sie uns 脙录ber die echten Probleme der Menschen reden, 脙录ber Rente, 脙录ber Sozialpolitik! Das wollen Sie nicht, denn die Superreichen spenden Ihnen viel Geld f脙录r Ihre Schnittchenfeiern.

Liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, Sie wollen Hass und Hetze sch脙录ren, Sie wollen keine L脙露sungen, keine Antworten. Wir wollen endlich die echten Debatten, die die B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger hier in diesem Lande oder in Europa wirklich interessieren. Tun Sie das! H脙露ren Sie auf mit Ihrer Hass-und-Hetze-Politik!

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Zsuzsanna Borvend脙漏g (ESN). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k 脙職r! Tudj脙隆k, mi a k脙录l脙露nbs脙漏g Budapest 脙漏s Br脙录sszel k脙露z脙露tt? Ha otthon durran脙隆st hallok az utc脙隆n, az jut eszembe, hogy egy aut脙鲁 defektet kapott. Ha Br脙录sszelben hallok hasonl脙鲁t, fedez脙漏ket keresek. Migr脙隆ci脙鲁s h脙隆tter脜卤 band脙隆k l脙露v脙露ld脙露znek Eur脙鲁pa f脜聭v脙隆ros脙隆ban. A v脙隆ros 脙录gy脙漏sze hal脙隆los fenyeget脙漏sek miatt rend脜聭ri v脙漏delem alatt 脙漏l. A nyugati v脙隆rosokban, a migr脙隆nsok lakta gett脙鲁kban m脙隆r nincs eur脙鲁pai jogrend. A b脜卤ncselekm脙漏nyek sz脙隆ma az egekbe sz脙露k脙露tt. A j脙鲁l脙漏ti 脙漏s biztons脙隆got ad脙鲁 脙隆llam a m脙潞lt脙漏. Elv脙隆rj脙隆k, hogy b脜卤n脙露s politik脙隆juk v脙漏gzetes k脙露vetkezm脙漏nyeit mindenki viselje. S脜聭t, azokat b脙录ntetik, akik kimondj脙隆k az igazs脙隆got itt a Parlamentben is. Magyarorsz脙隆got napi egymilli脙鲁 eur脙鲁s b脙颅rs脙隆ggal, b脙颅rs脙隆ggal s脙潞jtj脙隆k, ami脙漏rt k脙隆rt脙漏r脙颅t脙漏s ker脙颅t脙漏ssel v脙漏di a schengeni hat脙隆rokat.

B脙隆r a vend脙漏gmunk脙隆sok be脙隆raml脙隆s脙隆t is aggaszt脙鲁nak tartom, de a ker脙颅t脙漏ssel m脙漏gis Eur脙鲁pa v脙漏d脜聭b脙隆sty脙隆ja vagyunk. Nem akarunk h脜聭s脙露k lenni, csak b脙漏k脙漏ben akarunk 脙漏lni. Fizess脙漏k ki v脙漏gre a hat脙隆rv脙漏delmi k脙露lts脙漏geinket, 脙漏s v脙漏dj脙漏k meg 脙聳n脙露k is az eur脙鲁pai embereket!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lena D脙录pont (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, Minister, what have we found last term? We found Member States that were not able to conclude on a common asylum and migration policy. We found a Parliament that had led heavy discussions, intense debate, in order to find a way forward.

What have we done? We are taking stock of the common asylum and migration policy, what was left of it from the previous term before that. And we are taking that stock critically because we saw the Dublin transferees, for example, were and are still dysfunctional. But why so? Because they were not connected to solidarity. Because we did not find the right balance between frontline Member States and Member States of secondary movement.

We added safeguards against secondary movement. We added an efficient procedure with the border procedure, and we added the Eurodac in order to prevent double and triple applications. We are also taking stock of the geopolitical situation because it脗聽had changed in the meantime. So we added something on instrumentalisation in order to defend the Member States that were faced with that, and we are still doing that.

Are we done yet? No, we are not. We see that the numbers are going down, but there are still deficiencies in the system. There are still loopholes that can be exploited. And make no mistake, this system is exploited by criminal gangs, by networks, by smugglers, and, of course, also by third-country actors. We should not let that out of mind.

So we focus now on the missing pieces to the pack, which is the return policy, which is the safe third country concept and the safe country of origins. And we will continue with that, with a strengthened Europol, with a strengthened Frontex, and a stronger answer when it comes to a sanctions regime on organised crime and smuggling business.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anders Vistisen (PfE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, in 2020, a 15-year-old German girl was gang-raped by nine different migrants in a Hamburg city park. In October last year, a 12-year-old girl was murdered in Paris by a Nigerian migrant who had already been ordered to leave the country. And just eight weeks ago, a 16-year-old Ukrainian refugee was pushed in front of a train in Germany and killed by an Iraqi migrant under deportation orders.

These are not individual examples. In Denmark alone, over the last 10脗聽years, 1 in 3 women killed were killed by a migrant. Without mass migration, countless European women and children would still be alive today, spared from violence, abuse and exploitation.

When will the Commission and the majority in this Parliament begin to take the side of the victims instead of the side of the perpetrators? When will you put the safety of our mothers, sisters and daughters above your failed ideology? I fear never, but already now it's too late.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kristoffer Storm (ECR). 芒聙聯 Hr. formand! De sidste 10 脙楼r har v脙娄ret en katastrofe for Europa. Migrationen mod Europa har alt for l脙娄nge v脙娄ret totalt ude af kontrol, s脙楼 vi st脙楼r nu over for en udfordring, der ikke bare kr脙娄ver vores opm脙娄rksomhed, den kr脙娄ver handling 芒聙聯 rigtig handling, ikke bare snak!

Den massive migration fra muslimske lande har medf脙赂rt en kultur, hvor voldt脙娄gter og kriminalitet er dagligdag. Og det, k脙娄re kollegaer, er konsekvensen af venstrefl脙赂jens naive og landsskadelige politik. Og dem, som betaler den st脙赂rste pris, det er vores kvinder, og det er vores b脙赂rn, men alle kvinder og alle b脙赂rn fortjener at f脙赂le sig trygge.

Derfor skal vi tage et opg脙赂r med de regler, der i 脙赂jeblikket forhindrer os i at afvise og udvise u脙赂nskede indvandrere. Vi skal have mulighed for at tr脙娄ffe beslutninger, der gavner vores samfund og beskytter vores borgere mod migrationen. Derfor er det p脙楼 tide, at vi f脙楼r vendt udviklingen og genoprettet Europa oven p脙楼 de massive 脙赂del脙娄ggelser, som Socialdemokraterne og deres venner p脙楼 venstrefl脙赂jen har skabt.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Raquel Garc脙颅a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, Minister, I actually came here to have a serious debate on root causes of criminality and violence against women, but I'm not going to do that because I'm not going to legitimise this intention of this debate after what we have heard today from the extreme right to demonise entire groups of people and put them aside and put groups of people against one another.

Because if the extreme right was interested in fixing the problems which might come with migration, they would be working constructively to implement a good migration pact and everything which comes with it.

If the extreme right was interested in the rights of women, my own daughters, they would not be voting as they do in the Netherlands against more money to combat femicide.

And if the extreme right was interested in building a healthy society where we can live with one another, they would not have put this debate forward. I refuse.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mary Khan (ESN). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! So, vielleicht an die Vorrednerin: Die AfD hat schon vor zehn Jahren gesagt: 芒聙聻Wir schaffen es nicht芒聙聹. Und deshalb bin ich auch dieser Partei beigetreten, weil sie damals schon vorhergesehen hat, dass wir es nicht schaffen werden, T脙录r und Tor f脙录r jeden zu 脙露ffnen.

Und sie hatte auch recht damit. Denn heute, zehn Jahre sp脙陇ter, liegen uns die blutigen Zahlen vor: Alle 30脗聽Minuten ver脙录bt ein Ausl脙陇nder eine Messerattacke in Deutschland, jeden Tag zwei Gruppenvergewaltigungen und unz脙陇hlige islamistische Anschl脙陇ge. Das Blut dieser Opfer klebt genau an Ihren H脙陇nden. Und das Widerliche ist: keine Reue, keine Entschuldigung, kein R脙录cktritt. Stattdessen will man weitermachen.

Aber warum auch? Sie fahren mit Ihrem Dienstwagen. Ihre Kinder m脙录ssen nicht auf Brennpunktschulen gehen, und Sie k脙露nnen jederzeit umziehen, wenn in Ihrer Wohngegend ein Fl脙录chtlingslager platziert wird. Die Leidtragenden, gerade bei mir in Deutschland, sind die Schw脙陇chsten der Gesellschaft: die Mutter, die ihr Kind nicht auf eine Privatschule schicken kann; das M脙陇dchen, das nicht von seinen Eltern abgeholt werden kann. Und ich werde Ihnen versprechen, wir werden die Verantwortlichen 芒聙娄

(Der Pr脙陇sident entzieht der Rednerin das Wort.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fran脙搂ois-Xavier Bellamy (PPE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, pour essayer de revenir 脙聽 la raison, revenons aussi aux faits. Certains contestent depuis tout 脙聽 l'heure le titre m脙陋me de ce d脙漏bat. Mais le lien entre l'immigration et la d脙漏linquance, si nous regardons les faits, ne fait aucun doute.

Prenez quelques grandes villes de France. 脙聙 Paris, 14脗聽% de la population est d'origine 脙漏trang脙篓re, 48脗聽% des mis en cause dans les faits de d脙漏linquance sont d'origine 脙漏trang脙篓re. 脙聙 Marseille, 11脗聽% d'脙漏trangers, 55脗聽% d'脙漏trangers parmi ceux qui sont mis en cause pour des faits de d脙漏linquance. Et c'est vrai en particulier quand on parle des violences sexuelles: en 脙聨le-de-France, 63脗聽% des agressions sexuelles dans les transports sont le fait d'脙漏trangers.

Depuis tout 脙聽 l'heure, j'entends des coll脙篓gues qui disent que c'est un scandale que de faire ce lien et qui ont pr脙漏f脙漏r脙漏 m脙陋me expliquer que, pour ce qui concerne les violences sexuelles, tous les hommes 脙漏taient coupables, plut脙麓t que de regarder la r脙漏alit脙漏 en face. Je crois qu'il faut revenir 脙聽 la raison.

Le sujet n'est pas seulement le constat. Le sujet c'est d'apporter une r脙漏ponse 脙聽 cette situation que les citoyens dans nos pays vivent tous les jours dans leur chair. Pour cela, nous avons devant nous l'opportunit脙漏 de la r脙漏vision du r脙篓glement sur les retours qui permettra enfin d'expulser, en dehors d'Europe, les 脙漏trangers en situation irr脙漏guli脙篓re.

J'esp脙篓re que tous les groupes politiques ici seront capables de se saisir de cette occasion d脙漏terminante.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jorge Buxad脙漏 Villalba (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, hace diez a脙卤os, Merkel dijo que cab脙颅an todos, y ustedes aplaudieron y ejecutaron la orden, pero ahora una violencia brutal se ha apoderado de nuestras calles.

Por supuesto que alzamos la voz por todas esas mujeres y ni脙卤as a las que la izquierda desprecia, porque sus agresores son extranjeros. Por Lisa, 17 a脙卤os, asesinada en Holanda, por todas las ni脙卤as agredidas sexualmente este verano en Espa脙卤a: en Alcal脙隆, en Hortaleza, en Gald脙隆cano, en La Laguna o en Olot. Por todas esas a las que despreci脙隆is. La civilizaci脙鲁n se pone a prueba todas las noches, en todos nuestros barrios, en todas las ciudades de Europa.

Si importas millones de personas que no respetan a la mujer, tendr脙隆s violaciones y agresiones sexuales. Y no es la pobreza ni es la necesidad. Los pobres no violan ni脙卤as, los pobres no violan mujeres, los pobres trabajan. Ustedes son responsables: los que les llaman, les excusan.

Miren, Merkel les minti脙鲁. Yo les digo una verdad: no cabe ni uno m脙隆s. La 脗芦remigraci脙鲁n脗禄 proteger脙隆 a las mujeres.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jadwiga Wi脜聸niewska (ECR). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Kryzys migracyjny wywo脜聜any przez Merkel niszczy europejsk脛聟 to脜录samo脜聸脛聡. Prowadzi do islamizacji Europy. Skutki widzimy dzi脜聸 w zachodniej Europie: gwa脜聜ty, napady, kradzie脜录e, wojny gang脙鲁w. Nielegalna migracja jest te脜录 elementem wojny hybrydowej, jak脛聟 tocz脛聟 z Uni脛聟 Putin i 脜聛ukaszenko. Widzimy to szczeg脙鲁lnie na granicy polsko-bia脜聜oruskiej, gdzie lewicowi aktywi脜聸ci, wspieraj脛聟c nielegalnych migrant脙鲁w, de facto stali si脛聶 u脜录ytecznymi idiotami Putina. Nie ma przypadk脙鲁w. To zaplanowane i skoordynowane dzia脜聜ania. Dzi脜聸 mamy do czynienia z bezpo脜聸rednim atakiem na Polsk脛聶. Putin wie, 脜录e Europa jest bezsilna, bo na czele Komisji stoi von der Leyen, kt脙鲁ra by脜聜a najgorszym ministrem obrony Niemiec. Co wi脛聶c nale脜录y zrobi脛聡? Trzeba natychmiast wprowadzi脛聡 przymusowe deportacje, uszczelni脛聡 zewn脛聶trzne granice Unii, a pakt imigracyjny wyrzuci脛聡 do kosza. I na koniec powiem otwarcie: r脛聶ce precz od Polski! Polska nie b脛聶dzie p脜聜aci脛聡 za b脜聜脛聶dy migracyjne Berlina i Brukseli.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alma Ezcurra Almansa (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or脙颅as, yo les quiero hacer una pregunta: 脗驴a alguien aqu脙颅 le parece patriota que se utilice la leg脙颅tima preocupaci脙鲁n de millones europeos para inyectar el odio y no impulsar soluciones?

Aqu脙颅 lo que vemos es que hay una izquierda que fabrica v脙颅ctimas y multiplica el problema y una extrema derecha que nos dice que todos los inmigrantes son verdugos, con independencia de lo que hagan. Y todos 芒聙聰de una forma u otra芒聙聰 llaman a incumplir la ley. Lo que tienen que hacer es reconocer, abiertamente, que no les mueve ni la solidaridad ni la seguridad, sino agitar la pena y el miedo por puro r脙漏dito electoral; y, entre tanto, abandonan a los europeos.

Claro que hay un problema de seguridad y claro que est脙隆 vinculado, muchas veces, con la inmigraci脙鲁n ilegal, pero la respuesta no son las colectivizaciones comunistas ni las deshumanizaciones fascistas: la respuesta es expulsar al que no se quiere integrar, integrar al que cumple y proteger, de verdad, la identidad europea, la democracia y la libertad. Eso s脙颅 es ser un patriota: no calentar la calle escondido detr脙隆s de un esca脙卤o, sino anteponer el bien com脙潞n a los intereses partidistas por respeto a la gente que nos da de comer.
