脗聽脗聽Georgiana Teodorescu (ECR). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, acesta este un mesaj pentru to脠聸i morali脠聶tii care ne 脠聸in lec脠聸ii despre generozitate, acuz脙垄ndu-ne c脛聝 suntem extremi脠聶ti pentru c脛聝 nu deschidem larg bra脠聸ele Europei pentru migran脠聸i.

Suntem ale脠聶i de cet脛聝脠聸eni europeni din state membre ale UE. Avem misiunea de a prioritiza interesele lor, ale celor care ne-au ales. Asta 脙庐nseamn脛聝 s脛聝 punem pe primul loc dreptul lor de a ocupa locurile de munc脛聝 existente, siguran脠聸a lor pe str脛聝zi, accesul lor la servicii medicale, dreptul lor de a-脠聶i manifesta apartenen脠聸a la o religie.

脙聨n lupta de a demonstra public c脙垄t de politically correct sunte脠聸i, ajunge脠聸i s脛聝 脙庐l sacrifica脠聸i fix pe cet脛聝脠聸eanul european pentru a-l ajuta pe altul. Poate nu 脠聶ti脠聸i, dar Comisia European脛聝 脙庐ns脛聝脠聶i a adoptat o propunere de regulament pentru 脙庐ntoarcerea migran脠聸ilor ilegali 脙庐n 脠聸脛聝rile de origine. Ba mai mult, noi atribute Frontex pentru combaterea acestui fenomen cople脠聶itor, suplimentarea trupelor la grani脠聸脛聝 cu 10 000 de oameni 脠聶i triplarea personalului de coast脛聝.

Dac脛聝 脠聶i Comisia European脛聝 e extremist脛聝 pentru voi, v脛聝 provoc s脛聝 v脛聝 demonstra脠聸i a脠聶a-zisa generozitate cu fapte, nu cu vorbe. To脠聸i cei care 脙庐ndr脛聝zni脠聸i s脛聝 ne ataca脠聸i, deschide脠聸i-v脛聝 casele 脠聶i primi脠聸i s脛聝 locuiasc脛聝 al脛聝turi de copiii vo脠聶tri c脙垄te o familie de migran脠聸i. Abia dup脛聝 ce face脠聸i asta ave脠聸i dreptul s脛聝 ne da脠聸i nou脛聝 lec脠聸ii.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Arno Bausemer (ESN). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Zehn Jahre Massenmigration, zehn Jahre Leid und Elend f脙录r die B脙录rger Europas. Als die damalige Kanzlerin Angela Merkel 2015 die deutschen Grenzen 脙露ffnete, da wurde ganz Europa mit teilweise gewaltbereiten und asozialen Fl脙录chtlingen geflutet. Dublin-Abkommen und Grundgesetz脗聽芒聙聯 f脙录r Angela Merkel und ihr Kabinett der willkommensverbl脙露deten, kulturell verbl脙露deten Fl脙录chtlingsfreunde gab es kein Recht mehr. Es gab nur noch den Willen, die ganze Welt nach Deutschland zu holen.

Aber diese moralische 脙聹berheblichkeit nach dem Motto 芒聙聻Am deutschen Wesen soll die Welt genesen芒聙聹 war doch in der Geschichte schon einmal gescheitert.

Massenvergewaltigungen, Terror, schwere K脙露rperverletzung, Mord und Totschlag脗聽芒聙聯 kein Tag vergeht seit zehn Jahren ohne derartige Meldungen, und dennoch ist bis heute kein ernsthaftes Umdenken in den Reihen der verantwortlichen Altparteien zu erkennen脗聽芒聙聯 in Ihren Reihen. Statt Abschiebungen nach Afghanistan kommen weitere zweifelhafte Afghanen nach Deutschland. Statt geschlossener Abschiebehaft werden neue offene Asylbewerberheime gebaut. Statt Vermittlung in Arbeit werden Fl脙录chtlinge mit Geldleistungen noch belohnt. Statt notwendiger Anreize zur Ausreise bieten CDU und SPD weiter fragw脙录rdige Anreize zur Einreise. Sie hier in den Altparteien haben es auch nach zehn Jahren nicht verstanden.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, I am referring to Rule 10(4), which prohibits hate speech.

Der Herr Kollege Bausemer 脙陇u脙聼ert sich 脙录ber die 脙聞ngste und Sorgen der Bev脙露lkerung im Hinblick auf Migration. Er 脙陇u脙聼ert sich 脙录ber die Probleme, die mit der Migration zusammenh脙陇ngen. Er bekommt es aber nicht hin, dies zu 脙陇u脙聼ern, ohne die damit zusammenh脙陇ngenden Menschen als Asoziale zu bezeichnen. Das ist wissenschaftlich nachgewiesenerma脙聼en eine Sprache, die sich bereits die Nationalsozialisten, also das ideologische Vorbild des Kollegen Bausemer und seiner Kameraden, zunutze gemacht haben.

Auch werden hier Repr脙陇sentanten der Europ脙陇ischen Union in den Schmutz gezogen, und das ist nicht nur unanst脙陇ndig, sondern widerspricht eben auch den Regeln unserer Gesch脙陇ftsordnung. Es w脙陇re also sehr zu w脙录nschen, wenn Sie es in Zukunft schaffen, Ihren politischen Punkt klarzumachen, ohne andere Menschen unn脙露tig zu beleidigen. Ich hoffe, das Pr脙陇sidium wird die entsprechenden Ma脙聼nahmen ergreifen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Der Pr脙陇sident. 芒聙聯 Danke vielmals! Ich habe die Rede im deutschen Original geh脙露rt, weil ich Deutsch verstehe.

But it's easier for me to speak in English.

Yes, we will look at it, but also I have to respect the freedom of speech 芒聙聯 it's a really fine balance. Thank you for raising the historical consequences, the context that might have broken the Rules of Procedure. Please also submit it in writing.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Filip Turek (PfE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, you have launched a self-destructive strategy on Europe's energy economy and industry, with CBAM, ETS, ELV, ESG and other absurd acronyms. And now you take it to an extreme. You want central and eastern Europe to carry the bad karma of your colonial past and your failed social policies: 'Wir schaffen das.' In this way, you will break the very soul of Europe.

As Gabriel Attal said about the migration pact, the West had convinced eastern countries to accept migrants or to contribute financially to the management of migration. But believe me, the Czech Republic, Poland, Slovakia, Hungary and others without colonial guilt or the burden of World War II will never accept the relocation of your migrants to our lands.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paolo Inselvini (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, l'immigrazionismo e l'ideologia globalista rappresentano una sciagura per l'Europa e per il mondo intero. Il desiderio di eliminare le nazioni, le identit脙聽 e le differenze rappresenta una distopia distruttiva: lo 脙篓 in teoria e si sta purtroppo dimostrando nella pratica.

Per troppi anni speculatori senza scrupoli, come Soros, e trafficanti di esseri umani si sono arricchiti senza badare a chi muore in mare, fregandosene degli scontri sociali, del disordine e della perdita di radici.

Questa visione danneggia i popoli che subiscono l'immigrazione, ma anche le persone che si spostano e i paesi di partenza stessi. L'Italia, infatti, sta facendo tanto nonostante gli indegni tentativi di boicottaggio della sinistra e di una certa parte di magistratura. E dobbiamo fare, e faremo, ancora di pi脙鹿 per quanto riguarda i rimpatri, l'opposizione all'immigrazione clandestina e gli accordi bilaterali.

L'Europa ci segua, perch脙漏, se vogliamo preservare ci脙虏 che siamo, dobbiamo garantire a tutti 芒聙聯 come ha detto papa Benedetto XVI 芒聙聯 il diritto a non emigrare, il diritto a vivere in pace nella propria terra.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andr脙隆s L脙隆szl脙鲁 (PfE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, the EU has been harassing Hungary for ten years now because we built a big, long and beautiful border fence to stop illegal migrants.

Ten years ago, Germany and the EU adopted the open borders policy of George Soros. Rapists, extremists and violent criminals have flooded Europe. It has ruined the lives of millions.

Hungary did the exact opposite. We stopped more than 1脗聽million脗聽illegal immigrants. Europe, you are welcome. But instead of a reward, we got a fine of EUR脗聽200脗聽million plus a fine of EUR脗聽1脗聽million per day until we don't allow illegals into Europe. This is pure madness.

At the same time, Ursula von der Leyen visits the Polish border and announces more EU funding for it. Denmark is allowed to detain illegals indefinitely: the double standards and hypocrisy is massive. The damage you have done to Europe is irreversible. Hungary is showing however the way.

We won't give in. We won't pay. We will defend our borders. We will defend our women and we will defend our identity, and this is the way to make Europe great again.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Charlie Weimers (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr President, a parent's worst nightmare: your little girl never arrives home. You search the streets. Your fear turns to panic. You see the police blockade. Your worst fears are confirmed. She's found in the woods, brutally raped, bound and strangled with her own shoelaces.

Luna, a beautiful nine-year-old girl, lived through this nightmare. Now she has irreversible brain damage and is unable to care for herself. She is a victim of mass migration.脗聽And why? The predator that attacked her, a teenage migrant, had already assaulted a woman and groped classmates. The authorities knew, yet did nothing.

As a father of four young daughters, my duty is to protect my wife and my children. As legislators, our solemn duty is to protect Europeans. And to those of you that deny the link between migration and safety for women: how many more? Luna, Lola, Liana, Elin, Lisa, Alexandra, Saga, Philippine, Mia and Adriana. How many more will there have to be before you at least care?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nikola Bart脜炉脜隆ek (PfE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Kollegen! 2015, als Bundeskanzlerin Merkel ihre Politik der offenen T脙录r verk脙录ndete, begann ich, an vorderster Front der Migration in 脙聳sterreich zu arbeiten, direkt in den Asylzentren. Ich sah Tausende von illegalen Migranten ankommen. Mit eigenen Augen habe ich erlebt, wie einige von ihnen sich radikalisierten, und oft endete dies mit tragischen Konsequenzen.

Zehn Jahre sp脙陇ter m脙录ssen wir ehrlich sagen: Die Masseneinwanderung l脙露ste Europas Probleme nicht; sie hat neue geschaffen. Die Integration scheiterte, Parallelgesellschaften sind gewachsen, und die Sicherheit hat sich verschlechtert. Unsere B脙录rger, insbesondere Frauen, zahlen den Preis, wenn Messerangriffe und 脙聹bergriffe europ脙陇ische St脙陇dte unsicher machen.

Das ist nicht die Zukunft, die wir f脙录r unseren Kontinent wollen. Deshalb bin ich im Europ脙陇ischen Parlament脗聽芒聙聯 um Europa zu sch脙录tzen, die Sicherheit und W脙录rde unserer B脙录rger wiederherzustellen und klar zu sagen: Illegale Migration muss gestoppt werden.

脗聽脗聽Jaroslav B脜戮och (PfE). 芒聙聯 Pane p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, deset let potom, co Angela Merkelov脙隆 脜聶ekla to jej脙颅 slavn脙漏, nebo sp脙颅脜隆e neslavn脙漏 芒聙聻Wir schaffen das!芒聙聹, v脙颅me, kde jsme. Dnes u脜戮 jsme sly脜隆eli o tom, 脜戮e tlak na na脜隆e hranice je ni脜戮脜隆脙颅. To m脜炉脜戮e b脙陆t sice pravda, ale za posledn脙颅ch deset let, co jsme tady, zde m脙隆me velk脙陆 probl脙漏m. M脙隆me zde probl脙漏m s t脙颅m, 脜戮e migranti, kte脜聶脙颅 k n脙隆m p脜聶i脜隆li, a Ursula von der Leyenov脙隆 o nich dnes tak脙漏 mluvila, o kter脙陆ch jsme si mysleli, 脜戮e zapln脙颅 na脜隆e pracovn脙颅 m脙颅sta, tak se tak nestalo. Neintegrovali se, necht脛聸j脙颅 se integrovat a m脙颅sto toho si sem nos脙颅 a voz脙颅 svoje n脙隆vyky, kter脙漏 n脙隆脜隆 zp脜炉sob 脜戮ivota bort脙颅.

Evropsk脙隆 unie deset let p脜聶e脜隆lapuje na m脙颅st脛聸. Syst脙漏my, kter脙漏 m脛聸ly chr脙隆nit hranice a m脛聸ly n脙隆m pom脙隆hat 脜聶脙颅dit migraci, st脙隆le nefunguj脙颅. N脙隆vratov脙隆 politika v脜炉bec 脜戮脙隆dn脙隆 nen脙颅. Vid脙颅me rostouc脙颅 脙潞toky no脜戮em. Vid脙颅me nejv脛聸t脜隆脙颅 hrozbu, kterou jsou st脙隆le teroristick脙漏 脙潞toky a p脜聶edev脜隆脙颅m d脜戮ih脙隆distick脙漏 脙潞toky, ruku v ruce s rostouc脙颅m vlivem Muslimsk脙漏ho bratrstva. Po d脜戮ih脙隆distick脙陆ch teroristick脙陆ch 脙潞toc脙颅ch je zde dal脜隆脙颅 probl脙漏m. A to je levicov脙陆 terorismus, kter脙陆 jde ruku v ruce pr脙隆v脛聸 s t脙颅m d脜戮ih脙隆distick脙陆m. Tohle nen脙颅 ot脙隆zka 脛聧脙颅sel, tohle nen脙颅 ot脙隆zka statistiky. Jde o na脜隆脙颅 vnit脜聶n脙颅 bezpe脛聧nost. Jde o bezpe脛聧nost na脜隆ich rodin, d脛聸t脙颅. 脗聽脗聽Adrian-George Axinia (ECR). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, 脙庐ncurajarea migra脠聸iei legale 脠聶i ilegale a fost o gre脠聶eal脛聝 uria脠聶脛聝. A generat costuri economice 脠聶i sociale mari, dar 脠聶i o sl脛聝bire semnificativ脛聝 a 脙庐ncrederii 脙庐n proiectul european.

Exist脛聝 un sentiment acut de cetate asediat脛聝. La 10 minute de mers cu ma脠聶ina de la sediul Parlamentului European, oricine poate vedea realitatea. Modul nostru de via脠聸脛聝 脠聶i valorile ne sunt amenin脠聸ate. Este timpul ca Europa s脛聝 脙庐n脠聸eleag脛聝 c脛聝 ne trebuie un gard mai 脙庐nalt 脠聶i sigur, nu doar fizic, ci 脠聶i legislativ. Spre exemplu, din 2020 脠聶i p脙垄n脛聝 acum, 脠聸ara mea a oferit permise de munc脛聝 pentru peste 500 000 de lucr脛聝tori din Asia, peste 120 000 se afl脛聝 fizic 脙庐n Rom脙垄nia, al脠聸ii au plecat spre vest, de aceea avem nevoie de o legisla脠聸ie unitar脛聝 脠聶i restrictiv脛聝.

Problema Uniunii Europene este sporul natural negativ. Ne trebuie un baby boom care s脛聝 fie 脙庐ncurajat 脠聶i finan脠聸at de la Bruxelles 脠聶i nu vom mai avea nevoie de migran脠聸i incompatibili cu valorile noastre. Liderii politici ar trebui s脛聝 o lase mai moale cu falsele virtu脠聸i. Nu a脠聶a se ob脠聸ine Premiul Nobel pentru Pace, cum voia 芒聙聻Mutti芒聙聺 Merkel.

Conducerea Uniunii Europene trebuie s脛聝 ac脠聸ioneze ra脠聸ional pentru rezolvarea problemelor reale, economice, sociale 脠聶i de securitate ale Europei.

脗聽脗聽Susanna Ceccardi (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, dopo dieci anni di flussi migratori incontrollati, i dati ufficiali ci dimostrano un quadro incontrovertibile.

Per esempio, in Italia gli immigrati rappresentano il 9脗聽% della popolazione, ma commettono oltre il 40脗聽% delle violenze sessuali. Pochi giorni fa, vicino Catania, due turiste ungheresi sono state violentate da tre cittadini nordafricani e questi numeri ci obbligano a riconoscere la realt脙聽: l'immigrazione incontrollata mette a rischio le persone pi脙鹿 fragili, specialmente le donne e i bambini.

Queste verit脙聽, spesso, sono ignorate dai media nazionali. Andate in giro per le nostre citt脙聽 e guardate le stazioni e i luoghi degradati, 脙篓 pieno di immigrati che spacciano, si ubriacano, delinquono, ed 脙篓 questa l'accoglienza della sinistra.

Per questo oggi dobbiamo dire con fermezza che l'Europa non pu脙虏 pi脙鹿 subire l'immigrazione di massa: bisogna cambiare le regole, le convenzioni, i regolamenti, perch脙漏 questa 脙篓 la vera emergenza!

Abbiamo speso 88 miliardi di euro per la guerra in Ucraina, ci basterebbe un decimo per fare i rimpatri di massa degli immigrati irregolari in Europa.

脗聽脗聽Ruggero Razza (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, il 17 settembre 芒聙聯 la prossima settimana 芒聙聯 saranno due anni dalla visita a Lampedusa, frontiera dell'Europa a Sud, del primo ministro italiano Giorgia Meloni e della Presidente Ursula von der Leyen.

Dopo quell'incontro, dopo quella prima volta in cui l'Europa ha preso atto della situazione in cui versa il Sud del proprio confine, 脙篓 arrivato il protocollo d'intesa con la Tunisia, grazie al quale, nei mesi e negli anni successivi, vi 脙篓 stato un evidente crollo degli sbarchi su quella rotta.

Siamo per脙虏 preoccupati, Commissario, siamo molto preoccupati, perch脙漏 avvertiamo una forte disattenzione da parte dell'Unione nel contesto nordafricano, con riferimento alla Libia, dove c'脙篓 la Russia che 芒聙聯 per dirla con la Presidente von der Leyen, questa mattina 芒聙聯 脙篓 il primo promotore dei trafficanti di uomini.

L'Europa non pu脙虏 rimanere silente di fronte a quello che accade in Libia.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Kruis (PfE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, in Nederland is vorige maand een meisje van脗聽17 vermoord door een asielzoeker. Dat is helaas geen incident, maar het gevolg van jarenlange massa芒聙聭immigratie. Links huilt krokodillentranen om Gaza, maar in onze eigen straten worden vrouwen belaagd door de beesten die zij zelf binnenhalen. Terwijl vrouwen, homo芒聙聶s en joden in Amsterdam niet meer veilig over straat kunnen, loopt de burgemeester van die stad wel voorop in Boedapest om te protesteren voor homorechten.

Wegkijken in eigen land, deugen in het buitenland. Zo gaat het er bij links aan toe. En nu willen ze ons laten geloven dat we een mannenprobleem hebben. We hebben geen mannenprobleem. We hebben een migrantenprobleem. Een probleem van massa芒聙聶s mannen uit vrouwonvriendelijke culturen. Een probleem van misbruik, moord, messteken, verkrachtingen, vernederingen en geweld. Want wie onze vrouwen wil beschermen, sluit onze grenzen. En wie onze grenzen niet sluit, offert onze vrouwen op. En dat kunnen wij als Partij voor de Vrijheid nooit aanvaarden.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Barbara Bonte (PfE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, Lisa was 17脗聽jaar. Ze is brutaal vermoord door een asielzoeker. Niet door toeval, niet door slechte straatlampen, niet door toxic masculinity, maar wel door iets wat onze media en linkse politici angstvallig doodzwijgen: het beleid van open grenzen. De waarheid is simpel: massamigratie maakt onze samenleving onveilig.

Hoeveel Lisa芒聙聶s moeten er nog volgen? Hoeveel vrouwen? Hoeveel dochters? Het kan mij echt niet schelen dat asielzoekers zich onveilig voelen in hun landen als onze vrouwen zich onveilig voelen in onze landen. Het is tijd om te kiezen: massamigratie of remigratie. Als wij, vrouwen, ons opnieuw veilig willen voelen, dan is er maar 脙漏脙漏n antwoord: remigratie.

脗聽脗聽Tomasz Buczek (PfE). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Po 10 latach najwy脜录szy czas zatrzyma脛聡 masow脛聟 imigracj脛聶, zacz脛聟脛聡 chroni脛聡 europejskie granice, a nielegalnych imigrant脙鲁w 芒聙聯 deportowa脛聡. Dopuszczenie do inwazji imigrant脙鲁w na Europ脛聶 jest najwi脛聶ksz脛聟 pora脜录k脛聟 europejskiej klasy politycznej od czas脙鲁w II wojny 脜聸wiatowej. Winnych trzeba nazwa脛聡 po imieniu. To Europejska Partia Ludowa, socjali脜聸ci, Renew, zieloni, lewica. To wy odpowiadacie za ten chaos. To wy odpowiadacie za zbrodnie i przest脛聶pstwa szturmuj脛聟cych nasze granice.

Na szcz脛聶脜聸cie obywatele Europy zaczynaj脛聟 rozumie脛聡, 脜录e w tym parlamencie, w Komisji Europejskiej i w Radzie s脛聟 politycy b脛聶d脛聟cy sojusznikami NGO-s脙鲁w, kt脙鲁re czerpi脛聟 korzy脜聸ci finansowe z przerzucania nielegalnych imigrant脙鲁w w g脜聜脛聟b Europy. Europejczykom otwieraj脛聟 si脛聶 oczy.

Kto odpowiada za sprowadzone na nich niebezpiecze脜聞stwo? Hiszpania lokuje imigrant脙鲁w w hotelach z widokiem na morze. Niemcy i Francja rozdaj脛聟 im paszporty. Nie id脜潞my dalej t脛聟 drog脛聟. Efekty takiej polityki wida脛聡 chocia脜录by w Brukseli, gdzie zagraniczne gangi urz脛聟dzaj脛聟 sobie strzelaniny w bia脜聜y dzie脜聞. Rodowici Belgowie masowo wynosz脛聟 si脛聶 ze stolicy, a porz脛聟dku musi pilnowa脛聡 belgijska armia. Nie chcemy takiej przysz脜聜o脜聸ci dla europejskich miast. Chcemy bezpiecznej Europy.

脗聽脗聽Andrius Kubilius, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, I would like to thank you very much for your contributions. The successful adoption of the pact shows that the EU is committed to solving the migration challenge. The pact and the newly proposed measures will re-establish credibility in a functioning system that balances responsibility and solidarity, whilst ensuring that those in need receive protection and that those that do not are effectively returned.

Our focus now must be on making these legislative acts an operational reality. We count on your support to make sure that the pending proposals are adopted soon, and these policies get appropriate funding.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, Commissioner, the President knows that the effectiveness of our migration policy depends on progress on all fronts. On the internal front, those who seek protection should receive a fair procession of their asylum claims, and those who do not qualify for protection should be returned. On the external front, we should address the root causes of migration, and cooperate on return and readmission to fight the smuggling of migrants.

The support of Parliament in these areas is very important, because one country cannot solve the problems alone. Like Mr脗聽Tob脙漏 from the EPP rightly said, migration requires 'a unified approach'. We need European solutions. Migration is a European problem. More work needs to be done, but the EU and Member States have made big progress in reducing the flows of irregular migrants.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Predsedaj脙潞ci . 芒聙聯 Rozprava k tomuto bodu sa t脙陆mto skon脛聧ila.

P脙颅somn脙漏 vyhl脙隆senia (脛聧l脙隆nok 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽C脙漏sar Luena (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 Este debate de actualidad propuesto por el Grupo Patriotas por Europa se enmarca en el contexto general de legislatura en curso y del panorama pol脙颅tico actual marcado por un viraje significativo hacia la derecha y la extrema derecha, que ha influido profundamente en la configuraci脙鲁n del espectro pol脙颅tico y en las prioridades parlamentarias.

La extrema derecha ha aprovechado este debate para, una vez m脙隆s, difundir su discurso antimigratorio y xen脙鲁fobo, empleando informaci脙鲁n totalmente falsa y recurriendo a la desinformaci脙鲁n, como acostumbra.

As脙颅 mismo, los grupos de extrema derecha endurecen cada vez m脙隆s su discurso en materia migratoria, promoviendo pol脙颅ticas restrictivas contra la migraci脙鲁n y asociando a los migrantes con la criminalidad. Lo que plantea importantes debates sobre el futuro de la solidaridad europea.

Desde el grupo S&D, hemos resaltado de nuevo la necesidad de adoptar un enfoque migratorio basado en los derechos humanos, m脙隆s humanitario y haciendo hincapi脙漏 en la integraci脙鲁n, en consonancia con el recientemente adoptado Nuevo Pacto de Migraciones y Asilo.

Del mismo modo, el grupo socialista reclama el fortalecimiento de los instrumentos que permiten una migraci脙鲁n legal, segura y ordenada en aras a hacer frente al reto demogr脙隆fico que sufrir脙隆 la UE en los pr脙鲁ximos a脙卤os.

脗聽

9. Implementation of EU-US trade deal and the prospect of wider EU trade agreements (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Predsedaj脙潞ci . 芒聙聯 脛聨al脜隆脙颅m bodom programu s脙潞 vyhl脙隆senia Rady a Komisie o vykon脙隆van脙颅 obchodnej dohody medzi E脙職 a USA a perspekt脙颅ve 脜隆ir脜隆脙颅ch obchodn脙陆ch doh脙麓d E脙職 (2025/2853(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, Commissioner, since our previous debate in July, the EU has continued its efforts to establish a transatlantic trade. Our basic aim remains to create fair, clear and predictable conditions for transatlantic trade and supply chains.

The joint statement sets a framework for our future work. It defines concrete steps for both sides. Notably, it gives EU companies a better position than other US trading partners.

As you know, the Commission has proposed two legal measures concerning market access for US products. These measures are the first steps in the EU implementation of the July political deal. The Council has started the examination of the two proposals. We hope we can reach an agreement with the European Parliament that will benefit our businesses, workers and citizens.

While working for a predictable framework for transatlantic trade, the Council will continue its efforts to deepen existing partnerships around the world. We have received the decisions on the signing and the conclusion of two landmark agreements: the EU-Mercosur Partnership Agreement and the EU-Mexico Modernised Global Agreement.

We count on your constructive cooperation so we can achieve our common objectives: to diversify trade and to fully deliver on European competitiveness and innovation.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maro脜隆 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, on 27脗聽July at Turnberry, Europe faced a stark choice: risk possible economic catastrophe or act to protect our economy. And we chose the latter: the only responsible option for our businesses and jobs.

So this was not, as some have claimed, a capitulation or a sell-out or no negotiations. Far from it: it was a choice of stability and predictability over uncertainty, escalation and, ultimately, a trade war that would have benefited no one in Europe. And throughout these tough and demanding negotiations, our commitment to the EU values and its unique economic and social model has been unwavering. This model, including our regulatory autonomy, is not for sale, ever. And we stand ready to defend it, if need be also with our trade instruments. So the choice we have made was, and still is, in our best interests.

After spending hundreds of hours talking to my counterparts, I can tell you with clarity: the US is convinced that it needs to change the terms of its trade with the rest of the world. Our vision is different, but the world as we knew it will not come back, and nostalgia will not help us adapt to new reality. Making this deal is the right move for the European Union for four reasons.

First, by reaching an agreement with the United States, Europe avoided tariffs on our exports of 30脗聽% to 50脗聽%. This would have had a severe impact on our farmers, our carmakers, our manufacturers and our citizens. It would have severely damaged our political partnership and economic relations with the US, affecting EUR脗聽1.7脗聽trillion of trade every year, EUR脗聽5脗聽trillion in investment on both sides of the Atlantic, and 5.7脗聽million jobs that depend on EU-US trade. To be honest, I did not hear many in politics 芒聙聯 maybe until this morning 芒聙聯 and no one in the industry arguing that we should put this at risk, particularly not with the war still raging on the European continent. And ultimately, we secured the best deal possible.

Second, this agreement provides more stability and predictability 芒聙聯 vital for EU businesses suffering from volatility and uncertainty.

Third, the deal helps ensure access for European products to the US market on competitive terms, in particular compared to imports from other countries. Finally, the deal provides the opportunity to move beyond recent tensions. It creates a platform for more positive engagement with the US, with the roadmap for expanding market access and deepening our trade and investment relationship.

Given these factors, I believe that when we ask ourselves whether the EU is better off with this deal than without it, there is only one answer, and I am convinced that it is the right political decision for the EU now to follow through and implement this deal.

Honourable Members, I was doing my best to keep you abreast of all key developments over the past month. As for the next steps, the Commission has already followed through on our commitment to introduce legislation allowing for lowering of tariffs on certain products, in line with the joint statement.

We have presented to you two legislative proposals: one covering bilateral reductions of tariffs for all industrial products and some non-sensitive agriculture and fisheries products; the other relating to prolonging the so-called EU-US 'lobster deal'. They are now in the hands of this House and the Council.

We therefore stand at a crucial democratic moment. I have heard the criticism that some of you have expressed, but we now need to make the politically responsible choice and take the necessary steps to move our relationship with the US forward. I ask you for your support and assure you that the Commission stands ready to assist and find constructive solutions.

At the same time, we have to ensure that the US honours its obligation under the deal as well. As a first step, on 31脗聽July, the US issued an executive order confirming the implementation on 7脗聽August of a single, all-inclusive 15脗聽% tariff on imports of goods from the EU. On 5脗聽September, another executive order was signed, enabling Secretary of Commerce Howard Ludwig and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer to implement the tariff reductions and exemptions in the deal.

I welcome the confirmation by Secretary Ludwig on Monday that the US will implement these commitments by reducing tariffs on car and car parts from 27.5脗聽% to 15脗聽% retroactively from 1脗聽August 芒聙聯 crucial for the competitiveness of our car sector. It means savings of more than EUR脗聽500脗聽million each month in duties.

Similarly, I welcome the confirmation that the US will apply, retroactively as of 1脗聽September, exemptions from duties for various sectors, including aircraft and components, generic pharmaceuticals, active ingredients, some chemical and agricultural products, natural resources and other key inputs.

We have also worked with the US to expand the number of sectors that are exempted from the 15脗聽% tariff, including wines, spirits, medical devices and more. We are seeking to kick-start work with the US on ring-fencing our economies from overcapacity, and ensuring that EU exports of steel and aluminium to the US benefit from the tariff rate quota.

I have heard your concerns regarding the expansion of the derivative list. I have raised this with my US counterparts and I will continue doing so until we resolve this issue. Let me emphasise, however, that our draft legislation includes safeguards to ensure that we can suspend our tariff liberalisation in part or in full should the US not respect the terms of the deal. I want to assure you that I will continue our established practice and keep this House fully informed about, and involved in, the work to implement the joint statement.

I want to finish, Mr President, by considering the broader context of our global trade relations. After all, the US is not the only game in town. In 2024, the US was very important but represented 17脗聽% of our total trade. But we also need to take care of the other 83脗聽%. That means continuing our efforts to diversify our trade relations, so we are pushing an ambitious and pragmatic free trade agreement agenda to expand our trade, diversify our supply chains and offer our companies new export markets.

A week ago, I was proud to present the final text for the deals with Mercosur and Mexico. The EU-Mercosur partnership will create a market of over 700脗聽million people, with the tariffs saving over EUR脗聽4脗聽billion a year. And EU exports to Mercosur countries are expected to surge by 39脗聽% by 2040. Mexico is our second-largest trading partner in Latin America, a net food importer and one of our top suppliers of critical raw materials.

Elsewhere, President von der Leyen and Indonesian President Prabowo announced in July a political agreement to conclude an FTA this September. I will be travelling to Indonesia later this month with a view to doing just that. Before that, I will also visit India, where we aim to conclude negotiations on our FTA in 2025.

We will also move ahead with our other ongoing negotiations, such as those with the Philippines, Thailand, Malaysia, the United Arab Emirates and Australia. We are also working towards structured collaboration with our partners in the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership, known under the abbreviation CPTPP. Last, but not least, our support for the multilateral trading system and the WTO remains unwavering and we want to work together with the like-minded partners to reform it.

So we need to proceed with determination, courage and strong political will to protect our interests in a volatile global environment. The deal with the US restores stability and predictability with our number one trade and investment partner and a key ally, ensuring a platform from which we can take discussions forward.

As we progress with our global FTA agenda, we will be working with you very, very closely on all these negotiations. At this stage, I would like to thank you for your kind attention, and I am very much looking forward to your questions and interventions.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽J脙露rgen Warborn, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Minister, Commissioner, we are indeed living in very uncertain times. We have war on our continent and, unfortunately, we still need the US for security reasons. Therefore, I think it would be very irresponsible to also enter into a trade war with the US at this time.

This deal is not perfect. I do not particularly like the outcome of this deal, but I think the most responsible thing to do at this moment is to go through with the deal and then continue to negotiate. I know there are groups or MEPs in this Chamber that would like to escalate the situation and then continue to negotiate. I do not think that will have the best outcomes for Europeans.

So it is not a perfect deal, but it is the deal that we have on the table, and we have security reasons for adopting the deal. We have to change it a bit, though. Of course we are not voting on the deal as such. We will be voting on the two legislative proposals that the Commissioner mentioned, and we will certainly have to amend it in order to make sure, not least, that it is fully compatible with WTO rules. And I have mentioned several times in this Chamber and to the Commission that I am not certain it does. I am rather quite certain that it does not. But I think, colleagues, that we, in this House, could fix that by amending the legislation.

In the meantime, we also, of course, have to continue to negotiate and, not least, ratify the agreements we have on the table. The Mercosur Agreement is really important from us at this point of stage. When we have problems doing business in one part of the world 芒聙聭脗聽the US right now 芒聙聭 then we have to open business in other parts of the world. Mercosur is one. Mexico is another. Indonesia. India. Thailand. Malaysia. Philippines. Australia. There is a lot of possibilities, and I think possibilities to negotiate are better now than ever.

So I urge you, colleagues, please do not create this trade war with Trump. We do not need it. We need security in Europe and we need these deals.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

IN THE CHAIR: JAVI L脙聯PEZ
Vice-President

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bernd Lange, im Namen der S&D-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Herr Kommissar, Frau Ministerin! Wir machen keinen trade war. Das ist wirklich der Ausgangspunkt in den USA. Wir sehen, dass wir in eine Situation gekommen sind, wo wir quasi erpresst worden sind, auch mit der Frage unserer Sicherheitspartnerschaft. Gl脙录cklicherweise ist die Zeit der K脙露nige vorbei, wo K脙露nige Vertr脙陇ge machen, ohne die Bev脙露lkerung zu befragen. Jetzt ist die Zeit des Parlamentes.

Und Maro脜隆: Sicherheit und Stabilit脙陇t bringt dieser Deal nicht.脗聽14脗聽Tage, nachdem der Deal geschlossen worden ist, hat der USTR 407脗聽Produkte aus den 15脗聽Prozent herausgenommen und unter 50脗聽Prozent Stahl und Aluminium gesetzt. Und damit werden auch keine Arbeitspl脙陇tze gesichert, sondern ich kann dir die Fabriken zeigen, die Kompressoren, Pumpen herstellen, die jetzt 40脗聽Prozent Zoll zahlen m脙录ssen und noch selbst nachweisen m脙录ssen, wie viel Stahl und Aluminium in den Produkten ist脗聽芒聙聯 und die sind in Existenznot.

Also insofern m脙录ssen wir hier im Parlament agieren und die M脙露glichkeiten nutzen, auch den Legislativvorschlag zu 脙陇ndern. Solange diese Situation besteht, dass europ脙陇ische Firmen durch die Zollpolitik der USA infrage gestellt werden, bin ich nicht bereit, eins zu eins den Vorschlag der Kommission durchzuwinken. Hier brauchen wir 脙聞nderungsantr脙陇ge.

Und wir brauchen auch 脙聞nderungsantr脙陇ge zu einem klaren Link zwischen Ver脙陇nderungen der USA und dem, wie wir uns ver脙陇ndern脗聽芒聙聯 sehr schnell und eindeutig, weil es da keine Stillhalteklausel gibt. Die haben es abgelehnt zu sagen: Das ist das Ende der Fahnenstange.

Und das dritte: Ja, ich m脙露chte auch, dass das WTO-kompatibel ist. Und was Ursula von der Leyen heute Morgen im Plenum gesagt hat, dass sie froh ist, dass wir einen besseren Deal haben als andere, und damit die ausstechen k脙露nnen, die im Wettbewerb mit uns stehen, widerspricht doch total der WTO. Wir m脙录ssen sehen, dass wir unseren Deal in einen Rahmen bringen, wo auch die L脙陇nder des Globalen S脙录dens eine Chance haben, gemeinsam mit uns weiter Handel zu treiben, nicht nur in Handelsabkommen, die wir schlie脙聼en, sondern auch hinsichtlich des US-Deals.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Christophe Bay, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, l'accord sign脙漏 par Ursula脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen et Donald脗聽Trump est un march脙漏 de dupes. L'Europe a tout donn脙漏: suppression des droits sur les produits industriels am脙漏ricains, ouverture de notre march脙漏 europ脙漏en aux produits agricoles am脙漏ricains bourr脙漏s de produits interdits en Europe.

Monsieur le Commissaire, vous parlez de stabilit脙漏. Mais alors comment expliquer que l'Europe accepte de renoncer 脙聽 3,6脗聽milliards d'euros de recettes? Que gagne la France dans cet accord? Rien. Ni le vin, ni les spiritueux n'ont obtenu la moindre ouverture. Nos viticulteurs sont les grands sacrifi脙漏s, tandis que, il faut le reconna脙庐tre, l'industrie automobile allemande est la grande b脙漏n脙漏ficiaire.

Comme l'a rappel脙漏 M.脗聽Warborn, comme l'a rappel脙漏 le pr脙漏sident脗聽Lange, en outre, cet accord viole les r脙篓gles de l'OMC. Il est le symbole d'une Europe qui c脙篓de sous la pression au lieu de d脙漏fendre ses int脙漏r脙陋ts. Nous avons besoin d'accords commerciaux 脙漏quilibr脙漏s et respectueux de nos fili脙篓res, pas d'une capitulation face aux diktats allemands. Cet accord est gagnant pour les 脙聣tats-Unis, perdant pour l'Europe et humiliant pour la France. Sachez que le Rassemblement national s'opposera fermement 脙聽 cet accord.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Daniele Polato, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, l'accordo con gli Stati Uniti 脙篓 un punto fermo da cui ripartire, d脙聽 stabilit脙聽 e chiarezza, come chiesto da categorie ed imprenditori.

Nella dichiarazione comune sottoscritta ai punti 9, 10, 11 e 12, la Commissione europea si impegna ad adoperarsi per fornire modifiche e flessibilit脙聽 nell'attuazione di direttive e regolamenti come deforestazione, CBAM, due diligence, al fine di ridurre gli oneri alle imprese americane: questo 脙篓 quello che da sempre chiediamo per il rilancio della competitivit脙聽 europea.

Dobbiamo, parallelamente all'accordo commerciale con gli Stati Uniti, procedere con i tagli di quelle normative che bloccano l'economia europea e aprire a nuovi mercati, accelerando negoziati che, ad oggi, non sono stati conclusi, come il GSP.

Gli Emirati Arabi, il Messico, l'India, i paesi ASEAN sono partner con cui dobbiamo concludere ed intensificare la cooperazione.

Da ultimo permettetemi di ringraziare il Commissario 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧 e le direzioni interessate per aver chiuso il miglior accordo possibile date le circostanze, preferendo la via del dialogo negoziale ai "bazooka commerciali".

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Karin Karlsbro, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, Council, colleagues, there is no reason to put lipstick on a pig. The deal with the United States is not good. It's not good for the EU, nor for the US or for shared security interests. It's because of Trump, who has taken the United States out of rules-based international order and into an era of disorder and chaos.

In this situation, a stronger EU is needed more than ever. But I hear colleagues from the extremes, from the left and from the right, complain that we did not get a better deal, while at the same time doing everything they can to weaken the EU. This is not serious. Our best response is now to strengthen the single market, to say yes to more free trade agreements, to support Ukraine and to impose tougher sanctions on Russia.

We must also draw a hard line when Trump attempts to interfere with legislation that is ours to decide. We can't have a situation where Donald Trump dictates the rules for the biggest tech companies operating in Europe, or how we shall protect children from addictive use and endless scrolling of social media. That is up to us to decide. No one else.

When the EU speaks, it does so with the voice of 27 countries, 450 million Europeans, and the world's second-largest economy. Let's use that.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Saskia Bricmont, au nom du groupe Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, quelle stupeur cet 脙漏t脙漏 de d脙漏couvrir le deal, la capitulation de la Commission europ脙漏enne face 脙聽 Trump en acceptant des droits de douane 脙聽 0脗聽% pour les produits am脙漏ricains, 脙聽 15脗聽% pour les produits europ脙漏ens.

Pr脙漏tendre que c'est facile, 脙漏videmment on ne le fera pas. On a eu plusieurs 脙漏changes ici, au Parlement europ脙漏en, et il me semble que le Parlement europ脙漏en a 脙漏t脙漏 tr脙篓s clair. Nous voulons une unit脙漏 europ脙漏enne face 脙聽 Trump; il ne sera pas facile de n脙漏gocier avec ce personnage, mais l'Union europ脙漏enne a entre ses mains des instruments qu'elle doit actionner: l'instrument anti-coercition, qui est l脙聽 exactement pour ce genre de cas de figure, mais aussi cibler脗聽芒聙聯 la possibilit脙漏, en tout cas, de le faire脗聽芒聙聯 les services am脙漏ricains ou encore imposer les grandes entreprises technologiques am脙漏ricaines. Mais non! Le choix de la Commission europ脙漏enne a 脙漏t脙漏 de donner raison 脙聽 Trump dans un deal totalement asym脙漏trique 脙聽 l'avantage des 脙聣tats-Unis.

Alors, que vaut la promesse de Mme脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, entendue ce matin, que l'Union europ脙漏enne d脙漏cidera toujours pour elle-m脙陋me de ses propres standards et r脙漏gulations environnementales et digitales? Que vaut cette promesse face 脙聽 un Trump que le deal asym脙漏trique n'a apparemment pas rassasi脙漏, vu qu'il veut revoir nos l脙漏gislations, qu'il critique l'Union europ脙漏enne qui entend imposer Google et qu'il entend aussi b脙漏n脙漏ficier de flexibilit脙漏.

Que vaut la promesse aussi de Mme脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen que l'Union europ脙漏enne doit atteindre son ind脙漏pendance 脙漏nerg脙漏tique quand, dans ce deal, elle promet aux 脙聣tats-Unis d'investir 750脗聽milliards de dollars pour les 脙漏nergies fossiles et l'脙漏nergie nucl脙漏aire, alors que cet argent doit imp脙漏rativement 脙陋tre investi en Europe?

Le manque de vision pour l'avenir et pour l'autonomie europ脙漏enne est profond脙漏ment pr脙漏occupant. Nous n'accepterons pas ce deal en l'脙漏tat. Nous n'avons pas 脙聽 nous prononcer sur les autres aspects du deal, et pourtant c'est l'avenir d脙漏mocratique de l'Union europ脙漏enne qui en d脙漏pend. Nous craignions que ce soit l'extr脙陋me droite le fossoyeur de l'Europe, mais depuis un an, depuis les diff脙漏rentes d脙漏r脙漏gulations, les paquets Omnibus, la capitulation face 脙聽 Trump, mais aussi face 脙聽 la Chine, les diff脙漏rentes d脙漏cisions de renationalisation, on ne peut que constater que pour l'instant le PPE est en train de d脙漏tricoter l'Europe et de nous rendre extr脙陋mement vuln脙漏rables.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Martin Schirdewan, im Namen der Fraktion The Left. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Herr Kommissar, ich finde, Sie haben wirklich eine sehr spezielle positive Lesart dieses Handelsdeals. Ich glaube, es t脙陇te uns gut, wenn Sie uns hier nicht in die Tasche l脙录gen w脙录rden. Z脙露lle auf europ脙陇ische Industrieg脙录ter von 15脗聽Prozent, 50脗聽Prozent auf Stahl und Aluminium, und im Gegenzug k脙露nnen amerikanische Industrieprodukte zum Nulltarif in die Europ脙陇ische Union exportiert bzw. dann aus unserer Perspektive importiert werden: Das ist nat脙录rlich ein Angriff auf unsere industrielle Basis und wird viele tausend Arbeitspl脙陇tze vernichten. Kollegen haben darauf hingewiesen, und Sie wissen das auch.

Und dieser Deal ist nicht, wie einige dargestellt haben, vonseiten der Europ脙陇er losgetreten, dieser Krieg, dieser Handelskrieg, ist nicht vonseiten der Europ脙陇er losgetreten worden, sondern von der Regierung Trump. Sie haben sich aber in diesem Handelskrieg nicht gewehrt, und deshalb kann man durchaus sagen, dass dieser Deal eine totale Niederlage in diesem Handelskrieg ist.

Und zus脙陇tzlich werden 750脗聽Milliarden脗聽Euro in US-Fracking-Gas und -脙聳l investiert; 600脗聽Milliarden脗聽Euro sollen vor allem in die US-Milit脙陇rindustrie flie脙聼en. W脙陇hrend die EU versucht, ihre digitale Souver脙陇nit脙陇t mit Gesetzen wie DMA und DSA zu st脙陇rken, und zu Recht gegen Unternehmen wie Google vorgeht, droht die Trump-Administration mit massiven Zollerh脙露hungen gegen L脙陇nder, die angeblich US-Tech-Firmen diskriminieren. Das ist der Ausverkauf der digitalen Zukunft, europ脙陇ischer Energiesouver脙陇nit脙陇t, der Sicherheit und des Klimaschutzes.

Herr Kommissar, Sie zementieren dauerhafte Abh脙陇ngigkeiten von den USA. Alles, was von dieser Kommission von einer strategisch unabh脙陇ngigen Europ脙陇ischen Union erz脙陇hlt wird, ist mit diesem Deal hinf脙陇llig. Meine Fraktion wird diesen Deal nicht mittragen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Arno Bausemer, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Die USA k脙露nnen sich gl脙录cklich sch脙陇tzen: Man muss Donald Trump ja nicht m脙露gen, aber der US-Pr脙陇sident erf脙录llt zu 100脗聽Prozent seine Aufgabe. Er handelt im wahrsten Sinne des Wortes im Interesse seiner Mitb脙录rger.

Auf der anderen Seite des Tisches hingegen sitzt Ursula von der Leyen, eine Frau, die bei der Europawahl auf keinem einzigen Stimmzettel gestanden hat, die bereits mit fragw脙录rdigen Impfdeals negativ in Erscheinung getreten ist, die mit dem脗聽Green Deal seit Jahren die Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit unserer Industrie gef脙陇hrdet und die erst vor einigen Wochen mit Ach und Krach hier im Hause ein Misstrauensvotum 脙录berstanden hat.

Derart geschw脙陇cht hat Frau von der Leyen nun also einen Deal pr脙陇sentiert, der unsere europ脙陇ische Wirtschaft weiter in Bedr脙陇ngnis bringt. Bei Z脙露llen auf Stahl und Aluminium von 50脗聽Prozent sind ja auch alle Endprodukte betroffen, und genau das macht diese Endprodukte wie Maschinen derart teuer, dass unsere deutschen Exporteure die H脙陇nde 脙录ber dem Kopf zusammenschlagen. Die Deutsche Industrie- und Handelskammer hat ermittelt, dass mehr als die H脙陇lfte der Unternehmen diesen Deal f脙录r eine Belastung halten. Es wird Zeit, dass die Mehrheit dieses EU-Parlaments erkennt, dass die Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin selbst mittlerweile die gr脙露脙聼te Belastung f脙录r die Zukunft der europ脙陇ischen Wirtschaft ist.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脜陆eljana Zovko (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear colleagues, before our eyes, a new world order is emerging. Authoritarian leaders are watching closely, waiting for the Western alliance to show weakness.脗聽This is why I welcome this trade agreement as a clear sign of renewed transatlantic cooperation 芒聙聯 something that is urgently needed at this critical moment.

The European Union cannot afford to find itself on the wrong side of history. To those colleagues who reject this agreement and call for harsher measures, I must say such positions are deeply irresponsible in these challenging times. Yes, this agreement is not perfect, but it provides stability and predictability 芒聙聯 qualities our workers and businesses desperately need right now 芒聙聯 and not demagogic talk.

This deal must be seen as the only first step towards building a stable, balanced and fair transatlantic economy. We must strengthen our coordination to develop a unified strategy for ensuring economic security and preventing unfair competition. Only in a strong, competitive and prosperous European Union, supported by powerful partners, can we continue to safeguard security, stability and our shared values.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jussi Saramo (The Left), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Thank you for your speech. You are the second one to say that it's not perfect. So I would like to ask: what are the good things in this Trump芒聙聯von der Leyen deal?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脜陆eljana Zovko (PPE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 The good things are that our jobs now have predictability and stability: 15脗聽% is better than 39脗聽% or 50脗聽% or 100脗聽%, or whatever. If you didn't do the maths, that's a good thing. If you are measuring high blood pressure, going over that would be a really bad thing for our businesses.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Van Brempt (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, colleagues, can I start by saying that nobody 芒聙聯 I guess nobody 芒聙聯 is asking for an escalation in the trade war, especially not the S&D. But that doesn't matter that this is a good deal or that the response of Europe is sufficient. And yes, I do talk to industries and workers in the industry as well; they feel the impact of these tariffs every single day.

From the very beginning, we, as the S&D, were crystal clear: Trump's tariff war is illegal, it is unfair and it is unacceptable. And on top of that, I have to say to our EPP colleagues, unfortunately 芒聙聯 and I really say unfortunately 芒聙聯 it does not give our people and businesses the certainty and the stability that they need. Just look at the 407 products that were added to the 50脗聽% tariff after the deal was struck.

Dear colleagues, we can never allow for the rule of international law to be replaced by the law of the jungle. So we need solutions 芒聙聯 and they will not come from the US, they need to come from Europe, and that requires a strong, broad and united response that we will have to work on in this Parliament.

Therefore, we urge you, Commissioner, first to look to activate the anti-coercion instrument. It will make us ready to take countermeasures when necessary in the face of Trump's threats. We need to implement a sunset clause, take measures to protect our industries and strengthen the internal markets. And third, we need to unite with our partners around the globe 芒聙聯 like-minded partners 芒聙聯 to coordinate our response to the US and to China. President von der Leyen said this morning a new Europe must emerge, and that requires leadership and courage. It does 芒聙聯 for our workers, our businesses and in our future. And I count on your support to do that.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽J脙露rgen Warborn (PPE), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Thank you for accepting the blue card. Well, you said that you don't want to initiate a trade war, and let's say, it is difficult times for Europe and the situation is not pleasant for any one of us. But you also say that you would like to use the ACI 芒聙聯 the 'trade bazooka'. Isn't that as close as you would come to respond? That will lead to a trade war.

And don't you think that that will make it even harder to negotiate a better deal after you initiate the ACI? And still we know that the Member States, they do not have any appetite for this.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Van Brempt (S&D), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Well, fortunately, I am not part of the Member States. Well, I am part of a Member State, but not from the Council. And I understand your question. We said from the start, in many debates we had in the INTA Committee that we wanted to activate the Anti-Coercion Instrument. It doesn't mean that you have to use it, but then you stand ready to take much broader countermeasures than only on tariffs.

You know, one of the reasons why the countermeasures on tariffs were not so effective in making sure that it makes some impression to Trump is because in the Council there were so many exceptions. And we already, from the start, said we also have to look at services. And the Anti-Coercion Instrument will give us the opportunity to have much more broader measures and we can take them into account.

You saw what happened exactly after this deal. Immediately, Trump started to threaten us when it came to the DSA and the DMA. So we need to stand ready. And that's why we want to activate the Anti-Coercion Instrument.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Enik脜聭 Gy脜聭ri (PfE). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt K脙漏pvisel脜聭t脙隆rsaim! Ez a meg脙隆llapod脙隆s Eur脙鲁pa gyenges脙漏g脙漏nek 脙漏s zsarolhat脙鲁s脙隆g脙隆nak bizony脙颅t脙漏ka. Az Uni脙鲁 egyre betegebb. 2008-ban a vil脙隆ggazdas脙隆g negyed脙漏t adta, ma 17%-脙隆t. Tegnap 脙鲁ta azt is tudjuk, hogy a javallott recept, a Draghi芒聙聯jelent脙漏s, 11%-脙隆t siker脙录lt csak v脙漏grehajtani egy 脙漏v alatt. Ne csod脙隆lkozzunk teh脙隆t, ha az Uni脙鲁t lekezelik 脙漏s megal脙隆zz脙隆k a nemzetk脙露zi sz脙颅nt脙漏ren, ahogy az a turnberry-i golfp脙隆ly脙隆n t脙露rt脙漏nt. De tudjuk az ok脙隆t. A Bizotts脙隆g nem titkolta: ez egy politikai alku volt az脙漏rt, hogy az USA tov脙隆bbra is finansz脙颅rozza Ukrajn脙隆t. Teh脙隆t von der Leyennek fontosabb Ukrajna, mint a saj脙隆t polg脙隆rai 脙漏s v脙隆llalkoz脙隆sai. 脙聣n nem tudok tapsolni annak, hogy az exportok ut脙隆n eddig 2-3%-ot fizett脙录nk, most 15-脙露t kell, mik脙露zben az amerikai ipari term脙漏kek v脙隆mmentesen j脙露nnek be. Ez t脙隆vol van a tisztess脙漏ges, k脙露lcs脙露n脙露s 脙漏s kiegyens脙潞lyozott meg脙隆llapod脙隆st脙鲁l.

R脙隆ad脙隆sul nem esett sz脙鲁 arr脙鲁l, mik脙漏nt k脙颅v脙隆nja a Bizotts脙隆g kompenz脙隆lni a vesztes szektorokat, p脙漏ld脙隆ul az eur脙鲁pai bor脙隆szatot. A Bizotts脙隆g t脙潞lterjeszkedett a hat脙隆sk脙露r脙漏n, 脙颅g脙漏rt Amerik脙隆nak 芒聙聻cheap芒聙聺 energiav脙隆s脙隆rl脙隆st - felhatalmaz脙隆s n脙漏lk脙录l - 脙漏s beruh脙隆z脙隆sokat. A t脙露rt脙漏nelemk脙露nyvekbe j脙潞lius 28-a Eur脙鲁pa megal脙隆ztat脙隆s脙隆nak napjak脙漏nt fog bevonulni.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rihards Kols (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, I guess there is a confusion. We are not talking about the EU-US trade agreement. We are talking about a political deal. So there is a long way ahead to actually reach the trade deal. But trade in general is good and freer trade is even better. So this principle really has underpinned decades of the EU-US cooperation. And while this new arrangement is imperfect, a deal, even a political one, is better than no deal.

But we need to be serious. Tariffs are back and they are not balanced: 15 % of the most EU goods, while US exports enjoy broad exemptions. That is not a sustainable model for the world's two largest democratic economies.

We welcome the political will to avoid escalation, but political will must now be followed by structure, transparency and enforcement. Our businesses are still operating in a grey zone. Legal ambiguity, regulatory inconsistency. That is not how to strengthen transatlantic supply chains, especially not in a global environment where authoritarian regimes are aggressively consolidating influence.

So three things must be pushed for. First, a formal review mechanism with a fixed deadline. Second, a clear legal framework that gives EU exporters predictability. And third, but not least, a roadmap, ambitious but realistic towards a barrier-free transatlantic trade area. That is what will keep the EU-US partnership competitive, resilient and strategic.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie-Pierre Vedrenne (Renew). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, ce deal n'est pas un deal. Il incarne une id脙漏ologie et une strat脙漏gie, celle du protectionnisme, celle de l'unilat脙漏ralisme, celle de l'autoritarisme: tout ce que je rejette en tant que femme politique, tout ce que je combats en tant que lib脙漏rale, tout ce que je m脙漏prise en tant que d脙漏mocrate.

Monsieur le Commissaire, je mesure votre difficult脙漏 et votre engagement pour vous aussi r脙漏sister. Mais le jour d'apr脙篓s脗聽芒聙聯 car soyons certains que Donald脗聽Trump ne s'arr脙陋tera pas l脙聽脗聽芒聙聯 souhaitons-nous donner aux Europ脙漏ens, une nouvelle fois, l'image de responsables faibles et c脙漏dant au narratif de la Maison blanche? Ce jour d'apr脙篓s, il doit 脙陋tre anticip脙漏, car ni l'espoir ni la nostalgie ne nous aideront face au chantage permanent; ce jour d'apr脙篓s, il doit correspondre au renforcement de l'unit脙漏 europ脙漏enne, 脙聽 l'acc脙漏l脙漏ration de la sortie de nos d脙漏pendances et 脙聽 l'utilisation de l'instrument sur la coercition 脙漏conomique. Car ici, en Europe, ni l'intimidation ni les menaces n'ont leur place. C'est 脙聽 nous de retrouver nos propres choix, notre propre destin.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kira Marie Peter-Hansen (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Hr. Formand! N脙楼r Trump truer Europa, s脙楼 er svaret ikke at b脙赂je nakken. Derfor g脙赂r det mig urolig at l脙娄se i den her handelsaftale, at EU-Kommissionen lover, at vores regler for b脙娄redygtighed og menneskerettigheder ikke m脙楼 blive en hindring for amerikansk handel. For EU's b脙娄redygtighedsregler er sat i verden for at sikre, at virksomheder ikke tjener penge p脙楼 tvangsarbejde, p脙楼 forurening eller p脙楼 b脙赂rnearbejde. Det er grundl脙娄ggende europ脙娄iske v脙娄rdier, som har gjort Europa st脙娄rkt og rigt. Og nu er det tid til, at Europa ranker ryggen og st脙楼r vagt om de v脙娄rdier, for hvis vi f脙赂rst begynder at lade vores politik diktere af et USA, der f脙赂rer handelskrig mod Europa med den ene h脙楼nd og fors脙赂ger at afpresse os med den anden, ja s脙楼 svigter vi ikke bare vores virksomheder, s脙楼 svigter vi vores europ脙娄iske v脙娄rdier, og vi svigter den styrkeposition, som Europa har opbygget over 脙楼rtier. Nemlig et Europa med de h脙赂jeste standarder inden for forbrugerrettigheder, gr脙赂nne ambitioner og ligestilling, et Europa, der er ambiti脙赂st og n脙娄gter at v脙娄re en del af r脙娄set mod bunden. S脙楼 derfor, k脙娄re Kommission: Drop fedteriet for Trump og tro p脙楼 Europa!

脗聽脗聽Jussi Saramo (The Left). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, Euroopan antautumissopimus. Siit脙陇 on kyse Trumpin ja Von der Leyenin sopimuksessa. Yhdysvalloilla ei ole mit脙陇脙陇n moraalista oikeutta vaatia EU:lta tukea heid脙陇n teollisuudelleen, eik脙陇 komissiolla ollut mit脙陇脙陇n laillista oikeutta sellaista luvata.

Trump perustelee tulleja sill脙陇, ett脙陇 Yhdysvallat ostaa Euroopasta enemm脙陇n tavaroita kuin myy, mutta palvelukaupassa tilanne on t脙陇ysin p脙陇invastainen. Silti Trump kielsi EU:ta verottamasta vastapainoksi yhdysvaltalaisten digij脙陇ttien t脙陇脙陇lt脙陇 ker脙陇脙陇mi脙陇 voittoja, ja oikeisto tottelee n脙露yr脙陇sti.

Trump on koulupihan pahin 脙露ykk脙陇ri, joka kiusaa muita yksi kerrallaan. Orb脙隆n, Meloni ja muu naurettavaa kulttuurisotaansa k脙陇yv脙陇 oikeisto taputtaa vieress脙陇, vaikka Trump vie heid脙陇nkin taskurahansa. T脙陇h脙陇n ei pid脙陇 suostua. Kiristykseen ei koskaan pid脙陇 suostua. Mit脙陇 enemm脙陇n rahaa ja tukea lupaamme Yhdysvaltoihin kaataa, sit脙陇 enemm脙陇n meilt脙陇 aina vaaditaan lis脙陇脙陇.

脗聽脗聽Fabio De Masi (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, meine Damen und Herren! Der Deal von Frau von der Leyen ist eine Kapitulation und hat die Lebensdauer einer Eintagsfliege: 15脗聽Prozent auf europ脙陇ische Waren in die USA, 0脗聽Prozent auf US-Ausfuhren nach Europa, und nach wenigen Tagen hie脙聼 es: 50脗聽Prozent auf unseren Stahl- und Aluminiumsektor. Das ist ein 脙露konomischer Genickschuss, und die EU-Kommission hat sich eben in eine v脙露llig einseitige Abh脙陇ngigkeit von den USA man脙露vriert. W脙陇hrend uns Donald Trump mit Strafz脙露llen 脙录berzieht, hauen wir ihm bei schmutzigem Fracking-Gas und bei der R脙录stungsbeschaffung die Auftragsb脙录cher voll脗聽芒聙聯 das ist ja eine v脙露llig schizophrene Herangehensweise!

Wir haben unsere Daumenschrauben gar nicht ausgepackt. Wir sind der gr脙露脙聼te Binnenmarkt der Erde: Wir k脙露nnten zum Beispiel Strafsteuern auf US-Big-Tech-Konzerne verh脙陇ngen, die hier nicht ordentlich Steuern zahlen. Nichts h脙露rt man dazu! Wir k脙露nnten zum Beispiel die Verm脙露gen von US-Milliard脙陇ren ins Visier nehmen, wie es zum Beispiel renommierte 脙聳konomen wie Gabriel Zucman vom EU Tax Observatory fordern. Nichts davon wurde auf den Tisch gepackt, und gleichzeitig hat Frau von der Leyen noch zugesagt, f脙录r 750脗聽Milliarden脗聽US-Dollar Fracking-Gas und andere Energieerzeugnisse aus den USA einzukaufen. Wie will sie das 脙录berhaupt versprechen, sie trifft doch nicht die Investitionsentscheidungen der Unternehmen!

Das ist eine Vereinbarung zulasten Dritter, deswegen gibt es nur eine Konsequenz: Frau von der Leyen sollte f脙录r diesen Deal zur脙录cktreten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Ignacio Zoido 脙聛lvarez (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, creo que se est脙隆 poniendo de manifiesto que el acuerdo con los Estados Unidos no es el que habr脙颅amos deseado los europeos, pero, como dec脙颅a Bismarck: 脗芦La pol脙颅tica es el arte de lo posible, no de lo deseable脗禄.

El acuerdo alcanzado es, probablemente, el mejor de los posibles con la actual Administraci脙鲁n Trump: con 脙漏l se evita un choque mayor con nuestro principal socio comercial y ganamos certidumbre para nuestros productores. Por todo ello, valoramos el trabajo del equipo negociador en un contexto claramente adverso.

Sin embargo, quedan problemas de fondo que tenemos que procurar resolver: los aranceles al acero y al aluminio siguen amenazando nuestra industria y sectores como el agroalimentario podr脙颅an perder competitividad si flexibilizamos las normas para la entrada de productos estadounidenses.

Si la certidumbre es el argumento a favor de este acuerdo, la necesitamos para todos los sectores y que sea prolongada en el tiempo. Europa no puede conformarse con soluciones a medias: si este es el acuerdo elegido para defender nuestros intereses no podemos dejar a ning脙潞n sector atr脙隆s.

(El orador acepta responder a una pregunta formulada con arreglo al procedimiento de la 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jonas Sj脙露stedt (The Left), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 When we were kids, we sometimes met the schoolyard bullies, those who threaten us with violence. Those who asked to have our money. They might even steal your phone. You can take two approaches to the bullies. You can give them what they want and humiliate yourself. And they will most likely be back the day after asking for your money again. Or you can take a stand together with others and say, 'No, this is not fair.'

What makes you think that the bully Donald Trump won't come back asking for more?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Ignacio Zoido 脙聛lvarez (PPE), respuesta de 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 No voy a entrar en las descalificaciones que hacen hacia quien ha sido elegido democr脙隆ticamente presidente de un Estado. Esa experiencia que usted tuvo cuando era joven quiz脙隆 yo la he podido tener tambi脙漏n, pero hay algo que est脙隆 muy claro: se ha podido alcanzar un acuerdo en condiciones muy dif脙颅ciles 芒聙聰como acabo de decir en mi intervenci脙鲁n芒聙聰 y tenemos que hacer un esfuerzo para no dejar absolutamente a nadie atr脙隆s.

脗驴Qu脙漏 era lo que ten脙颅amos? Yo creo que se gana en previsibilidad 芒聙聰no cabe la menor duda de que esto es as脙颅芒聙聰, las empresas saben a qu脙漏 atenerse, y vamos a tener un tratamiento mejor que otras naciones. Pero hay algo que tambi脙漏n me preocupa mucho: las circunstancias que podr脙颅amos tener de no haberse alcanzado este acuerdo. Hay cosas que son todav脙颅a mucho peores, por eso vamos a intentar hacer todo lo posible y no aquello que no se puede alcanzar, porque tenemos que ser razonables.

Y le voy a garantizar una cosa: siempre he defendido que la humillaci脙鲁n no es el camino para andar por la vida, as脙颅 que tampoco en pol脙颅tica. Humillaciones, ninguna.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Brando Benifei (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, Trump minaccia sanzioni all'Europa perch脙漏 non gli piacciono le nostre leggi e von der Leyen sorvola su tutto questo nel discorso odierno, ma non possiamo farci ricattare e nessun accordo pu脙虏 basarsi sulla coercizione e le minacce.

Il presidente del PPE, Manfred Weber, ha detto che l'accordo UE-USA non lo convince, ma va sostenuto per senso di responsabilit脙聽.

Io credo, invece, che abbiamo la responsabilit脙聽 politica di dire la verit脙聽 ai nostri concittadini, 脙篓 cio脙篓 che non possiamo accettare l'accordo cos脙卢 com'脙篓 oggi se vogliamo fare il bene e gli interessi degli europei.

Il Parlamento europeo lavorer脙聽 sul regolamento sui dazi presentato dalla Commissione: dobbiamo inserire una data di scadenza all'accordo per poterlo rinegoziare entro un tempo definito; dobbiamo proteggere acciaio e alluminio e i prodotti che li contengono, fondamentali per la nostra industria, dai dazi ultra punitivi del 50脗聽%; dobbiamo tutelare la nostra agricoltura e la produzione vitivinicola, con esenzioni da negoziare subito.

La Commissione europea ha portato avanti un lavoro molto difficile. Alcuni governi, come quello italiano, hanno da subito boicottato questo sforzo, prostrandosi all'amministrazione americana, ma ora dobbiamo reagire.

Per questo dobbiamo aprire nuove rotte commerciali per l'Europa, rafforzare la nostra economia internamente e ricostruire un rapporto transatlantico equilibrato e basato sul rispetto.

Il lavoro continuer脙聽 in modo costruttivo, ma fermo e con un unico obiettivo: gli interessi dei nostri cittadini, dei nostri lavoratori e delle nostre imprese, difendendo i principi senza i quali il mondo diventa una pericolosa arena senza regole, anche in questo campo. Non possiamo permettercelo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vilis Kri脜隆topans (PfE). 芒聙聯 Priek脜隆s脛聯d脛聯t脛聛ja kungs, kol脛聯脛拢i! M脜芦su r脜芦pn脛芦cas ir apdraud脛聯tas, m脜芦su zemnieki ir atst脛聛ti vieni, un m脛聯s maks脛聛sim cenu par vieno脜隆anos, kas tika slepus nosl脛聯gta. Urzula fon der Leiena dar脛芦jumu starp Eiropas Savien脛芦bu un Ameriku sauc par pan脛聛kumu, bet paties脛芦b脛聛 tas ir kompromiss bez taisn脛芦guma un Eiropas nodo脜隆ana bez mand脛聛ta.

Tagad uz脜聠脛聯m脛聯jiem ir izdev脛芦g脛聛k atv脛聯rt r脜芦pn脛芦cas ASV nevis 脜隆eit, Eirop脛聛, un tas noz脛芦m脛聯 zaud脛聯tas darbavietas, nodok脛录u ie脜聠脛聯mumus un m脜芦su n脛聛kotni. Pret 脜隆o vieno脜隆anos iebilst ne tikai opoz脛芦cija. To kritiz脛聯 vald脛芦bu p脛聛rst脛聛vji, r脜芦pniec脛芦bas l脛芦deri un ar脛芦 da脛录a no liel脛聛kaj脛聛m frakcij脛聛m 脜隆aj脛聛 parlament脛聛. Eiropa ir sp脛聯c脛芦ga, m脜芦su tirgus ir milz脛芦gs, un tom脛聯r m脛聯s sa脜聠脛聯m脛聛m tikai drupa脛聧as. T脛聛 nav strat脛聯脛拢ija, t脛聛 ir kapitul脛聛cija.

Urzula fon der Leienas kundze! J脜芦su laiks ir beidzies! Atk脛聛pieties, l脜芦dzu!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dominik Tarczy脜聞ski (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, thank you. Dear leftists, you have been attacking President Trump for years. Now you are whining about tariffs. You are not happy that he doesn't want to be your private bodyguard. But it's not Trump's decision to spend money on illegal migrants instead of the army or industry. It's not Trump's fault that you restricted European industry because of your green god and because of your sick ideology. It's not his fault!

It's time to be a man. It's time to take care of yourself. It's time to stop wearing dresses and be a man. Put on armour, not a dress. Your laughter will not change the fact that this is your fault. This is your fault: you destroy and make Europe weak.

So be a man; be strong; be brave; be like Poland. You're not going to laugh in a minute. Russian drones are flying over Poland and what are you doing? You're having another discussion about helping migrants. And you're laughing. You're not going to laugh for long!

That is the problem: you do not understand your position. So stop attacking us, because we are the first in Europe to save Europe, to restore Europe. We are the brave ones!

And you have laughter only because you are safe here in this building, in this Parliament. Over a million people will take to the streets of London this Saturday and you will see the unity. You will see the strength of the Patriots. Be brave. Be like Poland.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I would like to remind the speaker that he has to respect the European values here, and we will discuss another day about what it is to be a man, here in the European Parliament.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Svenja Hahn (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, sometimes you think: how do you counter this misogyny? And then you think: maybe just continue and show them how strong women are in this House.

Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Kollegen! Der Deal mit den USA ist ein schlechter Deal. Frau von der Leyen hat sich nicht getraut, h脙陇rter zu verhandeln, weil wir wirtschaftspolitisch und sicherheitspolitisch abh脙陇ngig sind von den USA. Europa hat die Wahl: kein Deal oder ein schlechter Deal.

Vor allem muss Europa sich zusammenrei脙聼en, um endlich vom Katzentisch an die Verhandlungstische der Welt zu kommen. Geopolitische Relevanz k脙露nnen wir uns nicht herbeireden, die m脙录ssen wir in Handelsabkommen und mit Wirtschaftskraft erk脙陇mpfen. Frau von der Leyen m脙录sste alle Weichen auf Wirtschaftswachstum stellen. Stattdessen hat sie heute in der Rede zur Lage der Union die Marktwirtschaft begraben. Politische Leitm脙陇rkte,脗聽Buy European, Subventionen sollen kommen, aber zur St脙陇rkung des Binnenmarkts und zu B脙录rokratieabbau gab es leider nur hohle Phrasen statt Taten.

Wer unsere Wirtschaft schw脙陇cht, schw脙陇cht Europa. Ohne Wachstum werden wir keine Weltmacht, wir bleiben Spielball. Wer das Mercosur-Abkommen blockiert, blockiert Wachstum und vernichtet Arbeitspl脙陇tze. Stark in der Welt werden wir nur mit mehr Handel und mehr Partnern.

Nutzen wir den Moment! Ministerin Bjerre, ich baue auf Sie und die d脙陇nische Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft. Ratifizieren Sie so viele Handelsabkommen wie m脙露glich!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vicent Marz脙聽 Ib脙隆脙卤ez (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, traen un mal acuerdo, y no se lo tenemos que decir aquellos que estamos desde el inicio en contra, tambi脙漏n lo est脙隆n diciendo aquellos que intentan salir a defender su acuerdo.

De hecho, deber脙颅an reflexionar sobre si los 脙潞nicos que est脙隆n contentos en este Parlamento con este acuerdo 芒聙聰y lo defienden con mucha intensidad芒聙聰 son los que est脙隆n al servicio de Trump. Lo acabamos de ver. Los 脙潞nicos contentos son los socios de Trump en Europa y, por lo tanto, deber脙颅an hacer una reflexi脙鲁n, no solo porque es humillante y porque es desigual, sino tambi脙漏n porque no genera absolutamente ninguna estabilidad.

Los 脙潞nicos que est脙隆n defendiendo este acuerdo dicen que va a generar m脙隆s estabilidad. 14 d脙颅as despu脙漏s, ya ten脙颅amos una subida de aranceles en el aluminio. Un mes despu脙漏s, ya tenemos al se脙卤or脗聽Trump diciendo que nos va a subir otra vez los aranceles, porque empresas norteamericanas que no cumplen la legislaci脙鲁n que nos hemos dado soberanamente los europeos han sido multadas. 脗驴Qu脙漏 estabilidad est脙隆 dando este acuerdo, si tan solo un mes despu脙漏s ya se dice que se va a romper?

Por lo tanto, necesitamos avanzar en el cumplimiento de la normativa internacional de la Organizaci脙鲁n Mundial del Comercio y hacerlo como referentes, porque es nuestro momento. Es el momento de Europa, si seguimos anclados en nuestros valores y en aquellos que compartimos con el conjunto del mundo y dejamos de hacer negocios con aquellos que nos chantajean y no los cumplen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pernando Barrena Arza (The Left). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora Von der Leyen, las ciudadanas y los ciudadanos europeos seguimos avergonzados por las im脙隆genes y el contenido de su reuni脙鲁n con Donald Trump el pasado 27脗聽de julio.

Usted ha aceptado aranceles del 15脗聽% sobre los productos europeos y se ha comprometido a compras e inversiones millonarias en favor de los Estados Unidos.

La imagen que usted dej脙鲁 para la posteridad es la de una gobernadora de una colonia rindiendo pleites脙颅a al todopoderoso presidente de la metr脙鲁poli. Su encuentro con el presidente de los Estados Unidos es la imagen gr脙隆fica del fin de la autonom脙颅a estrat脙漏gica europea y pone en grave riesgo de inviabilidad el proceso de integraci脙鲁n europeo.

Su apuesta es la supeditaci脙鲁n econ脙鲁mica y militar a los Estados Unidos, recortes sociales para poder seguir comprando armamento. 脗驴A qui脙漏n? A esos Estados Unidos. Y, en consecuencia, convertir a Europa en c脙鲁mplice necesaria del genocidio contra Palestina. B脙隆sicamente, porque Europa ni est脙隆 ni se la espera y porque su capacidad de influencia pol脙颅tica como actor global es tendente a cero.

Est脙隆 usted resultando ser una presidenta de la Comisi脙鲁n abiertamente antieuropea. Probablemente, la pr脙鲁xima vez tengamos que interpelar a Donald Trump, quien, tras su encuentro, ya se jacta de ser el presidente de Europa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fernand Kartheiser (NI). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, ce sujet serait mieux d脙漏nomm脙漏 脗芦le diktat tarifaire am脙漏ricain脗禄, alors que la Commission n'a m脙陋me pas n脙漏goci脙漏 pour d脙漏fendre les int脙漏r脙陋ts commerciaux europ脙漏ens. Obnubil脙漏e par sa volont脙漏 de prolonger encore la guerre en Ukraine avec l'aide am脙漏ricaine, elle a c脙漏d脙漏 脙聽 toutes les exigences, et ce dans un domaine cl脙漏 de sa comp脙漏tence. Pourquoi avons-nous alors besoin d'une Commission europ脙漏enne?

Il faut mettre un terme 脙聽 la politisation des tarifs douaniers pour en refaire de simples instruments de politique commerciale. Toute tentative d'imposer des sanctions politiques par le biais des tarifs douaniers doit 脙陋tre 脙漏cart脙漏e. De m脙陋me, la Commission ne doit pas essayer d'inclure la censure des m脙漏dias sociaux 脙聽 effet extraterritorial dans des accords commerciaux conclus avec des tiers. Au contraire, la censure des services et des march脙漏s digitaux doit 脙陋tre abolie.

La Commission a fait le contraire du raisonnable. Elle aurait d脙禄 rester ferme sur les tarifs douaniers et acc脙漏der aux arguments am脙漏ricains pour garantir la libert脙漏 d'expression.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Szczerba (PPE). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Panie Komisarzu! Porozumienie handlowe z USA jest trudne, nie jest idealne, ale w obecnych warunkach jest najlepsze z mo脜录liwych. Ale dzisiejszy dzie脜聞 pokazuje, 脜录e fundamentem relacji transatlantyckich jest bezpiecze脜聞stwo.

Polska przestrze脜聞 powietrzna zosta脜聜a 19 razy naruszona i nie by脜聜o to zdarzenie przypadkowe. Odpowiada za nie kraj terrorystyczny, jakim jest Rosja Putina. To bezprecedensowy atak na Polsk脛聶, na terytorium Unii Europejskiej i NATO. Si脜聜y NATO w Polsce zosta脜聜y uruchomione.

W 32. bazie lotnictwa w 脜聛asku stacjonuje jedna z jednostek wojsk ameryka脜聞skich w Polsce. Dron spad脜聜 60 kilometr脙鲁w od tej bazy. To pokazuje, w jakim momencie si脛聶 znajdujemy. Dzisiaj licz脛聟 si脛聶 mocne sojusze. Potrzebne jest wzmocnienie sojuszniczej obrony przeciwlotniczej i potrzebujemy jako Polska, jako kraj graniczny, r脙鲁wnie脜录 system脙鲁w Patriot. Prosimy o solidarno脜聸脛聡.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lina G脙隆lvez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, hoy la presidenta de la Comisi脙鲁n Europea ha dicho que la Uni脙鲁n Europea tiene que seguir siendo libre e independiente, pero este acuerdo comercial con los Estados Unidos no parece facilitarlo.

Los socialistas somos muy conscientes de la importancia de evitar una guerra comercial y trabajaremos para mejorar, sin duda, este acuerdo, pero no estamos seguros de que aceptar condiciones asim脙漏tricas lo evite, como tampoco garantiza estabilidad o seguridad, como se demostr脙鲁 solamente unos d脙颅as despu脙漏s, cuando se subieron los aranceles a 407 art脙颅culos europeos.

Adem脙隆s, este acuerdo puede traer una contracci脙鲁n econ脙鲁mica que puede provocar una ola pol脙颅tica reaccionaria, desestabilizando a las democracias liberales de Europa y a la propia Uni脙鲁n Europea, que, tal vez, es lo que quiere el trumpismo.

Por eso, su aplicaci脙鲁n no puede debilitar la legislaci脙鲁n europea clave para nuestra autonom脙颅a. La Uni脙鲁n Europea tiene que preservar la unidad mientras se defiende ante coacciones desmedidas y protege sectores clave como la seguridad, la industria, la energ脙颅a, la tecnolog脙颅a o la agricultura.

Tenemos los recursos para hacerlo, tenemos peso econ脙鲁mico, influencia regulatoria y herramientas comerciales por usar: hag脙隆moslo y sigamos defendiendo el sistema multilateral basado en reglas, nuestra soberan脙颅a, nuestros empleos y fortaleciendo los acuerdos comerciales con nuestros socios.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gerolf Annemans (PfE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, er kan geen enkele twijfel over bestaan: buiten de beschermde bubbel van dit Europees Parlement en buiten de elitaire club van mensen die belang hebben bij haar politieke voortbestaan, zou Von der Leyen al lang verjaagd zijn. Haar Commissie is het grote conglomeraat van het eigenbelang en de脗聽trade deal met Trump is ongetwijfeld een kers op de taart van onbekwaamheid die door haar gebakken is de afgelopen vijf脗聽jaar.

Het uit handen geven van ons internationaal handelsbeleid aan een multilaterale instelling als de EU heeft ons geen voordelen opgeleverd. Het houdt geen rekening met de verschillende belangen. De stabiliteit die als reden wordt opgegeven voor de ongehoorde toegevingen, is een drogreden. Trump heeft, zoals het hoort, aan de eigenbelangen van zijn land gedacht. Von der Leyen niet. Daar zit het probleem, Europa芒聙聶s probleem.
