脗聽脗聽Dick Erixon (ECR). 芒聙聯 Herr talman! Vi m脙楼ste komma ih脙楼g att vid 脙楼rets b脙露rjan var det EU som hade h脙露gre tullar mot USA p脙楼 viktiga omr脙楼den som bilar och jordbruksprodukter. Detta har f脙楼tt den nya amerikanska administrationen att reagera med sin egen protektionism.

I det h脙陇r l脙陇get s脙楼 inneb脙陇r detta f脙露r mig att det blir n脙露dv脙陇ndigt att ber脙露mma kommissionen f脙露r att man kommit 脙露verens med USA om ett avtal, 脙陇ven om jag personligen hellre hade sett noll 芒聙聯 zero 芒聙聯 tullar 脙楼t b脙楼da h脙楼llen. Kommissionen sj脙露nk inte ner i barnsliga attacker p脙楼 USA med krav p脙楼 h脙陇mndtullar p脙楼 whisky, jeans och Harley Davidson.

V脙楼ra handelsf脙露rbindelser 脙陇r alldeles f脙露r viktiga f脙露r prestigefullt poserande. L脙楼t oss forts脙陇tta jobba f脙露r s脙陇nkta tullar med en ansvarsfull realism.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bart Groothuis (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, Council, as markets outside Europe are increasingly closing for our entrepreneurs, I would like to ask the Commission to open up new markets for our businesses, to finish new trade deals fast. But trade negotiations with the Gulf region have dragged on for 34脗聽years; India, 18脗聽years; Latin America, 26脗聽years and counting; Malaysia, 15脗聽years, etc. It's too slow for this geopolitical storm.

The problem is that by overloading trade agreements with additional demands on human rights, environmental or political conditions, we are stopping crucial trade deals from ever reaching the finish line. As an extra result, ratification is too slow. And let's just stop the translation of each concept trade agreement into 24 different languages. It's used as an excuse for delay.

Commissioner, your excellent team in Brussels should become a lean, mean fighting machine. We need to reform faster than markets around us are closing. Reform starts here at home and you have our support in doing so.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mark脙漏ta Gregorov脙隆 (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, colleagues, the Commission promised to President Trump more than EUR 1 trillion in purchases and investments into the US without any binding nature, any way to measure them, and without even distinguishing them from plans already in place.

What happened on 27 July was presented to the public as necessary damage control and to me, as an MEP, as a hidden win for European industry because we supposedly avoided 30 % tariffs.

In truth, it was little more than performative politics. It was a petty shakedown by a hostile American administration, and the costs should haunt the US for years. We can play the same game. We can fairly tax US digital monopolies and support our own digital champions. We can stop relying on US weapons and build our own military capacity. When the US undermines its global trade and monetary role we should be ready to step in when the dollar falters.

Otherwise, what are we really expecting from giving the bully our lunch every day?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jonas Sj脙露stedt (The Left). 芒聙聯 Herr talman! Det h脙陇r avtalet 脙陇r ett svek. Det 脙陇r ett svek mot Europas arbetare. Det 脙陇r ett svek mot Europas f脙露retag. USA f脙楼r alla f脙露rdelar. Europa f脙楼r alla nackdelar. Det 脙陇r en kapitulation.

Men det 脙陇r ocks脙楼 ett svek mot det globala klimatarbetet. EU ska k脙露pa br脙陇nslen fr脙楼n USA till ett v脙陇rde av 750脗聽miljarder dollar. Det kommer att handla om olja och gas. Mycket av det tas fram med fracking. Det 脙陇r en av de mest milj脙露farliga metoder som finns f脙露r att utvinna fossila br脙陇nslen med stora lokala f脙露roreningar men ocks脙楼 extra utsl脙陇pp av v脙陇xthusgaser som metan.

Det h脙陇r 脙陇r en enorm 脙露kning av importen av smutsig fossil energi, en sorts bel脙露ning till Donald Trump som har l脙陇mnat Parisavtalet. Det ska inte vi g脙露ra. Vi ska st脙陇rka det globala milj脙露samarbetet, och vi ska s脙陇ga nej till den h脙陇r typen av smutsiga avtal.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thomas Geisel (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Es wurde schon mehrfach darauf hingewiesen, dass der Zoll-Deal von Ursula von der Leyen und Donald Trump unausgewogen ist und europ脙陇ische Interessen verletzt. Gravierender aber finde ich, dass die Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin dabei Versprechungen gemacht hat, die nicht nur inhaltlich absurd sind, sondern die sie auch gar nicht einhalten kann.

Wenn sie dem amerikanischen Pr脙陇sidenten verspricht, die Europ脙陇ische Union werde in den n脙陇chsten drei Jahren in den USA 脙聳l und Gas im Wert von 750脗聽Milliarden脗聽Dollar kaufen, dann w脙录rde das bedeuten, dass wir uns beim Gas vollst脙陇ndig von teurem amerikanischem Fracking-LNG abh脙陇ngig machen. Aber versprechen konnte sie das ohnehin nicht, denn es wird ja nicht aus dem Budget der Europ脙陇ischen Union bezahlt. Und dass europ脙陇ische Energieunternehmen so bescheuert sind, nur noch in Amerika Erdgas zu kaufen, kann ich mir kaum vorstellen. Insofern ist der Vertragsbruch vorprogrammiert, den Donald Trump zum Anlass nehmen wird, uns erneut zu erpressen.

Frau von der Leyen sollte ihren Hut nehmen; sie ist einfach nicht fit f脙录r den Job.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Borja Gim脙漏nez Larraz (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, la fuerza de Europa ante este desaf脙颅o reside en la uni脙鲁n comercial, y hoy est脙隆 llamada a tomar decisiones. El pacto con los Estados Unidos ha evitado una guerra comercial que no beneficiar脙颅a a nadie, pero no podemos ser complacientes: el acuerdo suscita dudas, y esto no podemos ignorarlo. Estados Unidos es un aliado primordial, pero ahora es impredecible, lo que nos pone en riesgo.

Tenemos que abrir nuevos mercados: ya no es una opci脙鲁n, es una obligaci脙鲁n, una necesidad estrat脙漏gica. Y hoy, entre todas las regiones, hay una que nos interpela de manera singular:脗聽Iberoam脙漏rica, por la historia, por los lazos culturales y ling脙录脙颅sticos, por la convergencia de intereses que nos proyecta hacia el futuro...

Los acuerdos de M脙漏xico y Mercosur deben ser una realidad lo antes posible. Frente al repliegue nacional y al proteccionismo, la Uni脙鲁n Europea debe abanderar la defensa del libre comercio; por nuestros principios, s脙颅, pero fundamentalmente por la prosperidad de nuestras empresas y del conjunto de los europeos.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rapha脙芦l Glucksmann (S&D). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, soyons directs: vous avez accept脙漏 cet accord profond脙漏ment injuste contre une promesse illusoire de stabilit脙漏 et de s脙漏curit脙漏 qui sera malheureusement battue en br脙篓che tr脙篓s rapidement. La pr脙漏sidente de la Commission脗聽est all脙漏e en 脙聣cosse comme on va 脙聽 Canossa et Trump a pris un malin plaisir 脙聽 l'humilier, et nous 脙聽 travers elle, entre deux parties de golf.

Alors, n'oublions jamais: nous sommes le premier march脙漏 du monde. Nous avons les moyens d'engager un rapport de force avec les 脙聣tats-Unis. Et si personne n'a int脙漏r脙陋t 脙聽 une guerre commerciale, en refusant d'engager ce rapport de force, nous avons pr脙漏par脙漏 les futurs chantages de Trump et nous avons incit脙漏 les divers pr脙漏dateurs de ce monde 脙聽 m脙漏priser nos principes comme nos int脙漏r脙陋ts.

R脙漏veillons-nous! Trump ne nous aidera pas. Ni sur l'Ukraine, ni sur rien. Il nous marchera dessus si nous le laissons faire. Alors la seule fa脙搂on de se faire respecter et de construire enfin notre puissance est de croire en notre propre force. Quand on cherche 脙聽 amadouer un adversaire, g脙漏n脙漏ralement il prend 脙搂a pour de la faiblesse et Trump ne tol脙篓re aucune faiblesse.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Benoit Cassart (Renew). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, l'Union europ脙漏enne s'est agenouill脙漏e face 脙聽 Donald脗聽Trump, c'est incontestable, mais il ne sert 脙聽 rien de pleurer sur le lait renvers脙漏. Demandons-nous plut脙麓t pourquoi. Et, surtout, retenons la le脙搂on.

Comment n脙漏gocier d'脙漏gal 脙聽 脙漏gal avec Washington ou P脙漏kin quand nous avons besoin d'eux pour prot脙漏ger nos fronti脙篓res, chauffer nos foyers ou faire tourner nos usines? Alors que nos d脙漏pendances strat脙漏giques nous transforment en oiseau pour le chat, vous continuez en signant l'accord avec le Mercosur sans clause miroir, mena脙搂ant ainsi notre souverainet脙漏 alimentaire. Or, la nourriture est une arme g脙漏opolitique. L'Ukraine nous l'a montr脙漏, Gaza nous le rappelle tous les jours cruellement. Continuer 脙聽 signer des accords de libre-脙漏change d脙漏s脙漏quilibr脙漏s sur le dos de notre agriculture est une faute politique grave et irr脙漏versible.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Vieira (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear Commissioner, you and our colleagues from the EPP called for political responsibility in supporting this deal. But I think it's very far-fetched to claim that it is responsible for us to spend USD脗聽750脗聽billion on natural gas, oil and nuclear energy from the US.

If Russia has taught us something, it is that Europe must become independent in its energy production through a green transition by no longer depending on others 芒聙聯 and especially not on unpredictable populists and dictators.

But looking at this deal with the US, it seems perhaps we have not learned our lesson. Let's face it, Commissioner S脤聦efc脤聦ovic脤聦, these billions are needed here in Europe, to benefit European consumers and companies, not to fill the coffers of ExxonMobil and Chevron 芒聙聯 the same corporations that have blocked ambitious climate action for decades.

Instead of pouring money into fossil fuels, we must invest in renewable energy production, in batteries, in the circular economy. Our regulation, our climate goals, our values are not for sale. And we cannot 芒聙聯 ever 芒聙聯 trade them away.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽C脙漏line Imart (PPE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, le 27脗聽juillet dernier, la pr脙漏sidente de la Commission europ脙漏enne a, au d脙漏tour d'un parcours de golf, ent脙漏rin脙漏 un accord compl脙篓tement d脙漏s脙漏quilibr脙漏: acc脙篓s libre et sans taxes pour les importations am脙漏ricaines contre 15脗聽% pour nos exportations europ脙漏ennes.

Je mesure la complexit脙漏 du sujet. Je connais le caract脙篓re impr脙漏visible du partenaire am脙漏ricain. Je suis consciente que nos entreprises et nos secteurs ont besoin de clart脙漏 et de stabilit脙漏, mais l'Europe a 脙漏t脙漏 pi脙漏tin脙漏e, le Parlement europ脙漏en 脙漏cart脙漏, l'accord conclu dans l'opacit脙漏. Ce n'est pas un compromis, c'est une capitulation.

Ajoutons 脙聽 cela la promesse de 1脗聽200脗聽milliards d'euros d'investissements europ脙漏ens sur le sol am脙漏ricain: 脙漏nergie, secteur strat脙漏gique, plus intelligence artificielle, sans oublier les promesses d'achats militaires. Nous finan脙搂ons avec l'argent de nos contribuables la r脙漏industrialisation des 脙聣tats-Unis et, ce faisant, sacrifions notre souverainet脙漏.

Monsieur le Commissaire, poursuivez ces n脙漏gociations, d脙漏fendez la dignit脙漏 de nos 脙聣tats et les int脙漏r脙陋ts de nos fili脙篓res et, si la ren脙漏gociation s'av脙篓re impossible, servez-vous des outils de d脙漏fense commerciale comme l'outil anti-coercition que ce Parlement vous a donn脙漏. Car si l'Europe est un g脙漏ant 脙漏conomique, cette n脙漏gociation d脙漏sastreuse nous rappelle que nous restons un nain politique.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tonino Picula (S&D). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, ovo je, na脜戮alost, za Europu bilo ljeto neiskori脜隆tenih prilika kada je rije脛聧 o odnosu prema Washingtonu.

U Ameriku izvozimo vi脜隆e robe, ali uvozimo vi脜隆e usluga na koje se ne pla脛聡a porez. Trgovinski odnos s Amerikom je bio balansiran i prije ovog sporazuma u kojemu nema ni pravnih ni politi脛聧kih garancija. Trump dosljedno name脛聡e svoj program selektivnog pove脛聡avanja poreza, op脛聡e deregulacije i nametanja carina.

Kao Europska unija, blokirani internim neslaganjima, poku脜隆avamo posti脛聡i najmanje lo脜隆 sporazum. Slijedimo, ne vodimo, a posljedice propu脜隆tenog djelovanja mogu biti dugoro脛聧ne.

Preuzimanjem odgovornosti za vlastitu sigurnost i obranu, ulaganjem u proizvodnju vlastite energije itekako bi oja脛聧ali globalnu pregovara脛聧ku poziciju.

Prioritet mora biti za脜隆tita radnih mjesta i konkurentnost kroz investicije, ali u Europi, ja脛聧anje europske autonomije i obrana vladavine prava, pogotovo na脜隆eg europskog zakonodavstva.

Europska unija je globalni simbol demokratskih vrijednosti i ekolo脜隆kih standarda. Europska unija je najve脛聡e jedinstveno tr脜戮i脜隆te na svijetu.

Zato iskoristimo svoja postignu脛聡a i hrabrije ispravljajmo svoje nedostatke. Zajedno.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mika Aaltola (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear Commissioner, at first glance, the new US-EU trade deal seems to be absurd. But it buys us something urgently needed 芒聙聭 predictability. Without it, Europe faced tariffs up to 30 % and loss of millions of jobs. Now, with average rate below 15 %, we bought time. Not comfort, but breathing space and transatlantic economic stability that has also important political ramifications.

Yet let us be clear. The deal is a fragile one. President Trump's unpredictability makes it temporary at best. This means acting now. Times are difficult. We must turn today's instability in tomorrow's power. It is about Europe's place in a world where power, not rules, unfortunately, increasingly sets the terms.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Victor Negrescu (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear Commissioner, the EU needs the friendship and democratic influence of the US as much as the United States needs a powerful and united Europe.

The recent summit of authoritarian leaders has shown us that we have countries seeking to change the current global status quo, to threaten our way of life and to challenge our democratic model.

Divided, we are clearly weaker. But united we can protect our citizens. And in order to do so, we need to be fair partners and respect our democratic rules. I support the need for a deal and the work done by the EU Commissioner. I can even accept the request for Europe to invest more in defence, but I have difficulties in accepting some of the imbalances in the agreement, in particular related to agriculture and our industry, but also the lack of consultation of Parliament and of the business sector.

This is Europe. We are not perfect, but we love our democratic imperfections and stand strong for our values. It is normal for the Parliament to scrutinise the agreement, but we stand strong and firm in supporting anything that can help us improve the text without jeopardising the deal.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Catch-the-eye procedure

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nina Carberry (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, this week, the Irish Fiscal Advisory Council warned that our drinks and spirits sector is uniquely exposed to tariffs.

Boann Distillery, in the Boyne Valley, only entered the US market in February of this year. The Shed Distillery in Leitrim is another example of an Irish SME that has rooted itself in the US market. For them, the long-term threats are the hardest to measure. With the future of Trump's tariffs now before the US Supreme Court, we must be ready to re-engage.

Commissioner, this threat also means new EU supports must be made available for exporters 芒聙聯 supports that open new markets, help SMEs diversify and help funding in the next EU budget. We must also recognise the knock-on effect tariffs in the whisky sector have on the agri-food supply chain, in tillage especially.

And finally, as Parliament's rapporteur on the UK relations, a word of caution: tariff divergence on the island of Ireland risks complicating the Windsor Framework. We cannot allow that to happen.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Grapini (S&D). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, domnule comisar, stima脠聸i colegi, rezultatul unei negocieri, fie acord politic, fie comercial, depinde de puterea de negociere. Sigur, 脠聶i de curaj, 脠聶i de competen脠聸脛聝. Haide脠聸i s脛聝 fim reali脠聶ti, asta a fost puterea Uniunii Europene. A sc脛聝zut an de an 脠聶i atunci nu putem s脛聝 spunem c脛聝 e vinovat cel care are putere mai mare de negociere.

Avem nevoie de acordul cu Statele Unite. Nu putem s脛聝 ignor脛聝m o pia脠聸脛聝 at脙垄t de mare. Industria noastr脛聝 are nevoie, agricultura noastr脛聝 are nevoie. Sigur c脛聝 nu suntem mul脠聸umi脠聸i, poate niciunul de aici, dar totdeauna trebuie s脛聝 ne g脙垄ndim la varianta cealalt脛聝. Ce ar fi fost sau ce este, dac脛聝 nu vom avea un acord cu Statele Unite? S脛聝 fie foarte clar, dac脛聝 nu a脠聸i negociat niciun contract, poate v脛聝 este greu s脛聝 脙庐n脠聸elege脠聸i. Niciodat脛聝 unul mai mic 脙庐n puterea de negociere nu va c脙垄脠聶tiga 脙庐n fa脠聸a celuilalt. Da, dar avem p脙垄rghii prin care putem s脛聝-l determin脛聝m pe Trump s脛聝 cedeze 脠聶i s脛聝 fie un acord pe principiul c脙垄脠聶tig-c脙垄脠聶tig.

Am f脛聝cut un prim pas, trebuie continuat 脠聶i eu am 脙庐ncredere, domnule comisar, c脛聝 vom putea 脙庐n timp s脛聝 corect脛聝m acest acord. Avem nevoie de el 脠聶i trebuie s脛聝 vedem cum 脙庐l putem 脙庐mbun脛聝t脛聝脠聸i. Trebuie fermierii no脠聶tri, agricultura noastr脛聝, produc脛聝torii de vin, industria s脛聝 poat脛聝 s脛聝 aib脛聝 acces la acea pia脠聸脛聝 脙庐n condi脠聸ii, a脠聶a cum v-am mai spus, similare, acelea脠聶i standarde, acela脠聶i acces reciproc pe pia脠聸脛聝. Asta 脙庐nseamn脛聝 un acord etic.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Francisco Jos脙漏 Mill脙隆n Mon (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, el reciente acuerdo comercial alcanzado entre la Uni脙鲁n Europea y los Estados Unidos 芒聙聰aunque imperfecto芒聙聰 representa un avance y resulta preferible a la ausencia de un marco com脙潞n o a una guerra comercial. Pensemos en los aranceles del 50脗聽% de la India o del 39脗聽% de Suiza.

No es f脙隆cil negociar con la Administraci脙鲁n Trump, pero debemos tener tambi脙漏n presente la dimensi脙鲁n de seguridad y defensa que nos une, de forma imprescindible, con los Estados Unidos en estos momentos tan dif脙颅ciles.

Es innegable que el acuerdo acordado est脙隆 lejos de nuestras expectativas. Es, adem脙隆s, muy limitado, pero creo que puede aportar alguna certidumbre a nuestras empresas frente a la volatilidad.

La Comisi脙鲁n debe seguir negociando para conseguir un trato arancelario m脙隆s favorable para otros sectores muy afectados como, por ejemplo, el vino y el aceite de oliva. En estas negociaciones pendientes, 脗驴de qu脙漏 plazos hablamos, se脙卤or comisario?

No olvidemos, adem脙隆s, que nuestras exportaciones ahora se ven tambi脙漏n muy perjudicadas por la actual devaluaci脙鲁n del d脙鲁lar. Lamentablemente, tenemos unos aranceles elevad脙颅simos a nuestro acero y a nuestro aluminio: el doble, por ejemplo, de los del Reino Unido. 脗驴Qu脙漏 est脙隆 pasando? 脗驴A qu脙漏 obedece esto? 脗驴Qu脙漏 pasa con los productos que tienen estos componentes?

En paralelo, necesitamos diversificar nuestras relaciones econ脙鲁micas y comerciales 芒聙聰pienso en el Mercosur y en M脙漏xico芒聙聰 y tambi脙漏n reforzar el mercado interior.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Mr Vice-President, this is our situation that we have to deal with. This House has said it already, no one is happy. And I don't think that we can change anything with that now. But we have to learn something for the future, while we now have to stand together implementing this.

I think the very first lesson is how to deal with bullies 芒聙聭 not to give in to them. And if I may suggest this for the next time that we find ourselves in a situation like this, Mr Commissioner, we should impose countermeasures already while negotiating this deal.

And the second lesson that we take is that we are not seen as weak in that sense. The European Commission should make more effort to actually communicate the efforts that have been made with the public to convince them that this was not just the best that we could get, but actually something good for Europe. Thank you very much for your work and thank you very much for your cooperation.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomislav Sokol (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, ovaj takozvani sporazum je generalno jednostran, neuravnote脜戮en i 脛聧ini europsku industriju manje konkurentnom u odnosu na SAD.

Postoje pozitivni elementi poput oslobo脛聭enja za generi脛聧ke lijekove, ali to su, na脜戮alost, iznimke.

Europska unija je tr脜戮i脜隆te s vi脜隆e od 450 milijuna potro脜隆a脛聧a. Imamo suficit sa SAD-om u trgovini robom, a deficit u trgovini uslugama, 脜隆to zna脛聧i da imamo jake alate da odgovorimo na bilo kakve ucjene iz Washingtona.

Na脜戮alost, ni脜隆ta od toga nismo napravili, ve脛聡 smo popustili kako bismo dobili sigurnosne garancije koje su vrlo upitne pouzdanosti.

脜聽to je najgore, nismo dobili ni izvjesnost, budu脛聡i da Amerikanci ve脛聡 vr脜隆e pritiske kako bi dobili povla脜隆teni status za svoje big tech kompanije, 脜隆to je, na脜戮alost, bilo i za o脛聧ekivati.

Uvo脛聭enje carina od strane SAD-a predstavlja poraz, ali EU ima snagu i mogu脛聡nost razvijati nova partnerstva, bilo s Japanom, Australijom, Kanadom ili dr脜戮avama Latinske Amerike.

Diverzifikacija trgovinskih odnosa klju脛聧 je za ubla脜戮avanje negativnih u脛聧inaka i za脜隆titu na脜隆ih interesa. Budu脛聡nost trgovinskih odnosa Europske unije le脜戮i u kombinaciji trgovinskih mjera zasnovanih na reciprocitetu, diverzifikaciji partnerstava i ja脛聧anju europske industrije. Samo tako 脛聡emo osigurati jaku Europu i prosperitet za europske gra脛聭ane.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maro脜隆 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, I was listening to you very, very carefully this morning and also of course, now, this afternoon. I was just trying to imagine what our debate would look like if it would take place in a full trade war. I am sure I would not get any praise from you for hundreds of jobs lost, billions of euros of investment disappeared and thousands of SMEs gone bust.

I know that many of you would criticise me for failed negotiations and economic catastrophe brought to Europe. Therefore, allow me a personal remark; I am always surprised how, in Europe, we always manage to talk ourselves and Europe down. We got the best deal in the world with the US, which was extremely difficult, and we got this harsh treatment. Ask the countries who got the agreement with the US and ask those who didn't if they would trade places with us. Show me one who wouldn't 芒聙聯 show me one who wouldn't! Therefore, if you allow me, I would like to just run through some of the figures which have been quoted so widely this afternoon.

Our estimation after this deal was reached and we compared it to the situation with other countries having or not having the deal 芒聙聯 the fact is that impact on trade, because of our relative stronger position than other partners, would be negligible. I would be very happy 芒聙聯 and I did it already with many of you 芒聙聯 to share these figures. So, please, let's talk figures and not only emotions.

Our agri-food industry is and will remain competitive. We export EUR脗聽30脗聽billion to the United States. We import EUR脗聽11脗聽billion from the United States. Our concessions have been given on non-sensitive products. Again: compare these results with some other deals which have been achieved. On top of this, we export high-value products and we import the products we need like soybeans, animal feed and so on and so forth. So I am absolutely convinced that our agri-food sector will also stay competitive with this deal.

In many meetings I had yesterday with the honourable Members of the Parliament who are interested in trade and in the US, I gave the comparison with the tariffs on cheese. The MFN before the deal was 14.9脗聽%. After the deal, because we have an all-inclusive shield of 15脗聽%, it's 15%. What is it in other countries who do not have this non-stacking rule? It's 49% plus reciprocal tariffs. So in the case of some of our neighbours it's much much higher. I do not want to compare our advantages with other countries because it's not fair and I know that they've been doing their best in negotiations and we are partners and friends with them. But I just want to tell you this reality: please compare the figures.

Then I hear a lot of criticism about industrial products. Again: let's look at the numbers. Already now 67脗聽% of US industrial exports to the EU were tariff free. So what we are talking about here is 33脗聽%. We are talking a nominal value in billions of euros 芒聙聯 billions: much less than ten. And exports to the EU are around EUR脗聽360脗聽billion. Again: look at the figures, compare the facts and please, please, please be fair to us.

The EU has the best tariff compared to everyone else and, therefore, as I said, this is faring very favourably with others. On top of it, we got the exemptions, we got the key sectors capped at 15脗聽% and, most importantly, we secured also some agenda for the future. I totally agree with Mr脗聽Lange and with all of you who are unhappy about the derivatives. I totally agree with Mr脗聽Benifei脗聽that we need to work on steel 芒聙聯 and we will. This is reflected in the joint statement that we will be working on a 'ring of partners' to protect our steel industry. We didn't make it on 27脗聽July, but it's on the agenda and you have it reflected as one of the things which we will be working on together.

Of course I'm in touch with Secretary脗聽Lutnick脗聽on derivatives. Of course we are highlighting which customs codes should be looked at and upon which we should negotiate. The same goes for wine, the same goes for beer, the same goes for spirits. But do you think that we would be better off in a full trade war with the US to get these negotiations going? Do you think that we would have such constructive discussions as we have with them on many geopolitical issues? We've been discussing this morning what happened yesterday in Poland. Just imagine what the alternative would be.

Now, if I may give some concrete suggestions. As I said, and I think this was raised by my dear colleagues Mr Lange and Mr Benifei on the process ahead, the Commission is ready to work with you and support Parliament in the legislative process. I am sure that we can work together on constructive solutions. But I just have one plea: let's be reasonable, let's be responsible. Let's work in a way that will not unravel the deal, which, as I said, is best in the world even though I know many of you do not like it 芒聙聯 and I agree it could be improved 芒聙聯 but let's do it in a way which would help European businesses, European citizens and the European economy.

To my dear colleague, Mr Warborn 芒聙聯 we have been discussing this already together, but I think it has to also be said here, publicly and officially: we believe that the agreement and the joint statement as it was struck is compatible with the WTO, because we look at it as a 'first phase' agreement, and we want to proceed our negotiations to further expand trade. If you look at it from our perspective, the EU already covers substantially all trade and, of course, we would work with the US to further expand.

On top of that, I am in almost daily contact with Doctor Okonjo-Iweala, Director-General of the WTO. She knows, and you know it as well, that we want to reform the WTO, that we need to reform the WTO for the sake of the WTO. For the sake of trade multilateralism we have to reform the WTO, otherwise the future of this organisation would be very, very dire. We are working with her and we are working with our partners to achieve that.

I would like to thank all of you who support the point of diversification of our free-trade agenda, because, if we achieved everything that I described to you in my introductory remarks, this would already replace around three-quarters of current trade with the EU.

Let me also conclude, Mr President, by addressing some of the repeated claims which have also been voiced here and which I categorically reject. The first one is that there were no negotiations. Only a person who was not in the room could say something like that, because there have been hundreds of hours of negotiations from the first moment until the last minute in Scotland, and then after that to make sure that the joint statement was properly drafted, properly formulated, and we discuss every single executive order all the annexes which are being published there with our American partners. So it's continuous work. So if anybody says there was no negotiation: that's simply not true.

Then we've been discussing the anti-coercion instrument and the countermeasures a lot. I know that several of you have been calling for it. Of course we discussed it with Member States! Formally as 27, but especially very confidentially where we've been talking to each Member State one-by-one. And I can tell you there was no appetite for this at all, because the Member States, knowing what kind of situations we are in, prefer agreement, prefer jobs, prefer economic development and prefer as much stability as we can get at this very difficult stage.

To questions on energy and energy security. You would recall that 2 or 3 years ago I was asked to organise the common purchase of gas, because at that time we paid more than EUR脗聽1脗聽000 per megawatt/hour in some parts of Europe. I can tell you it was a pretty difficult job 芒聙聯 I was crisscrossing the world looking for the gas to make sure that our lights and our electricity would be kept on. Now we are completely phasing out Russian supplies; so we need gas, we need oil, we need nuclear fuel, which is new, we need AI chips. What we put on the table was based on a thorough analysis of DG脗聽ENER. So, again, I think that here we have been behaving responsibly. Of course, this would be private contracts but I think we are creating a framework for much stronger energy security than what we would otherwise have.

If you would allow me a last remark and to conclude by referring to Mr脗聽Groothuis, because I totally agree with him: we need to be faster in negotiating and ratifying our free-trade agreements. We have to focus much more on new market access for our businesses and for our economy. Just this year, we brought Mercosur and Mexico to your table, and I hope that very soon we will bring Indonesia. This is on top of making sure that we resolve our issues with Switzerland and that we resolve our issues with our UK friends, and that it works very well.

Now, I believe that we managed to de-escalate with the US, we managed to stabilise the relationship and I think, to be honest, let us be confident enough that we can push our trade agenda with the US further, that we can defend our values, we can defend our laws. I'm pretty sure that we can do it and I believe that to have it in the atmosphere of partnership and trade cooperation is much better than if you do it otherwise.

Mr President, thank you very much for being generous with my time, but we had a long day and this was a topic which was heavily discussed in the morning and in the afternoon. I understand the passion, I understand the interest 芒聙聯 I know that all of us want to do the best for Europe, for European citizens and for our economy. I appreciate all the remarks. I am ready to work, as I was until now, with every single Member of this Chamber, to make sure that we deliver the best possible results for Europe and its citizens. Thank you very much.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members and Commissioner, I have now, as a representative of the Council, followed quite a number of debates in here, and I have noticed strong opinions. But I have to say, this is the first time that I have heard complete consensus from left to centre to right.

And I agree with you: yes, the trade agreement is unbalanced. As both Mr Warborn and Mr Lange, from the S&D, said, it is not perfect. But it is the best result. The alternative would be worse, as Ms Grapini from the S&D just said. We need the agreement.

I also agree with Ms Karlsbro from Renew, who said the best response is to strengthen Europe. We should improve our internal market, make Europe more competitive and capable of defending itself.

Also, there is a point made by Commissioner S脤聦efc脤聦ovic脤聦 that I want to echo: trade with the US is only 17脗聽% of the world's trade; now it is time to embrace the other 83脗聽% of the world's trade, making new partnership trade agreements.

We, in the Council, remain committed to making the best of the understanding reached with the US. We want to continue the work to help our industry improve competitiveness and support economic growth in the EU. The Danish Presidency will support the work on the two proposals in the Council. We hope to reach an outcome that brings benefits to our businesses and to our citizens.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

S脛聮DI VADA: ROBERTS Z脛陋LE
Priek脜隆s脛聯d脛聯t脛聛ja vietnieks

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much, Minister, colleagues.

The debate is closed.

Rakstiski pazi脜聠ojumi (178. pants)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cristina Maestre (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 Teniendo en cuenta todos los elementos conexos, sobre la declaraci脙鲁n conjunta presentada por la UE y los EEUU de Am脙漏rica, varias conclusiones pueden extraerse hasta el momento:

芒聙垄El acuerdo es defectuoso y plantea serias preocupaciones de que sea compatible con la OMC.

芒聙垄No es ni equilibrado ni justo, y no es bueno para Europa. Puede llegar a socavar el empleo, debilitar nuestras industrias, el sector agrario y la econom脙颅a en general y erosionar el sistema comercial multilateral basado en normas.

芒聙垄Podr脙颅a poner en peligro la autonom脙颅a estrat脙漏gica de la UE en sectores clave como la energ脙颅a y la defensa, y aumentar nuestra dependencia de los Estados Unidos.

芒聙垄El acuerdo podr脙颅a tener un impacto y debilitar legislaci脙鲁n clave y nuestra autonom脙颅a y soberan脙颅a en el 脙隆mbito de los impuestos, la legislaci脙鲁n, las normas y los reglamentos digitales.

芒聙垄El acuerdo aumenta la dependencia europea de Estados Unidos para la energ脙颅a, la defensa y el acceso al mercado, en lugar de reforzar nuestra autonom脙颅a.

芒聙垄Adem脙隆s, la Comisi脙鲁n promete concesiones que dif脙颅cilmente van a poder convertirse en instrumentos legales que pueda procesarse por parte del Parlamento Europeo, en materia de concesiones, inversi脙鲁n corporativa europea en los Estados Unidos y compras de gas GNL, petr脙鲁leo y energ脙颅a nuclear.

脗聽

10. China芒聙聶s unjustified decision to impose duties on imports of pork products from the EU and the need to support European farmers and workers (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Priek脜隆s脛聯d脛聯t脛聛js. 芒聙聯 N脛聛kamais darba k脛聛rt脛芦bas punkts ir debates par Komisijas pazi脜聠ojumu par 脛露脛芦nas nepamatoto l脛聯mumu noteikt ievedmuitu c脜芦kga脛录as produkcijas importam no ES un nepiecie脜隆am脛芦bu atbalst脛芦t Eiropas lauksaimniekus un darba 脜聠脛聯m脛聯jus (2025/2869(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andrius Kubilius, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, again, I would like to present the Commission position on this very important issue. It was supposed that the position would be presented by Commissioner Hansen or Commissioner 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧, but they are at an important destination now going to develop other relations. So I will try to present in a very short way the Commission position on this topic.

On 5 September, China's Ministry of Commerce, MOFCOM, announced the imposition of provisional duties on imports of pork and pork by-products originating in the European Union. These measures will apply as of today, 10 September. Announced provisional duties are in the range between 15.6脗聽% and 32.7脗聽% for the three sampled EU companies and their affiliates. For other cooperating companies, duties are set at 20脗聽% and for all other companies at 62.4脗聽%.

The Commission takes note with great concern of MOFCOM's announcement, as according to our assessments, the investigation itself is based on highly questionable allegations and insufficient evidence. We are in close cooperation with the EU pork producers concerned.

I recall that China is a key market for EU pork exports. It's a primary destination, accounting in 2024 for 1.1 million tonnes of EU pigmeat worth EUR脗聽2.2 billion. Its importance is twofold. China imports meat, but its main importance lies in the country's import of offal that would otherwise not find a market that would offer the same high prices as China.

It should be noted that EU exports to China have decreased by more than 50脗聽% in 2024 compared to 2023, and we hold a continuously shrinking market share of less than 2脗聽%. A drop in exports to China has disturbing effects not only for exporting Member States, but indeed for the whole Union, due to a potential significant oversupply of the EU domestic market, especially for offal and correlated economic losses in agriculture and related sectors.

The Commission has engaged with MOFCOM in the course of the investigation, meeting all deadlines for submitting comments and pointing out the flaws of this investigation. Among other actions, on 3 September 2024, the Commission filed a submission highlighting the fatal shortcomings of the investigation and urging MOFCOM to terminate this unwarranted investigation.

Together with the sector, the Commission will further examine the facts underlying the imposition of these provisional duties and will carefully assess all the information available against compliance with WTO rules. The Commission will also consider all options to protect EU industry and economic interests.

To conclude, the European Union has been doing everything it takes to defend European Union farmers and exporters when confronted with the abusive use of trade defence instruments. The Commission had already taken action at the WTO against Chinese initiation of the earlier anti-subsidy investigation on dairy and China's and EU imposition of provisional measures on brandy, and will continue taking all the necessary steps to defend the rights of EU producers.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jessika Van Leeuwen, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, I called for this debate, as I am shocked by the unjust and outrageous decision of China to impose extra import duties on EU pork. It is a clear retaliation for our anti-dumping claim on electrical vehicles. And I remember clearly that when we decided to go ahead with this claim, I warned the Commission that retaliation on EU pork was a risk, and now we are faced with extra import duties varying between 15脗聽% and 62脗聽%.

These tariffs will hit our swine producers and the processing industry very hard. It will affect our export possibilities and reduce the circularity of the sector, as we export cuts that were not, or very rarely, consumed in the EU. And it will lead to unfair competition on the internal market, as the tariffs vary between companies.

Commissioner, the European swine industry is the best in the world. It has made great efforts to reduce antibiotics, has the lowest environmental impact and complies with the best animal welfare standards. Our farmers already faced threats as a result of our policies, reducing CAP support and upcoming trade deals, and now they are used as a bargaining chip in a trade conflict about electric vehicles. The Dutch Farmer芒聙聯Citizen Movement is not willing to let this happen.

We therefore insist, Commissioner, that the Commission challenges these duties at the WTO, launches a diplomatic initiative to resolve this dispute quickly and, if necessary, establishes a crisis fund to stabilise incomes and prevent job losses. Above all, the Commission should make it clear that farmers are not left behind in this geopolitical crossfire. It should send a strong message to show that we stand behind our farmers. I say let's reform Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Van Brempt, namens de S&D-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, wat ik daarnet nog vergeten was te zeggen: als je toelaat dat de internationale rechtsregels opzij worden gezet en toegeeft aan chantage, zoals bij Trump, dan krijg je copycat-gedrag. En dat is wat er nu gebeurt met China. De ene bruut inspireert de andere. China gaat genadeloos de Trump-methode achterna, legt illegale tarieven op en kopieert economische dwang. Dat heeft uiteraard niks te maken met eerlijke concurrentie of op regels gebaseerde handel. Het is puur machtsvertoon. Onze boeren, maar ook onze werknemers in de sector betalen de prijs. Daarom heb ik gevraagd om de titel aan te passen, want het is een breder probleem. Het gaat niet alleen om de boeren, maar om alle werknemers in de hele sector.

Dus moeten er snel acties komen. Wij roepen op om snel en doortastend te werk te gaan met legale tegenmaatregelen, met instrumenten die we zelf in de hand hebben. Bijvoorbeeld door een zaak aan te spannen bij de WHO, ook al geeft dit vandaag de dag niet de garantie dat er ook effectief actie wordt ondernomen. Het is belangrijk dat we steun verlenen aan onze getroffen bedrijven, de boeren en de werknemers.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gilles Pennelle, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, effondrement du budget de la PAC, funeste trait脙漏 du Mercosur, capitulation sur les taxes am脙漏ricaines sur les vins et spiritueux, inflation de normes et de contraintes sur nos agriculteurs et maintenant une mollesse脗聽芒聙聯脗聽on vient de le voir 脙聽 l'instant脗聽芒聙聯 de r脙漏action sur les taxes chinoises sur nos exportations de porc.

Je suis originaire de Bretagne, o脙鹿 je vis, la principale r脙漏gion fran脙搂aise d'脙漏levage porcin, et nos 脙漏leveurs sont excessivement inquiets. Ils sont inquiets parce que le march脙漏 chinois est pour eux indispensable.

Alors que fait la Commission? Rien. Elle abandonne une fois de plus nos agriculteurs et nos 脙漏leveurs. Pourquoi les abandonne-t-elle? Parce que l'agriculture est devenue une variable d'ajustement sur d'autres dossiers. On pr脙漏f脙篓re d脙漏fendre les int脙漏r脙陋ts allemands. On pr脙漏f脙篓re d脙漏fendre les int脙漏r脙陋ts industriels et automobiles allemands, y compris avec la Chine, que de d脙漏fendre notre agriculture. Nos 脙漏leveurs, nos agriculteurs ont compris que la Commission europ脙漏enne les abandonnait. Nous aussi.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Veronika Vrecionov脙隆, za skupinu ECR. 芒聙聯 Pane p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, pane komisa脜聶i, je pot脜聶eba si p脜聶iznat, 脜戮e 脛聦脙颅na proti n脙隆m dlouhodob脛聸 vede r脜炉zn脙漏 formy 脙潞tok脜炉, od pr脜炉myslov脙漏 脜隆pion脙隆脜戮e p脜聶es kyber脙潞toky a脜戮 po st脙隆le 脛聧ast脛聸j脜隆脙颅 ekonomick脙漏 脙潞toky. Sna脜戮脙颅 se zni脛聧it na脜隆e tradi脛聧n脙颅 pr脜炉myslov脙隆 odv脛聸tv脙颅, jako je ocel脙隆脜聶stv脙颅 nebo automobilov脙陆 pr脜炉mysl, nap脜聶脙颅klad skrze dumpingov脙漏 ceny. Nyn脙颅 dal脜隆脙颅m takov脙陆m pokusem je i snaha o podkop脙隆n脙颅 stability na脜隆eho zem脛聸d脛聸lstv脙颅 prost脜聶ednictv脙颅m cel na v脙陆robky z vep脜聶ov脙漏ho masa. Krom脛聸 samotn脙陆ch zem脛聸d脛聸lc脜炉 to m脜炉脜戮e m脙颅t z脙隆va脜戮n脙漏 dopady i na zpracovatelsk脙陆 脜聶et脛聸zec a spot脜聶ebitele. 脛聦脙颅na je n脙隆脜隆 syst脙漏mov脙陆 konkurent a 脛聧asto se chov脙隆 jako n脙隆脜隆 nep脜聶脙颅tel. A tak ji mus脙颅me vn脙颅mat.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie-Pierre Vedrenne, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, le commerce devrait rester un espace de coop脙漏ration et de prosp脙漏rit脙漏 partag脙漏e, non un champ de bataille g脙漏opolitique et de conflits 脙漏conomiques. Mais il faut 脙陋tre clair: ce monde r脙陋v脙漏 est une illusion 脙聽 l'heure du retour des imp脙漏rialismes belliqueux.

P脙漏kin, je le regrette, vient de faire un choix. Un choix dommageable, celui de cibler l'Union europ脙漏enne par des mesures de r脙漏torsion injustes. Une r脙漏action cons脙漏cutive au simple fait que les Europ脙漏ens souhaitent faire respecter les r脙篓gles, des r脙篓gles que la Chine dit elle-m脙陋me accepter.

Apr脙篓s avoir cibl脙漏 le cognac, l'augmentation des droits de douane en repr脙漏sailles sur nos exportations de porc repose sur des fondements fragiles. Une logique de pression politique plut脙麓t que de justice commerciale ne doit pas 脙陋tre la base des discussions et de notre relation. Ce n'est pas seulement une question de commerce. C'est une question de justice, de solidarit脙漏, d'脙漏quit脙漏. Ces mesures auront des cons脙漏quences concr脙篓tes: des milliers d'emplois menac脙漏s, des exploitations mises en p脙漏ril, dans des territoires comme ma r脙漏gion, la Bretagne.

Nous demandons 脙聽 la Chine de reconsid脙漏rer cette d脙漏cision, de dialoguer dans un esprit de coop脙漏ration et de respect mutuel. Les diff脙漏rends commerciaux ne se r脙篓glent pas 脙聽 coups de sanctions cibl脙漏es, mais par la n脙漏gociation, l'脙漏quilibre et la transparence.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Everding, im Namen der Fraktion The Left. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Der Export von Schweinefleisch nach China betrug im Jahr 2024 1,15脗聽Millionen脗聽Tonnen脗聽芒聙聯 Tonnen! Wir reden hier nicht 脙录ber Handelsg脙录ter wie Kohle, Stahl oder Weizen. Wir reden 脙录ber ehemals f脙录hlende Lebewesen. Schweine f脙录hlen wie wir Menschen Schmerz, Leid, Freude und Trauer und empfinden auch Empathie untereinander. Und diese intelligenten Lebewesen verbringen ihr kurzes Leben von meist nur 6 bis 8脗聽Monaten eingepfercht auf Spaltenb脙露den in Kastenst脙陇nden und sind entgegen ihres Instinktes gezwungen, an derselben Stelle zu schlafen, zu essen, zu koten und zu urinieren. Schweine haben unterschiedliche Charaktere. Sie erkennen sich selber im Spiegel. Schweinem脙录tter singen w脙陇hrend der Stillzeit ihren Kindern etwas vor.

Liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, sehen Sie diese Tiere nicht als Handelsware, sondern als das, was sie sind: f脙录hlende Lebewesen. Allein in dieser Minute meiner Redezeit wurden innerhalb der EU 421脗聽Schweine get脙露tet脗聽芒聙聯 die unz脙陇hligen Ferkel, die auf dem Weg zur Schlachtreife verstorben sind, nat脙录rlich nicht mitgez脙陇hlt.

脗聽脗聽Juan Ignacio Zoido 脙聛lvarez (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, la imposici脙鲁n de aranceles chinos a las exportaciones europeas de porcino supone un desaf脙颅o adicional para un sector que ya ven脙颅a afrontando dificultades.

En 2020, el 50脗聽% de las exportaciones espa脙卤olas de porcino ten脙颅an China como destino por un valor de m脙隆s de 3脗聽000 millones de euros. Cuatro a脙卤os m脙隆s tarde 芒聙聰y con presiones comerciales de por medio芒聙聰 esta cuota se ha desplomado hasta el 20脗聽%.

Con estos datos resulta evidente que los nuevos aranceles no responden a criterios t脙漏cnicos, sino que se enmarcan en un clima de instrumentalizaci脙鲁n del comercio.脗聽No es casualidad que se adopten despu脙漏s de que la Uni脙鲁n Europea impusiera aranceles a los coches el脙漏ctricos chinos, una medida que ya ha reducido su cuota de mercado en Europa en un 4脗聽%.

Por eso pido a la Comisi脙鲁n que siga defendiendo a nuestros productores 芒聙聰como ya hizo en los casos de los l脙隆cteos y el brandy芒聙聰 y que, para ello, refuerce el di脙隆logo comercial con China y pacte una soluci脙鲁n que garantice la estabilidad del sector.

脗聽脗聽Stefano Bonaccini (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, dopo i dazi di Trump i nostri agricoltori rischiano di pagare il prezzo di una disputa commerciale con cui nulla hanno a che fare, quella sulle auto elettriche cinesi.

L'indagine antidumping di Pechino sul nostro export di carne suina 脙篓 una rappresaglia che colpirebbe un settore gi脙聽 messo a dura prova dalla peste suina africana e porterebbe al crollo dei prezzi per tutti gli allevatori europei.

Commissario, serve un'azione diplomatica urgente per evitare ulteriori chiusure dei mercati, dimostrando l'infondatezza delle accuse cinesi, e serve un imponente piano di promozione delle nostre eccellenze agroalimentari sui mercati esteri, come chiediamo da mesi e che anche la Presidente von der Leyen ha riconosciuto questa mattina necessario in Aula.

Di fronte a colossi che lavorano per un'Europa pi脙鹿 debole non possiamo isolarci. Al contrario, occorre rafforzare il nostro peso geopolitico con accordi, ad esempio con aree strategiche dal Mercosur all'India; solo cos脙卢 potremo passare da gigante economico a vero gigante politico globale.

脗聽脗聽Csaba D脙露m脙露t脙露r (PfE). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k 脙職r! Fontos, hogy besz脙漏lj脙录nk arr脙鲁l, amikor harmadik orsz脙隆gok, 脙颅gy K脙颅na az eur脙鲁pai gazd脙隆k sz脙隆m脙隆ra k脙隆ros d脙露nt脙漏seket hoznak. Az脙鲁ta m脙隆r tudjuk, hogy t脙露bb mint 20%-kal megv脙隆gn脙隆k az agr脙隆rkassz脙隆t, ami sok sz脙隆zezer csal脙隆di gazdas脙隆got is s脙潞jtana. K脙露zben elkezdik megnyitni a piacokat keletr脜聭l 脙漏s a D脙漏l-Amerik脙隆b脙鲁l 脙漏rkez脜聭 term脙漏kek el脜聭tt. Azt rem脙漏lt脙录k, hogy vagy a m脙潞lt heti bizotts脙隆gi 脙录l脙漏sen, vagy a mostani plen脙隆rison err脜聭l majd lesz vita, de nem lett. Besz脙漏lnek ehelyett K脙颅n脙隆r脙鲁l.

Az脙漏rt nem akarnak vit脙隆t, mert nagyon is j脙鲁l tudj脙隆k az igazs脙隆got. Azt, hogy mindent al脙隆rendelnek a h脙隆bor脙潞s 脙漏s a b脜聭v脙颅t脙漏si kiad脙隆soknak. Ez脙漏rt cs脙露kkenten脙漏k 33 ezer milli脙隆rd forintnak megfelel脜聭 脙露sszeggel az agr脙隆rkassz脙隆t. Ez lehet a legnagyobb csap脙隆sm脙漏r脙漏s a k脙露z脙露s agr脙隆rpolitik脙隆ra, ami脙鲁ta l脙漏trej脙露tt. A Patri脙鲁ta frakci脙鲁 ez脙漏rt bizalmatlans脙隆gi ind脙颅tv脙隆nyt ny脙潞jt be Ursula von der Leyen ellen. Ez is ott van az okok m脙露g脙露tt.

脗聽脗聽Bert-Jan Ruissen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, vorige week kondigde China aan vanaf vandaag invoerheffingen in te voeren op varkensvlees uit de Europese Unie. De heffingen zullen ernstige gevolgen hebben voor de Europese varkensproducenten en de interne markt. Ik maak me met name zorgen over de co脙露peraties die met extra hoge heffingen te maken lijken te krijgen.

Ik wil daarom een dringende oproep doen aan de Europese Commissie om alle mogelijke diplomatieke instrumenten in te zetten en China ervan te overtuigen de invoering van de heffing op zijn minst uit te stellen en tot een oplossing te komen. Daarbij moet ook naar de WHO-afspraken worden gekeken. Benut de mogelijkheden om deze heffingen daar aan te vechten. En als China deze maatregelen niet terugdraait, dan moeten we, wat ons betreft, zeker ook kijken hoe we de verstorende effecten op de interne markt kunnen mitigeren.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Benoit Cassart (Renew). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, pour le conflit entre Airbus脗聽et Boeing, c'脙漏taient d脙漏j脙聽 les vins et les fromages europ脙漏ens qui avaient pay脙漏 l'addition. L'Union europ脙漏enne taxe les voitures 脙漏lectriques chinoises, P脙漏kin riposte en ciblant le porc, le cognac et les produits laitiers europ脙漏ens.

Pour limiter les d脙漏g脙垄ts des droits de douane am脙漏ricains sur nos voitures, l'Union europ脙漏enne accepte d'ouvrir son march脙漏 au porc am脙漏ricain, produit avec des additifs interdits chez nous. Pour ouvrir le march脙漏 du Mercosur, certains sont pr脙陋ts 脙聽 sacrifier les fili脙篓res viande de l'Union europ脙漏enne. On marche sur la t脙陋te! Stop!

L'agriculture europ脙漏enne doit cesser d'脙陋tre la variable d'ajustement de toutes les querelles commerciales. Je ne cesserai de le r脙漏p脙漏ter, l'Europe doit d脙漏fendre son agriculture comme un secteur strat脙漏gique 脙聽 part enti脙篓re, au m脙陋me titre que son industrie.

脗聽脗聽C脙漏line Imart (PPE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, tout est bon dans le cochon et surtout dans le cochon europ脙漏en. Ce n'est malheureusement pas l'avis des Chinois, qui se vengent sur ses pieds, ses oreilles, ses abats, en repr脙漏sailles des mesures europ脙漏ennes sur les v脙漏hicules 脙漏lectriques. Encore une fois, les Chinois tapent l脙聽 o脙鹿 脙搂a fait mal. Avec des droits de douane 脙漏lev脙漏s, c'est tout le commerce avec la Chine qui est en p脙漏ril pour la fili脙篓re. Or, ce sont ces parties du porc que nous ne consommons pas en Europe et qui trouvent leurs d脙漏bouch脙漏s l脙聽-bas. C'est vital pour l'脙漏quilibre de la fili脙篓re et ce blocage chinois aura des cons脙漏quences concr脙篓tes pour les consommateurs, l'envol des prix.

Apr脙篓s l'accord avec les 脙聣tats-Unis, les droits chinois sur le cognac, l'heure n'est plus au laisser-faire. L'Europe doit montrer les muscles. Nous avons des instruments anti-coercition efficaces qui permettent aussi de riposter avec force lorsque nos producteurs sont attaqu脙漏s. Ce sont des outils que nous pouvons mobiliser pour faire plier les autorit脙漏s chinoises. Leurs mesures sont parfaitement ill脙漏gales. P脙漏kin sort le bazooka, sortons le char d'assaut, sinon nous perdrons toute cr脙漏dibilit脙漏 aux yeux de nos producteurs, de nos citoyens. C'est la nouvelle r脙漏alit脙漏 du commerce international. Les r脙篓gles du jeu ont chang脙漏 et l'Europe doit 脙陋tre forte pour d脙漏fendre nos int脙漏r脙陋ts sans trembler.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cristina Maestre (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, alguien deber脙颅a mirar eso de que sea el campo el que pague siempre la factura de los conflictos ajenos.脗聽Ahora, el boomerang por la disputa de los coches el脙漏ctricos le llega al sector porcino.

El sector porcino europeo no compite con dumping, sino ofreciendo productos de calidad, con trazabilidad y transparencia. Es un sector que ha sabido ganarse la confianza de los consumidores chinos, no solo con sus carnes frescas y transformadas, sino tambi脙漏n con los despojos y subproductos, muy apreciados en medicina, biotecnolog脙颅a o alimentaci脙鲁n animal.

En 2023, las exportaciones de carne de cerdo a China superaron los 2脗聽500 millones de euros, generando decenas de miles de empleos en el medio rural. Esta fortaleza no nace de pr脙隆cticas desleales, sino del esfuerzo en competitividad e innovaci脙鲁n de nuestros productores y de sus trabajadores.

Es fundamental explicar a las autoridades chinas que jugamos limpio, y lo hacemos con transparencia. Por eso, se脙卤or comisario, apostamos por la v脙颅a del di脙隆logo y la negociaci脙鲁n. Queremos que los alimentos sigan siendo puentes de confianza y de prosperidad, y no moneda de cambio en disputas comerciales que nada tienen que ver con ellos.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mireia Borr脙隆s Pab脙鲁n (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, se脙卤or脙颅as, una vez m脙隆s, nuestros agricultores y ganaderos vuelven a ser la diana de disputas geopol脙颅ticas que no han provocado.

Es lo que ha ocurrido, por ejemplo, con Estados Unidos, donde han aceptado un arancel del 15脗聽% a nuestras exportaciones mientras la Comisi脙鲁n abr脙颅a el mercado, de par en par, a sectores vulnerables, como el de los frutos secos.

Pero tambi脙漏n est脙隆n padeciendo actualmente con la negociaci脙鲁n de Mercosur, donde la Comisi脙鲁n 芒聙聰con premeditaci脙鲁n y alevos脙颅a芒聙聰 est脙隆 intentando cerrar un acuerdo comercial que sabe, de antemano, va a ser letal para nuestro campo y para nuestras zonas rurales.

Y ahora, adem脙隆s, China, pues nuestros ganaderos son rehenes de una investigaci脙鲁n infundada como represalia por los aranceles europeos al coche el脙漏ctrico chino.

Y todo esto se lo debemos a la se脙卤ora Von der Leyen, todo un 脙漏xito negociador de la Comisi脙鲁n Europea que es incapaz de defender, de manera firme, a quienes producen, hoy en d脙颅a, la mejor carne de porcino del mundo. Y es que cada negociaci脙鲁n de Bruselas acaba con el mismo resultado: el campo europeo es el que paga la factura, y cada factura es un paso m脙隆s a la ruina de nuestros agricultores y ganaderos.

Por eso mismo decimos, se脙卤or comisario, que ustedes no est脙隆n construyendo Europa: la est脙隆n cavando.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Georgiana Teodorescu (ECR). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, fermierii europeni nu sunt doar produc脛聝tori de carne, lapte sau cereale. Ei sunt cei care dau via脠聸脛聝 satelor, p脛聝streaz脛聝 tradi脠聸iile 脠聶i se asigur脛聝 c脛聝 fiecare familie din Europa are hran脛聝 s脛聝n脛聝toas脛聝 pe mas脛聝, la pre脠聸uri accesibile. Ei muncesc 脙庐n frig, 脙庐n ar脠聶i脠聸脛聝, 脙庐n vremuri de incertitudine, pentru 450 de milioane de consumatori europeni 脠聶i pentru dreptul lor fundamental la hran脛聝.

Nu putem r脛聝m脙垄ne indiferen脠聸i atunci c脙垄nd decizii politice risc脛聝 s脛聝 le 脠聶ubrezeasc脛聝 munca; bugete reduse, acorduri comerciale nefavorabile, condi脠聸ii dure 脠聶i legisla脠聸ii contraproductive, toate t脛聝v脛聝lug 脙庐n ultimii ani.脗聽Solidaritatea european脛聝 trebuie s脛聝 脙庐nsemne mai mult dec脙垄t vorbe, trebuie s脛聝 fie un scut real pentru cei care ne hr脛聝nesc.

脙聨n acela脠聶i timp, 脠聶tim c脛聝 rela脠聸ia noastr脛聝 cu China este strategic脛聝 脠聶i de mare valoare. Nu c脛聝ut脛聝m conflict, ci cooperare. Credem c脛聝 orice provocare poate fi dep脛聝脠聶it脛聝 prin dialog sincer 脠聶i respect reciproc. Europa 脠聶i China pot g脛聝si solu脠聸ii echilibrate, care s脛聝 protejeze interesele ambelor p脛聝r脠聸i. 脙聨ntr-un context mondial tot mai complicat, este vital ca UE 脠聶i China s脛聝 g脛聝seasc脛聝 o cale diplomatic脛聝 s脛聝 脙庐脠聶i fac脛聝 reciproc concesii, astfel 脙庐nc脙垄t rela脠聸iile s脛聝 poat脛聝 evolua 脙庐n bine. E nevoie de 脙庐n脠聸elepciune 脠聶i flexibilitate.

Fermierii europeni se trezesc zilnic 脙庐nainte de r脛聝s脛聝rit pentru a hr脛聝ni milioane de oameni. Este obliga脠聸ia noastr脛聝 s脛聝 ne trezim 脠聶i noi ca lideri 脠聶i s脛聝-i ap脛聝r脛聝m cu aceea脠聶i d脛聝ruire.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Barry Cowen (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, colleagues, China's recent decision to impose tariffs on European pork is unjustified and it demands a response that is firm, fair and strategic.

China's market is vital for Europe's pig sector, as has been said, especially for bi-products, where no other market offers the same return.

Just last year, pork exports to China were worth over EUR 2.4 billion. In Ireland's case, that is 61脗聽000 tonnes of pigmeat worth nearly EUR 100 million.脗聽Losing that income would be a serious blow to farm families and rural communities.

The Commission has pledged to defend foreign producers and now it must follow through on that pledge. Farmers cannot be collateral damage in bigger trade disputes driven by other sectors.

Now is the time to be firm without being reckless. A trade war with China benefits no one, and protecting farmers must remain central to the EU's strategy going forward.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carmen Crespo D脙颅az (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, los aranceles vienen en un momento dificultoso del sector, que ha pasado de 2脗聽700 millones en脗聽2023 a 1脗聽000 millones en脗聽2024.

Por tanto, es un momento dif脙颅cil y tenemos que hacer una defensa firme y proactiva desde la Uni脙鲁n Europea. No puede ser que el sector agroalimentario vuelva a ser moneda de cambio, que pague las consecuencias de otras guerras comerciales y, adem脙隆s, hay que luchar porque este sector agrario no tiene colch脙鲁n, realmente no puede aguantar.

En cuanto a los Estados Unidos, tenemos que negociar, seguir negociando por el vino, los espirituosos, el aceite y la aceituna de mesa.

Y, en cuanto al porcino, que es fundamental para las zonas rurales, tambi脙漏n hay que luchar y poner encima de la mesa todas y cada una de las posibilidades de la Uni脙鲁n Europea. En este caso, creo que es fundamental que la Uni脙鲁n Europea solicite una investigaci脙鲁n a la Organizaci脙鲁n Mundial del Comercio sobre lo que est脙隆 haciendo China, que no tiene base en la investigaci脙鲁n.

Claro que hay que abrir nuevos mercados y claro que hay que abrir nuevos acuerdos comerciales, pero con reciprocidad y con esa oficina de control de importaciones.

脗聽脗聽Marko Ve脜隆ligaj (S&D). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, u vremenima kada trgovinski sukobi bujaju diljem svijeta poljoprivreda se na脜隆la na najve脛聡em udaru, od Mercosura, preko novog sporazuma sa Sjedinjenim Ameri脛聧kim Dr脜戮avama pa evo sada i kinesko uvo脛聭enje carina na svinjetinu i svinjske prera脛聭evine.脗聽Situacija je sve alarmantnija za na脜隆e poljoprivrednike.

Odluka koja dolazi iz Kine imat 脛聡e te脜隆ke posljedice za sektor svinjogojstva u Europskoj uniji, obzirom da je Kina posljednjih godina bila jedno od klju脛聧nih izvoznih odredi脜隆ta za europsku svinjetinu.脗聽Gomilat 脛聡e se vi脜隆kovi, cijene 脛聡e padati, a sektor 脛聡e patiti.

A va脜戮no je re脛聡i da ova odluka iz Kine je potpuno neopravdana jer proizvo脛聭a脛聧i svinjskog mesa iz Unije ne sudjeluju u antidampin脜隆kim praksama te su u potpunosti sura脛聭ivali s istragama i sa svim zahtjevima kineskih vlasti.

Hitno trebamo mjere koje 脛聡e suzbiti negativne posljedice ovih najnovijih carina, kao i trgovinsku strategiju koja u centar stavlja poljoprivrednu proizvodnju jer bez poljoprivrednika nema hrane, a bez hrane nema sigurnosti te nam stoga za脜隆tita doma脛聡e poljoprivredne proizvodnje mora biti prioritet.

脗聽脗聽Daniel Buda (PPE). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, stima脠聸i colegi, fermierii europeni sunt din nou pu脠聶i 脙庐n dificultate 脙庐n cadrul unor dispute comerciale. China impune tarife antidumping de p脙垄n脛聝 la 62脗聽% pentru carnea de porc european脛聝, care este o reac脠聸ie la presiunile legitime pe care Europa le exercit脛聝 脙庐n sectorul auto. Dar cei care pl脛聝tesc pre脠聸ul sunt agricultorii no脠聶tri, fermele de familie, procesatorii 脠聶i comunit脛聝脠聸ile rurale.

Fermierii europeni devin astfel o moned脛聝 de schimb 脙庐ntr-un conflict care nu are leg脛聝tur脛聝 cu munca lor, dar care le amenin脠聸脛聝 direct veniturile 脠聶i stabilitatea. Consecin脠聸ele se vor resim脠聸i 脙庐n 脙庐ntreaga Uniunea European脛聝, sc脛聝deri de pre脠聸uri pe pie脠聸ele interne, astfel 脙庐nc脙垄t, domnule comisar - am rug脛聝mintea s脛聝 m脛聝 脠聶i urm脛聝ri脠聸i, domnule comisar - v脛聝 cer ca executivul european s脛聝 adopte o pozi脠聸ie ferm脛聝 脠聶i s脛聝 vin脛聝 imediat cu m脛聝suri concrete de sprijin pentru acest sector, dar 脠聶i cu o strategie comercial脛聝 unitar脛聝, care s脛聝 reduc脛聝 vulnerabilitatea Uniunii Europene 脙庐n fa脠聸a presiunilor externe. Europa trebuie s脛聝-脠聶i apere fermierii 脠聶i s脛聝 transmit脛聝 un mesaj clar: nu vom ceda niciodat脛聝 脠聶antajului comercial.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Elisabetta Gualmini (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear Commissioner, China's decision to impose anti-dumping duties of up to 62脗聽% on the importation of European pork products is an act of profound economic injustice that damages our farmers a lot, with estimated costs of around USD脗聽2脗聽billion. It just happens when China itself is facing a significant domestic overcapacity of pork products, so there are no real and legitimate reasons for such tariffs.

Beijing's decision is nothing more than a retaliation to the principle of multilateralism and to a system of shared rules which is now more than ever under attack. So if we also consider Trump's duties by 15脗聽% on European food products, it is crystal clear that Europe is more and more squeezed between two powerful autocrats using trade as a weapon.

But the problem is bigger today. The problem is that the strategic autonomy of the European Union is not enough. We must work for a stronger, united, powerful political union, not only a market, which makes a difference in the world, and not be a hostage of lunatic superpowers.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Walsh (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, why should Irish pigmeat producers have to bear the brunt of political trade disputes, with a market as crucial as China? Just a few years ago, China accounted for 40脗聽% of Ireland's total pork exports. But recent trade tensions have seen this figure drop to only 19脗聽% last year. Hardworking producers are now slapped with import duties ranging from 15脗聽% to a staggering 62脗聽%.

Commissioner, I ask why these measures that have been proposed, which are weakening Ireland's pork sector at a time when our farmers are striving to compete fairly on the world stage, while at the same time working hard to maintain the food standards we have set here in this House for them.

The Commission must urgently rethink its trade approach to ensure that Irish farmers, and indeed the wider farming community, are not treated as a bargaining chip in political negotiations, but rather supported as vital contributors to our economy and food security.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Mr Vice-President, in accordance with Rule 178(10), I would also like to react to the answer that Ms Walsh will give.

Thank you, colleague, for accepting this blue card.

My question goes like this: we know that China watches really closely what we are doing, and they will also watch when we impose legislation that while it brings our continent forward, it also is hard for our farmers sometimes 芒聙聯 do you think that in this climate where we ask a lot from our farmers, they do this intentionally at this very precise moment to hurt us?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Walsh (PPE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 That's a good question, colleague, and I appreciate the opportunity to reply.

I mean, in many ways, yes, this is where politics 芒聙聯 and bad politics 芒聙聯 impacts the pockets and the tables of every EU citizen right now, and indeed international person. Because tariffs 芒聙聯 as we heard from the President of the Commission today and we've heard repeatedly 芒聙聯 only impact those at the end of the food supply chain, which is farmers and those that eat it.

For me, I think it's a really important, regardless of whatever happens outside of the EU Member State bloc, that we're really clear, concise, and we're working forward and protecting those that are impacted the most, which, for me and my constituency, are farmers.

脗聽脗聽Wouter Beke (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, de nieuwe Chinese invoerheffingen van 60脗聽% op varkensvlees zijn een harde klap voor onze landbouwers. Belgi脙芦 dreigt het hoogste tarief te krijgen, terwijl onze boeren in de onderzochte periode amper toegang hadden tot de Chinese markt. Dat is niet zomaar een oneerlijke handelspraktijk. Dat is pure geopolitieke druk en een poging om lidstaten uit elkaar te spelen. Boeren mogen niet de dupe worden van Europese plannen om extra heffingen in te voeren op elektrische auto芒聙聶s uit China. We mogen ons niet laten chanteren.

Commissaris, u bent commissaris voor Defensie. Onze landbouwers hebben ook iemand nodig die hun belangen verdedigt. Te meer omdat de invoerheffingen niet het enige probleem zijn. De heffingen komen bovenop de Europese milieuregels, minder landbouwbudget en angst bij de boeren voor Mercosur enzovoort. Dat alles terwijl we er allemaal van overtuigd zijn dat strategische autonomie zo belangrijk is. Onze landbouwers verdienen zekerheid, zodat we competitief kunnen blijven op die wereldmarkt en onze voedselzekerheid kunnen garanderen. Daarom hoop ik dat u deze bezorgdheden meeneemt en dat er maatregelen worden genomen tegen deze heffingen en ook maatregelen om de landbouwsector te ondersteunen.

脗聽脗聽Vytenis Povilas Andriukaitis (S&D). 芒聙聯 Gerbiamas pirmininke, a脜隆 kalb脛聴siu lietuvi脜隆kai. Juo labiau, kad Kinijos ir Europos S脛聟jungos santykiuose muit脜鲁 ir tarif脜鲁 problemos buvo vis脛聟 laik脛聟 ant darbo stalo. Tenka prisiminti, kada buvo afrikinio kiauli脜鲁 maro problema, sud脛聴tinga, ir 脜隆iuo atveju, kada tas maras po to persik脛聴l脛聴 脛炉 Kinij脛聟, Kinija taip pat prad脜戮ioje gyn脛聴si, gyn脛聴si nepagr脛炉stais tarifais, bet po to, kada jos 脜芦k脛炉 i脜隆tiko katastrofa, ji labai smarkiai atv脛聴r脛聴 mums kiaulienos rinkas. A脜隆 tikrai sveikinu Komisij脛聟 ir Komisij脛聟 raginu dabar viena 芒聙聯 kuo grei脛聧iau imtis priemoni脜鲁, i脜隆siai脜隆kinant iki galo ir keliant klausim脛聟 d脛聴l Kinijos 脛炉vedam脜鲁 nes脛聟脜戮ining脜鲁 muit脜鲁, j脛炉 perkelti 脛炉 Pasaulin脛聴s prekybos organizacijos gin脛聧us, nes tai leist脜鲁 pad脛聴ti grei脛聧iau priversti Kinij脛聟 v脛聴l, galb脜芦t, s脛聴sti prie deryb脜鲁 stalo ir taip pat kit脜鲁 rink脜鲁 atv脛聴rimas irgi yra numeris vienas.

脗聽脗聽Diana Iovanovici 脜聻o脜聼oac脛聝 (NI). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, da, v脛聝 sup脛聝ra脠聸i c脛聝 acum China instituie taxe foarte mari, nu numai pe carnea de porc, ci pe foarte multe alte produse. 脙聨ns脛聝, din p脛聝cate, nu a脠聸i folosit diploma脠聸ia cu China 脠聶i nu 脙庐i cunoa脠聶te脠聸i 脠聶i nu i-a脠聸i tratat cum se cuvine. China, la acest moment de脠聸ine cea mai 脙庐nalt脛聝 putere, este cea mai 脙庐nalt脛聝 putere a lumii. Europa a c脛聝zut. Din p脛聝cate, vom merge din ce 脙庐n ce mai r脛聝u.

V脛聝 deranjeaz脛聝 acum 脠聶i vre脠聸i s脛聝 ajuta脠聸i fermierii. P脛聝i, 脙庐n Rom脙垄nia, dumneavoastr脛聝, de aici, dar 脠聶i de la Comisia European脛聝, a脠聸i ac脠聸ionat 脙庐mpotriva fermierilor rom脙垄ni. Ne-a脠聸i adus pest脛聝 porcin脛聝, de脠聶i nu a existat niciodat脛聝 脠聶i ne-a脠聸i interzis exporturile - 脠聶i acum sunt blocate - pentru ca celelalte 脠聸脛聝ri, Fran脠聸a, Germania, Spania, Italia, puterile Europei s脛聝 aib脛聝 c脙垄脠聶tig.

C脙垄nd v-am rugat s脛聝 ne ajuta脠聸i fermierii, nu v-a interesat 脠聶i a脠聸i falimentat. Acum pe cine o s脛聝 ajuta脠聸i? Tot ai marilor puteri europene? Sau v脛聝 g脙垄ndi脠聸i 脠聶i la noi, cei din Europa de Est?

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Dost脙隆l (NI). 芒聙聯 Pane p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, r脙隆d bych se zdvo脜聶ile zeptal pana komisa脜聶e, pro脛聧 jste nevzal kolegu 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧e a taky pana S脙颅kelu od Global Gateway a nedoprovodili jste minul脙陆 t脙陆den slovensk脙漏ho premi脙漏ra Fica do Pekingu? Obchodn脙颅 diplomacie v n脙隆sleduj脙颅c脙颅m stolet脙颅 bude vy脜戮adovat vz脙隆jemnou 脙潞ctu. Kdybyste 脛聦脙颅n脛聸, kl脙颅脛聧ov脙漏mu obchodn脙颅mu partnerovi, p脜聶ijeli pop脜聶脙隆t k v脙陆ro脛聧脙颅, tak byste tam t脜聶eba vy脜聶e脜隆ili i vep脜聶ov脙漏 nebo elektroauta nebo t脜聶eba kriticky d脜炉le脜戮it脙漏 l脙漏ky. A t脜聶eba byste zlep脜隆ili vyjedn脙隆vac脙颅 postaven脙颅 Evropsk脙漏 unie v脜炉脛聧i Spojen脙陆m st脙隆t脜炉m, tak脜戮e byste na golfu nemuseli podepisovat nevyv脙隆脜戮en脙漏 dohody. V 脛聦脙颅n脛聸 m脙隆 Evropa otev脜聶eno. Je to pr脜炉myslov脙隆 velmoc. Pot脜聶ebuje, aby glob脙隆ln脙颅 obchod fungoval. Nem脙隆me 脜戮脙隆dn脙陆 skute脛聧n脙陆 d脜炉vod ke konfliktu, zato spoustu mo脜戮n脙陆ch synergi脙颅. T脙漏 spolupr脙隆ci sta脛聧脙颅 j脙颅t naproti.

Nebo je tak脙漏 m脜炉脜戮ete ur脙隆脜戮et jako Kaja Kallasov脙隆, ale pak holt neprod脙隆te to vep脜聶ov脙漏 a farm脙隆脜聶i utrp脙颅 脜隆kodu. Mil脙颅 komisa脜聶i, podle m脙漏ho n脙隆zoru jste 脙潞脜聶edn脙颅ci. M脛聸li byste v脙漏st kvalitn脙颅 obchodn脙颅 diplomacii a prosp脙颅vat prosperit脛聸 脛聧lensk脙陆ch st脙隆t脜炉. Opravdu to nejde l脙漏pe?

(Br脛芦v脛聛 mikrofona uzst脛聛脜隆anos beigas.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andrius Kubilius, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, honourable Members, let me start by really thanking all of you for your thoughtful contributions during today's debate. I understand and share many of your concerns raised.

Let me start from one general remark: as to EU relations with China, let me recall President von der Leyen's message from this morning's State of the Union address, which points to the significant challenges. She made clear that unless these challenges are adequately addressed by China, the European Union would not hesitate to use all instruments at its disposal to safeguard fair competition and defend its legitimate interests. The European Union's relationship with China is important, both trade and investment aspects. For the European Union, it will be crucial to rebalance this relationship around transparency, predictability and reciprocity.

Also, I want to underline that the Commission will continue to do everything possible to defend EU farmers and exporters when confronted with such abusive use of trade defence instruments. We consider the three investigations launched by China as retaliatory and unjustified, and a clear violation of the international trade rules. I recall that China is a key market for EU pork exports. Together with the sector, the Commission will examine the facts underlying the imposition of the provisional duties and will carefully assess all the information available in order to decide next steps. The Commission will consider all options to protect EU industry and economic interests.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The debate is closed.

IN THE CHAIR: ROBERTA METSOLA

11. Amendment of the agenda
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Pursuant to Rule 164(2), and in agreement with the political groups, I wish to make the following proposal for a change to the agenda:

Parliament's statements on 'Solidarity with Poland following the deliberate violation of Polish airspace by Russian drones' are added as the fourth item of tomorrow morning's sitting.

As a consequence, the sitting will start tomorrow at 8:30.

If there are no objections, then this change is approved.

This request is referred to the Committee on Legal Affairs.

S脛聮DI VADA: ROBERTS Z脛陋LE
