脗聽

脗聽脗聽Christine Anderson, on behalf of the ESN Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, yes, there is foreign propaganda from Russia and elsewhere, but the Commission's so-called Democracy Shield does not defend our democracy against outside threats. Your shield protects your narrative from internal opposition. You want to control and steer public debate. And for that purpose, you fund fact-checking networks, you pressure platforms through the DSA by threatening them with fines in the billions.

You smear the opposition, dismiss legitimate questions as conspiracy theories and brand descending views as hate speech or disinformation. What you call protection of democracy is, in fact, protection of your illegitimate power grab.

Citizens are sick of this, so you jail them for speaking their mind. Your idea of defending democracy destroys democracy. Without free speech there is no democracy. Without free speech, there is only tyranny and totalitarianism.

Well, congratulations, you are well on your way to totalitarianism. But the good news is you will fail. The people will stop you, and the day they do is the day that I will rejoice.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mika Aaltola (PPE). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, arvoisa komission j脙陇sen, demokratian kilvest脙陇: demokratia 芒聙聯 avoin sellainen 芒聙聯 on Euroopan perusta. Sen t脙陇ytyy hengitt脙陇脙陇, mutta sill脙陇 on ulkoisia uhkia: Ven脙陇j脙陇n, Kiinan, Pohjois-Korean ja Iranin kaltaisia toimijoita, jotka aktiivisesti k脙陇ytt脙陇v脙陇t suunnattomia resursseja meid脙陇n demokraattisen tilamme ja sananvapauden kaventamiseen Euroopassa.

Ven脙陇j脙陇n tiedustelupalvelujen budjetista 80 prosenttia menee ep脙陇vakauttamisoperaatioihin, ja ne suuntautuvat p脙陇脙陇osin t脙陇nne Eurooppaan. Meid脙陇n t脙陇ytyy olla siis hyvin tarkkana asioiden suhteen. Meill脙陇 on my脙露s demokratiassa ongelmia sis脙陇ll脙陇 芒聙聯 my脙露s Suomessa, joka on vahva demokratia. Mediatila kaventuu. Suomen Tietotoimiston mahdollinen rapauttaminen ei auta asiaa yht脙陇脙陇n. Demokratia el脙陇脙陇, jos me est脙陇mme sen ulkoisia uhkia ja annamme sen uusiutua.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vasile D脙庐ncu (S&D). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, domnule comisar, un European Democracy Shield nu este un lux institu脠聸ional, ci o necesitate strategic脛聝. Ast脛聝zi, democra脠聸iile nu mai sunt atacate doar cu arme, ci prin manipularea informa脠聸iei, prin distorsionarea proceselor electorale 脠聶i prin subminarea 脙庐ncrederii cet脛聝脠聸enilor. Avem nevoie de ac脠聸iuni urgente 脠聶i rapide.脗聽Propun trei direc脠聸ii urgente.

Prima, m-am bucurat s脛聝 o aud din partea domnului comisar, un centru european pentru rezilien脠聸脛聝 democratic脛聝, conectat cu serviciile na脠聸ionale, capabil s脛聝 monitorizeze 脠聶i s脛聝 reac脠聸ioneze la ingerin脠聸e 脠聶i atacuri hibride 脙庐n timp real.

A doua ar fi un cadru comun de protec脠聸ie a proceselor electorale, standarde minime pentru securizarea bazelor de date, transparen脠聸脛聝 脙庐n finan脠聸area campaniilor 脠聶i control asupra microtargeting-ului electoral.

A treia, un mecanism de alert脛聝 rapid脛聝 脠聶i contra nara脠聸iune public脛聝 pentru ca cet脛聝脠聸enii s脛聝 primeasc脛聝 informa脠聸ii verificate 脙庐nainte ca propaganda s脛聝 umple 脙庐ntreg spa脠聸iul public.

Dragi colegi, dac脛聝 nu vom construi aceste mecanisme, urm脛聝toarele alegeri vor deveni 脠聸int脛聝 pentru actorii ostili 脠聶i sper, domnule comisar, ca ReArm 脠聶i SAFE, mecanismele noastre s脛聝...

(Pre脠聶edinta a retras cuv脙垄ntul vorbitorului)

(Vorbitorul a acceptat s脛聝 r脛聝spund脛聝 la o 脙庐ntrebare adresat脛聝 脙庐n conformitate cu procedura 芒聙聻cartona脠聶ului albastru芒聙聺)

脗聽脗聽Jaroslav B脜戮och (PfE). 芒聙聯 Pan脙颅 p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, j脙隆 jsem dnes velmi pe脛聧liv脛聸 poslouchal projev Ursuly von der Leyenov脙漏 芒聙聯 a speci脙隆ln脛聸 v moment脛聸, kdy hovo脜聶ila o demokratick脙漏m 脜隆t脙颅tu. My pot脜聶ebujeme m脙颅t n脙隆stroje a pot脜聶ebujeme v脛聸d脛聸t, jak m脙隆me bojovat proti vn脛聸j脜隆脙颅mu vm脛聸脜隆ov脙隆n脙颅 do na脜隆eho demokratick脙漏ho syst脙漏mu i jak m脙隆me bojovat proti dezinformac脙颅m. Z脙隆rove脜聢 v tu chv脙颅li 脜聶脙颅kala, 脜戮e v s脙隆le zde se snad n脛聸kdo toho boj脙颅. No, j脙隆 si nemysl脙颅m, 脜戮e by se tady n脛聸kdo b脙隆l. J脙隆 si mysl脙颅m, 脜戮e to je Evropsk脙隆 komise, kter脙隆 se boj脙颅 s脙颅l脙颅c脙颅ho tlaku opozice a ob脛聧an脜炉 Evropsk脙漏 unie, kte脜聶脙颅 toho maj脙颅 u脜戮 pln脙漏 zuby. 脗聽脗聽Stefano Cavedagna (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, sappiamo bene che le ingerenze straniere, la manipolazione delle informazioni, le minacce ibride mettono in pericolo la libert脙聽 dei nostri cittadini, ed 脙篓 proprio per questo che lo scudo per la democrazia pu脙虏 essere utile.

Ma noi conservatori vigileremo fino all'ultimo giorno perch脙漏 questo non diventi un pretesto per tappare la bocca ai cittadini o per zittire le persone di cui non si condividono le opinioni. Siamo per la libert脙聽.

Lo dimostra lo scandalo del脗聽green gate, che ha mostrato come, purtroppo, alcune interferenze esistano ma siano pi脙鹿 interne all'Unione che esterne alla stessa. Ed 脙篓 per questo che, come gruppo ECR, abbiamo ottenuto un gruppo di lavoro per chiedere la trasparenza delle ONG, che purtroppo sono state un problema negli ultimi anni piuttosto che una risorsa.

E infine, il cosiddetto chat control: noi conservatori siamo contrari a qualsiasi sistema di sorveglianza di massa. La protezione dei minori dagli abusi 脙篓 una responsabilit脙聽 che ci chiama tutti, ma deve essere esercitata con buon senso. Una ferma risposta anche a tutela dei diritti fondamentali.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandro Gozi (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, nous sommes confront脙漏s 脙聽 des attaques in脙漏dites contre nos d脙漏mocraties.

Oui, les annonces de la pr脙漏sidente de la Commission ce matin sur la r脙漏silience des m脙漏dias et les fonds pour le pluralisme vont dans le bon sens. Nous devons pleinement exploiter les moyens dont on dispose et en introduire de nouveaux. La d脙漏mocratie europ脙漏enne n'est pas 脙聽 vendre 脙聽 Poutine et 脙聽 ses idiots utiles en Europe. Elle ne sera jamais une monnaie d'脙漏change dans un accord commercial avec Donald脗聽Trump.

Nous avons d脙漏j脙聽 des outils pour prot脙漏ger nos processus 脙漏lectoraux et lutter contre la d脙漏sinformation: le r脙篓glement sur les services num脙漏riques (DSA) et le r脙篓glement sur la transparence et le ciblage de la publicit脙漏 脙聽 caract脙篓re politique. Appliquons-les pleinement, Monsieur le Commissaire, sans faire aucune concession, et comblons aussi les lacunes existantes.

Les attaques des puissances 脙漏trang脙篓res envers notre libert脙漏 d脙漏mocratique sont de plus en plus s脙漏rieuses. Avan脙搂ons donc enfin, chers coll脙篓gues, vers un accord solide sur la directive sur la transparence de la repr脙漏sentation d'int脙漏r脙陋ts pour le compte de pays tiers comme la Chine. Nous n'avons pas le luxe du temps. Il faut agir maintenant.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kim Van Sparrentak (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, did you know that Chancellor Merz went to Canada this summer to shoot polar bear cubs? No? Good 芒聙聯 because that means the Kremlin's latest disinformation campaign did not reach you. Why would Russia spread such a ridiculous story, you might ask? Well, it's easier to sway an election than to win a war. So undermining democratic European leaders is part of their playbook.

I'm happy that the Commission will work on mapping these kinds of disinformation campaigns more efficiently, but when is the Commission finally taking action against the spread of disinformation? When is the Commission finally putting an end to the fact that we are all carrying addictive manipulation machines in our pockets?

We need to ban addictive design features and divisive recommender systems to make the internet safe for everyone, minors and adults alike. After their latest updates, generative AI models only produce more disinformation and, meanwhile, the Commission is flirting with the idea of reopening the AI Act 芒聙聯 the only protection we have. Commissioner, only ambitious and decisive action will protect both our citizens and our democracies.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Danilo Della Valle (The Left). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, una discussione seria e profonda su quali siano le minacce per la democrazia e su quale sia lo stato della democrazia andrebbe fatta seriamente nel Parlamento europeo, credo. Non solo per difendersi dalle minacce esterne.

A tal proposito potremmo chiedere una masterclass ai nostri alleati statunitensi, che hanno una lunga storia di tentativi di condizionamento non troppo democratico dei paesi non in linea con la loro politica estera.

Quello che davvero mi preoccupa, per脙虏, 脙篓 lo stato di salute del grado di democrazia nei nostri paesi, cosa che vedo non preoccupa affatto questa maggioranza: basta aprire uno smartphone, ogni giorno, per vedere le immagini di arresti e violenze contro le centinaia di migliaia di manifestanti pacifici che protestano contro il genocidio in corso a Gaza.

A Berlino, Roma, Parigi stiamo assistendo al proliferare di misure restrittive e leggi repressive che limitano in modo sempre pi脙鹿 marcato il diritto di protesta nei diversi paesi europei.

Cos脙卢 come mi preoccupa la censura nei confronti delle voci di dissenso che si occupano del genocidio in corso a Gaza, contro i palestinesi.

O la messa al bando di tutto quello che rappresenta la cultura russa...

(La Presidente toglie la parola all'oratore)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Milan Mazurek (ESN). 芒聙聯 Madam President, please don't tell us about democracy, because actually you, the unelected bureaucrats, hate democracy the most. What kind of democracy do you even talk about in Europe? The one where if you criticise migrants for killing or raping women and children, you can end up in prison for something like 'racism' or hate speech? Or the one where if you are against the sex transition of young children, you can be prosecuted? Or maybe the one where if you vote for a candidate who wins the election but you don't like him, you just cancel the whole election like you did in Romania?

How many people in the European Union are in prison because they said or wrote something on social networks? Just their opinions. How many independent journalists are prosecuted and persecuted because they openly expose the truth about you and the consequences of your own actions? Yes, I agree, we need to defend democracy. We need to protect democracy, we need to protect it from you, because you are the biggest threat for democracy in the European Union! And if we want to restore democracy in the European Union, at first, we need to defeat you.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Madam Vice-President, in accordance with the last sentence of Rule 178(10), I would also like to have the opportunity, please, to react to the answer that Mr Mazurek will give me.

Mr Mazurek, you talked about the not so democratically elected people in this room. So I was just wondering, as the Members of this Parliament are directly democratically elected and the Commission is indirectly, through this Parliament, democratically elected, how much time have you spent in your life studying the institutions of the European Union?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Milan Mazurek (ESN), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much, Mr Sieper. I would wish for you to be more educated, because I was speaking to the Commissioners, which are not directly elected by the people of the European Union, but indirectly 芒聙聯 and this is the biggest difference. That's all.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Kollegor. V脙楼ra 脙露ppna, demokratiska och fria samh脙陇llen uts脙陇tts g脙楼ng p脙楼 g脙楼ng f脙露r cyberangrepp. Mitt land, Sverige, 脙陇r inget undantag. Bara den senaste m脙楼naden har Stockholm, G脙露teborg och Sk脙楼ne drabbats. 脙聳ver hundra tusen anst脙陇llda i skola, v脙楼rd och omsorg har f脙楼tt sina personuppgifter l脙陇ckta.

N脙陇r svenska och europeiska myndigheter i somras slog ut ett pro-ryskt cybern脙陇tverk visade vi vad samarbete kan 脙楼stadkomma. Men det r脙陇cker inte. Vi beh脙露ver bland annat mer resurser till Europeiska centret mot it- brottslighet inom Europol. Vi beh脙露ver st脙陇rka EU:s cybers脙陇kerhetsbyr脙楼. Och framf脙露r allt beh脙露ver vi enad kraft.

Jag hade 脙露nskat att 脙陇ven extremh脙露gern i det h脙陇r parlamentet tog denna debatt p脙楼 allvar, ist脙陇llet f脙露r att g脙露ra sig sj脙陇lv till offer. Offerkoftan b脙露rjar bli gammal.

脗聽脗聽Mario Mantovani (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, signor Presidente del Consiglio regionale lombardo, amici di Monza e Brianza, rafforzare la democrazia difendendola da ingerenze o minacce straniere non 脙篓 solo una necessit脙聽, ma un dovere nei confronti delle future generazioni.

Tuttavia, non saranno le cybersecurity, il controllo dei social o la cancellazione delle elezioni a far la differenza, anzi, queste misure saranno vissute come autoritarie.

Contro i condizionamenti delle autocrazie, peraltro comuniste, c'脙篓 un solo scudo, il pi脙鹿 forte: 脙篓 il rafforzamento della democrazia europea dall'interno. Riforme istituzionali che avvicinino l'Europa ai cittadini, che la liberino dalla tecnocrazia, che affermino i valori occidentali europei e le comuni radici culturali e religiose; una difesa comune; una sola voce in politica estera.

Il Parlamento europeo 脙篓 uno dei pochi al mondo privo di iniziativa legislativa, non pu脙虏 andare. Bisogna ridare ai cittadini la guida di un'Europa di cui si sentano parte, un'Europa che non sia protetta solo da un algoritmo ma dal senso di appartenenza di tutto il popolo europeo.

Viva la democrazia!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nela Riehl (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, have you noticed how military language has become very common these days? You just heard it again this morning, I guess, and you may like that or even dislike that but the truth is, our daily lives are being weaponised.

Across Europe we see how hybrid warfare undermines our societies. Russia's disinformation tries to destroy our trust in democracy itself. If we want to defend Europe, we must strengthen the foundations of our democracy. Culture is one of these foundations 芒聙聯 it's how we express free thought, the way we connect and our source of identity and belonging. Independent media is another foundation. Reliability and stability in times of disinformation is how we actually grow 芒聙聯 and that is not a given. And, of course, education. For active citizenship and critical thinking, basic and civic education are a must. There is no Democracy Shield without these foundations. Defending Europe means more than stopping military attacks 芒聙聯 it means strengthening and protecting democracy from within and investing in people.

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Dost脙隆l (NI). 芒聙聯 Pan脙颅 p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, mnoh脙漏 projevy Evropsk脙漏 komise ukazuj脙颅, 脜戮e skute脛聧n脛聸 mus脙颅me chr脙隆nit demokracii. 脜聽edes脙隆t procent ob脛聧an脜炉 nechce Ursulu von der Leyenovou. Ne, nejsou hybridn脙颅 hrozbou, naopak d脛聸laj脙颅 svoji pr脙隆ci. A k ovliv脜聢ov脙隆n脙颅 voleb: ne脛聧i脜聢 druh脙陆m to, co nechce脜隆, aby druz脙颅 脛聧inili tob脛聸. Proti gruz脙颅nsk脙漏 vl脙隆d脛聸 plat脙颅me pu脛聧isty, jednu ze stran moldavsk脙陆ch voleb tady nech脙隆te agitovat v Parlamentu a pomlouvat konkurenci. Do Slovenska premi脙漏ra Fica se nav脙隆脜戮脙颅te soustavn脛聸. A kdy脜戮 byly volby ve Spojen脙陆ch st脙隆tech, projevili jste 脙潞ctu americk脙陆m voli脛聧脜炉m? 脜聵ekli jste: Budeme spolupracovat s t脙颅m, koho si zvol脙颅te? Ne, vedli jste kampa脜聢 proti Trumpovi a te脛聫 se v脙隆m to vymstilo. Tak脜戮e pros脙颅m, chra脜聢te demokracii, super, ale za脛聧n脛聸te u sebe.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lena D脙录pont (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, dear Minister, we also created that committee to protect ourselves and to better prepare ourselves for hybrid threats. But what actually makes hybrid threats so successful? They usually go into the grey zones of society. They go into discussions and values that we actually need to balance, where we need to find not an easy balance between them. They tackle and address unclear competences. They address the lack of protocols, especially the lack of emergency protocols. And, of course, they are running on the lack of attribution.

So as a day like today, where we have seen an attack on Poland and immediately a disinformation campaign starting next to it, let me be loud and clear here: that wasn't an accident, that was deliberate. And we can and we should do whatever we can to stand aside Poland and really defend not our democracies but as well the European Union and each and every Member State of that European Union, because we are in it together and we will face it together.

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, s脙颅, cada vez son m脙隆s frecuentes, intensos y perturbadores los ataques contra el modelo europeo de democracia que protege su Derecho, provenientes de interferencias extranjeras, pero no solo de actores estatales 芒聙聰notoriamente Rusia芒聙聰, sino tambi脙漏n de actores no estatales, como esas plataformas digitales con redes sociales, cuyo modelo de negocio son algoritmos adictivos que estragan los valores europeos y causan un da脙卤o incuantificable en las nuevas generaciones 芒聙聰como sabemos bien los que tenemos hijos e hijas芒聙聰, polarizando, confrontando y generando odio.

Y para eso hace falta no solamente alfabetizaci脙鲁n digital, sino sobre todo un Derecho europeo. La Comisi脙鲁n Especial sobre el Escudo Europeo de la Democracia, que se puso en marcha en este Parlamento Europeo el 18脗聽de diciembre de脗聽2024, acaba de renovar su mandato un a脙卤o m脙隆s, pero arrojar脙隆 conclusiones, y lo importante es que la Comisi脙鲁n convierta esas conclusiones en nueva legislaci脙鲁n para reforzar no solamente la capacidad disuasoria frente a Rusia y sus interferencias, sino tambi脙漏n frente a las plataformas digitales, con multas tan contundentes como la que ha tenido el coraje de sacar adelante esta Comisi脙鲁n 芒聙聰la comisaria Teresa Ribera芒聙聰 contra Google.

脗聽脗聽Marieke Ehlers (PfE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, de Europese Unie presenteert zich als het morele kompas van de wereld. Maar voor onze ogen ontvouwt zich schandaal na schandaal binnen de EU. Denk aan hoe de Commissie onder leiding van Timmermans belastinggeld gebruikte om ngo芒聙聶s te betalen die de klimaatagenda van diezelfde Commissie verkondigden. Denk aan de Commissie die weigert de Pfizer芒聙聭sms芒聙聶jes van Von der Leyen openbaar te maken. Denk aan Qatargate, waarbij zakken met geld werden gevonden bij linkse europarlementari脙芦rs. Steeds hetzelfde patroon: geen transparantie, geen verantwoording.

Dan zou je verwachten dat wij als Parlement de verantwoordelijkheid zouden nemen om dit tot op de bodem uit te zoeken. Maar keer op keer worden voorstellen zoals die van de Patriots om een onderzoekscommissie in te stellen, weggestemd. Niet alleen door links, maar ook met steun van de EVP. Het beschermen van onze democratie vraagt moed. De moed om op te treden tegen inmenging van buitenaf. Maar vooral de moed om in de spiegel te kijken.

脗聽脗聽Sandro Ruotolo (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, oggi la Presidente von der Leyen ha affermato che la disinformazione sta dividendo le nostre societ脙聽, erodendo la democrazia, e脗聽allora possiamo dire che uno scudo democratico efficace deve basarsi su due pilastri.

Il primo 脙篓 la libert脙聽 di stampa: un'informazione indipendente e la protezione delle fonti sono l'unico antidoto contro la disinformazione. Se i giornalisti vengono spiati con spyware, ed 脙篓 illegale!, e se i media non sono indipendenti dalla politica, la democrazia diventa fragile.

Il secondo pilastro dello scudo 脙篓 la difesa del voto dalle interferenze straniere e dalle grandi piattaforme digitali; non possiamo permettere che gli Elon Musk di turno manipolino i nostri processi democratici. Non 脙篓 possibile che siano passati quasi due anni dall'apertura dell'indagine su X e ancora non sappiamo nulla.

Libert脙聽 di stampa e trasparenza digitale sono la spina dorsale della democrazia.

脗聽脗聽Pascale Piera (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, le bouclier d脙漏mocratique est un leurre. Il s'agit l脙聽 de l脙漏gitimer une ing脙漏rence interne en la d脙漏guisant en protection. Ce bouclier ne nous d脙漏fendra de rien. 脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cynthia N脙颅 Mhurch脙潞 (Renew). 芒聙聯 A Uachtar脙隆in, T脙隆 an Eoraip ag dul i ngleic le m脙漏ad脙潞 m脙鲁r bagairt脙颅 脙鲁 chur isteach eachtrach agus 脙鲁 fheachtais bhr脙漏agaisn脙漏ise. T脙隆 muin脙颅n in脙隆r n-institi脙潞id脙颅 Eorpacha faoi ionsa脙颅 agus deighilt脙颅 脙隆 gcoth脙潞 in脙隆r bpobail. T脙隆 an domhan ag crosbh脙鲁thar: t脙隆 an daonlathas faoi bhr脙潞 ollmh脙鲁r agus baineann r脙漏imis uathlathacha ar fud an domhain 脙潞s脙隆id as an mbr脙漏agaisn脙漏is mar uirlis faobhair fuatha. I Renew Europe, creidimid go bhfuil g脙漏argh脙隆 le gn脙颅omh脙潞 agus muid ag dul i ngleic le feachtais shofaistici脙潞la agus le gr脙潞pa脙颅 eagraithe at脙隆 d脙颅rithe ar ionramh脙隆il mh脙颅-ionraic eolais is faisn脙漏ise. Baineann na feachtais seo leas as leochaileachta脙颅 digiteacha chun an bonn a bhaint den daonlathas san Eoraip. T脙隆imid ag caint faoi mheasc脙隆n de chibirionsaithe, ionramh脙隆il na me脙隆n s脙鲁isialta agus br脙漏agaisn脙漏is. Conas is f脙漏idir troid ar ais? Infheist脙颅ocht in iriseoireacht d'ardchaighde脙隆n neamhsple脙隆ch, mar at脙隆 molta ag mo ghr脙潞pa, Renew Europe. Molaim do mo chomhghleacaithe anseo sa teach tac脙潞 len脙隆r n-iarratas agus Ciste Eorpach den s脙鲁rt seo a bhun脙潞.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members and Commissioner, this debate has shown the importance of strengthening democratic resilience. Many of you have today mentioned the real risk that our democracies face. We hope that these issues will be addressed by the Commission in its upcoming European Democracy Shield, and we are looking forward to the cooperation with Parliament.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael McGrath, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, I'd like to thank all of the honourable Members who spoke in this debate 芒聙聯 a very interesting debate, quite polarised, which has been the experience whenever we discuss issues around democracy. But I do want to thank everybody for their contribution, and I want to reaffirm that the European Parliament is an essential partner for the Commission in designing this important initiative: the European Democracy Shield.

We have already a solid EU digital and democratic rulebook. Its implementation and enforcement will be part and parcel of our work on democracy. Under this mandate, the Commission aims to move to a more operational phase of our work, empowering the actors who are key to democracy, from national authorities to the media, from citizens to civil society organisations.

Our democracies have to ultimately improve people's daily lives, defend them in the face of threats and strengthen their say in democracy. And this is exactly what the European Democracy Shield will be about. It will be a statement of what we, a Union of democracies respecting fundamental rights and the rule of law, represent in the world. It is conceived in our European way, following a whole-of-society approach and promoting our common democratic values and fundamental rights, such as freedom of expression and the exercise of electoral rights.

We will adopt actions to empower citizens to play a more active role in democratic life. We will protect the integrity of the information space and of the electoral process against interference from hostile actors. And we will further support free media and civil society actors.

I can assure those who were highly critical of this project before they even see the output of our work, that the Democracy Shield will certainly not be about censorship. Where we see censorship, it's where we see the voice of the free and independent media being shot down, where we see the voice of civil society being restricted. Censorship is where we see control of the information space for political purposes, and it can be where we see interference in the courts of Member States of the European Union.

I want to assure all of the Members of this House that this Democracy Shield will be in full respect, and will actually strengthen and uphold, the right to freedom of expression of the people of the European Union. This is about democracy, which is enshrined in Article 2 of our Treaty, which is a legal foundation of the European Union.

Many of those who are critical of our proposal are not here to hear the answers to their questions. In criticising the European Commission for decisions of national courts, that is not a function or a decision of the European Commission in any way. In fact, the European Commission steadfastly respects and upholds the independence of national courts and their decisions, provided they are acting in line with EU law. Democratically elected Members of this House should know that. And in referring to the Commission in a derogatory manner as being unelected, Members, of course, should recognise that the Commission has been elected by this House, so elected by the democratically elected Members of the European Parliament, which, of course, is the same way that prime ministers and governments all over Europe are elected year in, year out.

I want to assure people that the purpose of the Democracy Shield is to ensure that there is a safe space for people to make up their own mind when it comes to casting their vote. It is not about influencing how they vote; it is the sovereign, independent right of each individual citizen of the European Union to make a decision as to who to provide their support to in a democratic election, but they deserve to do so in a safe environment, free from interference by people and actors who have a political motivation for seeking to influence the outcome of our elections.脗聽What we need is more democracy: we need democracy at a local level, at a community level; we need more young people to become engaged in European democracy.

When people accuse us of being unelected, I would also make the point that many of us come from electoral backgrounds. I myself contested public elections in Ireland, where my name and my face was on the ballot paper, since the age of 22. Therefore I am very, very proud to be the European Commissioner for Democracy, and I will do all that I can working with the directly elected Members of this House, irrespective of their views, to ensure that we preserve and indeed strengthen the integrity of European democracy in the European Union. That is the purpose of what we will bring forward in the coming months in the European Democracy Shield. And I do not see how anybody could object to what I think is a noble and laudable objective.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

VORSITZ: KATARINA BARLEY
Vizepr脙陇sidentin

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Die Aussprache ist geschlossen.

脗聽

16. Serious threats to aviation and maritime transport from Global Navigation Satellite System interference: urgent need to build resilience against spoofing and jamming (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber die Erkl脙陇rung der Kommission zu dem dringend notwendigen Aufbau von Widerstandsf脙陇higkeit gegen Spoofing und Jamming angesichts der schwerwiegenden Gefahren f脙录r den Luft- und Seeverkehr durch St脙露rungen des globalen Satellitennavigationssystems (2025/2858(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andrius Kubilius, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, I start my statement on behalf of the Commission by saying very simple words: thank you. Thank you to the pilots of the aircraft that landed us safely when we, together with President von der Leyen, were travelling to Bulgaria during a very successful tour of seven frontline Member States, and when our satellite navigation on board was jammed.

These pilots are not the only ones who deal with this kind of threat. As President von der Leyen said today: 'Europe is in a fight. A fight for a continent that is whole and at peace.' We are being tested, we are being challenged and we are under attack. The military build-up on our borders is constantly being supplemented by hybrid threats and it has become a new normal. But we will never accept this as normal. So, honourable Members, I thank you for the opportunity to discuss this important topic today.

Our space-based services, including satellite navigation, are world class: Galileo, Copernicus and, soon, IRIS脗虏. Positioning, navigation and timing (PNT) services have long been an engine for economic growth and are now routinely integrated into modern infrastructure. They support a wide array of sectors including aviation, maritime, land-based applications and critical infrastructure.

Global satellite navigation systems (GNSS), such as the European Union's Galileo and EGNOS, or the American GPS, are the backbone of existing PNT services. They provide highly accurate, reliable, worldwide and free PNT services unmatched today by any other technology. But they're not immune to radio frequency interference. Neither the European Galileo nor the United States GPS are immune. Deliberate international interference into navigation systems most commonly occurs as jamming, which aims to blind satellite navigation signal receivers and prevent them from receiving navigation signals, thus preventing determination of position or timing and rendering the service unavailable.

More concerning is spoofing, which aims to deceive navigation signal receivers with counterfeit satellite signals. They appear as a normal service to most GNSS signal receivers.脗聽Spoofing is extremely dangerous, especially in safety applications such as aircraft navigation. Jamming and spoofing attacks on our satellite navigation systems severely affect transport by air, sea and by road. They also impact farming and other sectors.

Today, 40脗聽% of European air traffic operates in regions strongly affected by radio frequency interference. 40脗聽% 芒聙聯 four, zero. Since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, interference has worsened, a shift not only in quantity but also in quality, with sophistication of jamming and spoofing attacks increasing, posing big risks both in the air and at sea.

The situation is especially grim on the EU's eastern flank. Just over the past year in the Baltic, there has been a five-fold increase in flights affected by radio frequency interference. Just in August this year, the authorities of the country which I know the best (Lithuania) registered 1脗聽000 jamming disruptions of navigation signals for aircrafts.

But this daily interference goes well beyond the Baltic 芒聙聯 it affects all Member States in the east, especially areas close to the Ukrainian border and the Black Sea and our neighbours close to the war: the eastern Mediterranean, Black Sea, Middle East and Arctic. Against this growing danger, the European Commission has taken three main lines of action.

Firstly, at the international political level, to address and condemn such acts, the Commission supports the Member States in key international fora: the International Maritime Organisation, the International Telecommunications Union and the International Civil Aviation Organisation. Radio frequency interference of our space systems will be one of the most important topics later this month at the Assembly of the International Civil Aviation Organisation.

But defending ourselves in international fora is not enough 芒聙聯 we need a coordinated EU action plan to protect our interests. This was high on the agenda of the June Transport and Telecommunications Council and several Member States sent a letter to the Commission calling for common action against jamming and spoofing threats.

That is why our second line of action is reinforcing the resilience and robustness of our EU space infrastructure. The first item which I want to stress is that since July this year, our Galileo satellite system protects millions of people who receive messages with a new and unique capability. Galileo Open Service Navigation Message Authentication is the first such service offered worldwide, which detects spoofing attacks and alerts users. It protects against spoofing attacks by certifying the authenticity of the Galileo signal.

Second, from 2027, we shall offer even better protection against an even broader set of spoofing threats with the Galileo Signal Authentication Service. Third, now more than ever, providing an encrypted and secure positioning, navigation and timing service is critical for our governmental users and is a real game changer in modern warfare. That is why, by the end of this year, Galileo will feature a Public Regulated Service with encrypted navigation signals to protect the signals for governmental entities. It will be far more robust than the standard open satellite navigation signals.

Fourth, in the short term, starting from 2026芒聙聯2027, the European Commission is preparing a radio frequency interference monitoring service capable of detecting and localising GNSS interference that targets aircraft, ships and critical infrastructure. Future satellites will carry auxiliary payloads, allowing continuous monitoring and early detection of interference.

At the same time, we are anticipating future threats. We aim to increase the availability, resilience and robustness of Galileo services. That is why in the mid-term, a set of complementary measures will be undertaken in close coordination with Member States, including a constellation of satellites in low Earth orbit (LEO PNT) which, together with the existing Galileo infrastructure, 27 satellites in medium Earth orbit, will create a multi-orbit constellation capable of offering increased resilience along with additional fallback capabilities for continuous time and position determination.

Thirdly, we are addressing interference operationally. Our EU Aviation Safety Agency has published recommendations. If the situation deteriorates, the agency will take further actions to ensure safety. The EUROCONTROL network manager is defining the infrastructure needed for safety and service continuity 芒聙聯 both civilian and military 芒聙聯 and they organised a crisis exercise with national authorities to help prepare for the event of a wide-scale attack on the European Union.

We are working also on alternative systems and on components like active antennas and receivers that increase aviation robustness and strengthen resilience to jamming. We also welcome further development of national terrestrial capabilities as backup in case services are disrupted.

The Commission is preparing an action plan for aviation together with Member States, EUROCONTROL, the European Aviation Safety Agency and industry, and to address interference at sea the Commission has set up an expert group, together with Member States and the European Maritime Safety Agency, to work out reporting and sharing of incidents via EU-wide maritime surveillance systems. Our main goal at sea and in the air: to ensure safety and limit impact on travel and the economy.

Honourable Members 芒聙聯 we are engaged in a difficult struggle against a dangerous threat. To win the battle for resilience we need full interinstitutional cooperation, cooperation with the Member States and full support for the European Competitiveness Fund and the new space systems and services in the next Multiannual Financial Framework. We count on the European Parliament to support an ambitious agenda so that technical, operational and legislative tools, including ambitious funding, are fully in place as soon as possible to end this rising threat to our economy and way of life.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dariusz Jo脜聞ski, w imieniu grupy PPE. 芒聙聯 Szanowna Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo! Panie Komisarzu! To mia脜聜o by脛聡 wyst脛聟pienie o nawigacji satelitarnej, ale 10 wrze脜聸nia 2025 r. oko脜聜o p脙鲁脜聜nocy do Polski wlecia脜聜o 19 rosyjskich dron脙鲁w. I nie tylko znalaz脜聜y si脛聶 przy wschodniej granicy, ale r脙鲁wnie脜录 dolecia脜聜y do Polski centralnej. I dzisiaj musz脛聶 m脙鲁wi脛聡 o solidarno脜聸ci i o wsp脙鲁lnej obronie, kt脙鲁rej nowoczesna nawigacja jest, ale te脜录 b脛聶dzie, integraln脛聟 cz脛聶脜聸ci脛聟.

Tak jak powiedzia脜聜em, kilkana脜聸cie rosyjskich dron脙鲁w naruszy脜聜o dzi脜聸 w nocy polsk脛聟 przestrze脜聞 powietrzn脛聟. I wszyscy, absolutnie wszyscy wiemy, 脜录e nie by脜聜 to przypadek, ale to by脜聜a po prostu prowokacja. Polska zareagowa脜聜a stanowczo i adekwatnie. Nie by脜聜a to reakcja jednego pa脜聞stwa. Odpowiedzieli脜聸my w imieniu solidarnej Europy, bo nasze granice, NATO i Unii Europejskiej, to s脛聟 w脜聜a脜聸nie nasze granice. A je脜聸li one s脛聟 testowane, to testowani jeste脜聸my wszyscy.

I dlatego z tego miejsca chcia脜聜bym podzi脛聶kowa脛聡 Europie, Unii Europejskiej, Komisji Europejskiej za prawie 180 mld z脜聜otych, 44 mld euro, kt脙鲁re zostan脛聟 zainwestowane z programu SAFE w Polsce na obron脛聶 Polski i na obron脛聶 Europy. Te 脜聸rodki oczywi脜聸cie wzmocni脛聟 nasz脛聟 obron脛聶 powietrzn脛聟 i cyfrow脛聟, ale bezpiecze脜聞stwo to nie tylko rakiety i radary. To tak脜录e odporno脜聸脛聡 satelitarna i precyzyjna nawigacja. Chc脛聶 dzi脜聸 bardzo jasno powiedzie脛聡, 脜录e ta noc, kt脙鲁ra min脛聶脜聜a, to by脜聜 jasny sygna脜聜 dla Europy. I tylko jedno脜聸脛聡 mo脜录e nas obroni脛聡 w Europie. Polska granica to granica Europy. Bezpieczna Polska to bezpieczna Europa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Johan Danielsson, f脙露r S&D gruppen. 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Kommission脙陇ren. Under den senaste tiden har antalet allvarliga GPS-st脙露rningar 脙露kat lavinartat. Oavsett om det handlar om ambulanshelikoptrar p脙楼 Gotland eller kommissionsordf脙露randens plan.脗聽Ett hundra tjugotre tusen flyg har st脙露rts av signaler bara i 脙聳stersj脙露omr脙楼det under 脙楼rets fyra f脙露rsta m脙楼nader. Det h脙陇r 脙陇r inte planl脙露st. Det 脙陇r en tydlig del av rysk hybridkrigf脙露ring riktat mot Europa,脗聽och varje st脙露rning f脙露r oss n脙陇rmare en allvarlig olycka.

EU m脙楼ste ta detta p脙楼 st脙露rsta allvar och inf脙露ra samlade 脙楼tg脙陇rder f脙露r att motverka dessa angrepp. Vi beh脙露ver st脙陇rka v脙楼r tekniska kapacitet. Vi beh脙露ver inf脙露ra h脙楼rdare sanktioner mot de akt脙露rer som st脙楼r bakom detta och strypa deras tillg脙楼ng till v脙楼ra satellit- och navigeringstj脙陇nster. Det handlar om v脙楼r s脙陇kerhet. Det handlar om v脙楼r frihet.脗聽Kommissionen kan f脙露rv脙陇nta sig ett brett st脙露d fr脙楼n parlamentet med de 脙楼tg脙陇rder vi beh脙露ver ta tillsammans i Europa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kosma Z脜聜otowski, w imieniu grupy ECR. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Panie Komisarzu! Polska od miesi脛聶cy alarmuje o zak脜聜脙鲁ceniach sygna脜聜u GPS nad Morzem Ba脜聜tyckim, o rosn脛聟cym zagro脜录eniu dla 脜录eglugi i lotnictwa cywilnego 芒聙聯 tak脜录e w okolicach mojego miasta, Bydgoszczy 芒聙聯 i o konieczno脜聸ci rozwoju system脙鲁w monitoringu i obrony.

Dzisiejsze wydarzenia to kolejna forma rosyjskiej agresji. Moskwa naruszy脜聜a przestrze脜聞 powietrzn脛聟 mojego kraju. Kilkadziesi脛聟t dron脙鲁w dotar脜聜o nad terytorium Polski. Cz脛聶脜聸脛聡 zosta脜聜a zestrzelona przez wojsko. Gratuluj脛聶 naszemu dow脙鲁dztwu i 脜录o脜聜nierzom zdecydowania oraz skuteczno脜聸ci.

Rosja staje si脛聶 coraz bardziej bezczelna i agresywna i przekracza kolejne granice. To ju脜录 nie tylko cyberataki, dezinformacja czy zak脜聜脙鲁canie sygna脜聜u. To otwarte, fizyczne zagro脜录enie dla bezpiecze脜聞stwa Unii i NATO. B脛聟d脜潞my solidarni, nie lekcewa脜录my zagro脜录enia, wzmacniajmy odporno脜聸脛聡 i odpowiadajmy stanowczo. Rosja rozumie tylko taki j脛聶zyk, jakim sama do 脜聸wiata przemawia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jan-Christoph Oetjen, im Namen der Renew-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Kommissar, verehrte Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Die Luftfahrt ist ein strategischer Sektor in Europa, ist eines der R脙录ckgrate unserer Wirtschaft. Viele Menschen fliegen, verbinden St脙陇dte in Europa miteinander, und seit 2018/2019 kommt es immer h脙陇ufiger dazu, dass Navigationsdaten gest脙露rt werden脗聽芒聙聯 Navigationsdaten gest脙露rt werden durch einfache Versuche, die Radiosignale zu st脙露ren.

Aber jetzt wird es immer schlimmer, denn insbesondere Russland versucht, in hybrider Kriegsf脙录hrung gezielt Flugzeuge vom Kurs abzubringen; versucht gezielt, Flugzeugen eine andere Navigationsposition vorzugaukeln. Das nennt man Spoofing.

Ich finde es sehr gut, Herr Kommissar, dass Sie anerkennen, dass das ein wachsendes Problem ist und dass wir etwas tun m脙录ssen, dass wir als Europ脙陇er investieren m脙录ssen darin, uns zu verteidigen, um die Luftfahrt als strategischen Sektor zu sch脙录tzen, aber auch, weil viele Menschen davon betroffen sind. Das ist versuchter gezielter Massenmord von Russland an europ脙陇ischen B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rgern, und dem etwas entgegenzuhalten, ist wichtig!

Da m脙录ssen wir in Forschung investieren. Ich finde es gut, dass Sie es angesprochen haben: N脙陇chste Woche ist die Versammlung der ICAO in Montreal, da gibt es eine Delegation aus dem Europ脙陇ischen Parlament. Wir m脙录ssen dieses Thema dort ansprechen, damit wir internationale Regeln bekommen und Russland deutlich machen: Diese hybride Kriegsf脙录hrung d脙录rfen wir nicht zulassen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sergey Lagodinsky, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, colleagues, just weeks ago, when this incident happened, we must be very clear, this is not an isolated incident. And some of the numbers were already mentioned. In Estonia, 85 % of flights have been affected. Lithuania, in your country, as you mentioned, saw a 22-fold increase in jamming. Latvia recorded 820 disruptions last year, whereas the year before it was just 26.

And according to my information, the drones that were in the sky above Poland were manipulated with SIM cards from European countries. Those were the SIM cards from Lithuania and Poland, so that they can use the European system in order to fly.

So let's see clearly, Russia is behind much of this 芒聙聭 GNSS jamming and spoofing are what they are. It's weapons of hybrid war, and they strike into the heart of our Europe's infrastructure.

That's why we must act. And we will act the Space Act with secure, binding security, cybersecurity rules, and also the Quantum Act with 97 % accurate quality detection systems that will allow us to build a digital defence shield.

So this is about resilience. This is about responsibility. This is about us acting urgently. And yes, we can also in this Parliament.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Merja Kyll脙露nen, The Left-ryhm脙陇n puolesta. 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, arvoisa komission j脙陇sen, GPS-h脙陇irint脙陇 tappaa turvallisuuden. Turvallisuus on lento- ja meriliikenteen kriittist脙陇 ydint脙陇.

Ongelma on ollut edess脙陇mme jo vuosia. Me olemme valitettavasti aivan liian hitaita ja sinisilm脙陇isi脙陇 reagoinnissamme ulkoisten valtojen vaikuttamiseen. Olen huolissani siit脙陇, ett脙陇 siin脙陇 miss脙陇 moninainen eurooppalainen j脙陇rjestelm脙陇mme turvaa kyll脙陇 demokratian toteutumisen, osassa asioita me tarvitsisimme v脙陇hemm脙陇n byrokratiaa ja komiteoita ja enemm脙陇n nopeampaa toimintaa. Meid脙陇n on kyett脙陇v脙陇 vahvempiin ja nopeampiin muutoksiin kaikkien j脙陇senvaltioiden kanssa. Se on ehdoton elinehto koko Euroopalle, jotta me selviydymme t脙陇st脙陇 taistelusta.

Kiitoksia komissiolle toimista, mutta toivon, ett脙陇 me pystymme koordinoimaan jatkossa paremmin, jotta me saamme nopeammin asiat liikkeelle.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paulius Saudargas (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Commissioner Andrius Kubilius, dear colleagues, countries bordering Russia have been facing hybrid warfare, including GPS disruptions, for years. A major incident such as the spoofing of the plane carrying Madam President Ursula von der Leyen and you, dear Commissioner, was unfortunately only a matter of time.

Russia is developing technologies not only to target specific regions of the European Union, but to threaten Europe as a whole. The number of spoofing and jamming sources continues to rise. For example, in 2025, the number of spoofing sources in the Kaliningrad region alone, bordering Lithuania, increased from 5 to 29.

This phenomenon increasingly extends beyond the aviation sector. The maritime sector is affected, with ships forced to bypass seaports because their signals are jammed. Farmers near the border struggle to cultivate their land due to interference that disrupts connected tools such as drainage systems. Even private devices and civilian applications are being impacted.

Most importantly, Russia remains largely unaccountable before the international community. Actors who disregard international rules and pose a direct threat to the safety of international transport must be sanctioned. Our response must be firm and coordinated: strong sanctions and investment in resilient technologies.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fran脙搂ois Kalfon (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, cette nuit, ce sont 19脗聽drones qui ont touch脙漏 le territoire de l'Union europ脙漏enne en Pologne. Fin ao脙禄t, l'avion d'Ursula脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen a 脙漏t脙漏 la cible d'un brouillage GPS au-dessus de la Bulgarie, le for脙搂ant 脙聽 atterrir en urgence. Ces attaques s'inscrivent dans la guerre que m脙篓ne la Russie contre l'Europe. Dans cette guerre, les fantassins sont des hackers, les chars d'assaut sont des drones et les bombes sont informatiques. En 2024, les cas de brouillages du syst脙篓me de navigation par satellite GNSS ont augment脙漏 de 500脗聽% 脙聽 l'脙漏chelle mondiale. Ce sont 46脗聽000脗聽vols commerciaux qui ont 脙漏t脙漏 touch脙漏s, faisant courir un risque 脙聽 4脗聽millions de passagers.

Mais l'Europe est capable de se d脙漏fendre. D'abord, avec le bouclier num脙漏rique autour de Galileo, qui garantit la fiabilit脙漏 des signaux GPS. Ensuite, avec le gardien EGNOS, qui alerte les cibles, en quelques secondes, d'une attaque. Enfin, gr脙垄ce 脙聽 la constellation europ脙漏enne IRIS2, nous disposerons de signaux chiffr脙漏s et prot脙漏g脙漏s r脙漏ellement de mani脙篓re souveraine.

Alors je le dis, Vladimir脗聽Poutine doit savoir que l'Europe est d脙漏j脙聽 en mesure d'apporter les bonnes r脙漏ponses face 脙聽 ces agressions devenues incessantes.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Elena Donazzan (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, credo che sia molto opportuno che, al di l脙聽 dei fatti che accadono oggi per cui va la nostra vicinanza alla Polonia, ma pu脙虏 accadere dappertutto, noi si stia iniziando da qualche giorno a discutere lo Space Act.

Ha molto a che fare con il tema della sicurezza; ha molto a che fare con la sovranit脙聽 dell'Europa nella gestione dei propri dati e dei propri sistemi di controllo, della produzione, che deve vederci capaci di recuperare un divario che c'脙篓 tra i maggiori produttori al mondo.

Perch脙漏 senza il controllo anche della produzione, senza il rafforzamento delle nostre grandi industrie di Stato 芒聙聯 e ce ne sono e di grande valore 芒聙聯 insieme a una supply chain che deve essere costruita e rafforzata anche con l'aiuto dei fondi europei, la nostra capacit脙聽 di avere il controllo della componentistica, il controllo dei dati e il controllo dei sistemi sarebbe inadeguata.

Oggi il tema della sicurezza riguarda la componente civile e militare, ma forse un unico grande ambiente: 脙篓 quello che noi dobbiamo provare a difendere e tutelare, comprendendo che la prima questione dello spazio 脙篓 riconoscerlo come dominio.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Christophe Grudler (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, chaque jour, des dizaines d'avions subissent des brouillages de la part de la Russie. De la Finlande 脙聽 la France en passant par la Bulgarie, c'est une guerre des ondes, invisible, mais bien r脙漏elle. Une tentative d'intimidation qui vise 脙聽 nous aveugler dans notre propre ciel.

Ce n'est pas seulement le GPS am脙漏ricain qui est brouill脙漏, c'est aussi notre GPS europ脙漏en Galileo, plus performant encore. Mais l'Europe ne pliera pas, tout comme elle ne doit pas plier face aux intimidations des drones russes, ce matin, en Pologne. Nous avons une solution pour nous prot脙漏ger, c'est le service public r脙漏glement脙漏 (PRS) de Galileo, con脙搂u pour r脙漏sister aux brouillages.

Monsieur le Commissaire, il est regrettable que ce service par satellite, plus r脙漏sistant, ne soit pas encore totalement d脙漏ploy脙漏, alors que le syst脙篓me initial du PRS est en place depuis 2016. Je compte sur votre engagement pour acc脙漏l脙漏rer ce PRS. Acc脙漏l脙漏rons pour garantir notre ind脙漏pendance sur les ondes et notre souverainet脙漏 dans les airs.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Se脙隆n Kelly (PPE). 芒聙聯 A Uachtar脙隆in, Commissioner, the number of jamming and spoofing incidents targeting global navigation satellite systems has risen at an alarming rate, with an estimated 140脗聽000 flights affected in 2024 alone.

This is a real threat and it is one that has already cost lives: tragically, 38 people perished on an Azerbaijani Airlines flight in December, and there have been several near misses since. Sadly, these incidents are likely to continue, particularly with the increase in the use of hybrid warfare tactics deployed by Russia and other hostile actors.脗聽This is a direct attack on Europe's safety, on our connectivity and our strategic autonomy.

The satellite constellations underpinning the GNSS are essential to aviation, shipping, energy, agriculture and the smooth functioning of our entire economy. That is why Europe must act to resolve. And I welcome the Commission's statement of intent in this regard this evening, particularly investing in our Galileo system.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jos脙漏 Cepeda (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, comisario Kubilius, mis primeras palabras quieren ser de apoyo, de solidaridad y de fuerza. Y tambi脙漏n, por qu脙漏 no decirlo, de un sentimiento de resiliencia que Europa tiene que tener, justo en este momento, con todo el pueblo polaco. Me parece que es muy importante que sepan que no nos van a doblegar y que no nos van a faltar esfuerzos, recursos ni ganas para defender la paz en el territorio europeo, y desde luego en Polonia, porque est脙隆 sufriendo estos devaneos por parte de algunos que piensan que los van a desestabilizar.

Buena parte del discurso de esta tarde tiene mucho que ver con el trabajo que se est脙隆 desarrollando; el se脙卤or Kubilius ya ha puesto encima de la mesa algunas medidas, pero creo que es muy importante remarcar que nos hacen falta, fundamentalmente, dos elementos: primero, tener infraestructuras propias 芒聙聰por ejemplo, no tenemos un plan satelital militar propio europeo y ser脙颅a muy importante desarrollarlo芒聙聰 y, segundo, tener un sistema de inteligencia robusto, netamente europeo, para proteger a los Veintisiete. Y en eso vamos a seguir trabajando intensamente durante los pr脙鲁ximos meses.

脗聽脗聽S脙漏rgio Humberto (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, caro Comiss脙隆rio, caros colegas, a rede transeuropeia de transportes s脙鲁 ser脙隆 cred脙颅vel se estiver preparada para resistir a este tipo de novos ataques. Para isso, claro que sim, precisamos de sistemas de navega脙搂脙拢o alternativos e de protocolos de resposta mais r脙隆pida.

Mas n脙拢o chega agir apenas dentro das fronteiras da Uni脙拢o 芒聙聰 os c脙漏us e os mares n脙拢o conhecem fronteiras pol脙颅ticas. Devemos cooperar com os nossos vizinhos e parceiros, sobretudo no Mediterr脙垄neo, no Atl脙垄ntico e no mar Negro, para manter as rotas internacionais seguras e abertas.

A Uni脙拢o Europeia tem aqui uma oportunidade clara de liderar na prote脙搂脙拢o da avia脙搂脙拢o e do transporte mar脙颅timo e de refor脙搂ar a credibilidade, a competitividade e a prote脙搂脙拢o. 脙聣 nossa responsabilidade garantir que voar e navegar na Europa continua a ser sin脙鲁nimo de confian脙搂a e seguran脙搂a.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, comisario Kubilius, esta ma脙卤ana, al conocer la noticia de la flagrante violaci脙鲁n del espacio a脙漏reo polaco 芒聙聰ergo europeo芒聙聰 por parte de drones rusos, escuchamos a la presidenta Von der Leyen en el debate del Estado de la Uni脙鲁n hacer una apuesta muy concreta por la industria de defensa europea.

S脙颅, sabemos que no ha existido en la historia un motor m脙隆s eficaz de unidad federal que la defensa, porque la ense脙卤anza es clara; en ning脙潞n caso vamos a ser m脙隆s fuertes sumando los esfuerzos fragmentados de los 27 Estados miembros, pero tampoco detrayendo recursos de las pol脙颅ticas distintivas de la Uni脙鲁n Europea: cohesi脙鲁n, solidaridad y pol脙颅tica regional.

Lo que se quiere decir, en este debate, es que claro que hay que apoyar ese refuerzo frente a las interferencias en la navegaci脙鲁n a脙漏rea 芒聙聰el sistema Galileo芒聙聰, pero, fundamentalmente, hay que apoyar que 芒聙聰para que tengamos una unidad de fuerza com脙潞n芒聙聰 es imprescindible tener inteligencia com脙潞n que conduzca a la acci脙鲁n com脙潞n, no solamente de los Estados miembros, sino del conjunto de la Uni脙鲁n, con valor a脙卤adido europeo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam President, the recent news of GPS jamming along our eastern borders is extremely alarming, but it is not a surprise. The recent suspected targeting of the aircraft of the President of the Commission demonstrates that Putin does not care who is on the receiving end.

While Russia has been conducting its barbaric war in Ukraine, hybrid operations have also been detected across Europe. Anyone can be a target, and the message is clear: we must be prepared for every possible scenario. It is imperative that we work together with our allies to guarantee safe passage for both civilian and military air traffic. Failure to do so could have catastrophic consequences, and I urge Europe to act decisively.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helmut Brandst脙陇tter (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Wir sprechen hier oft 脙录ber die verschiedenen Formen des hybriden Krieges. Heute haben wir 脙录ber eine besonders gef脙陇hrliche Form des hybriden Krieges, die Putin gegen uns in Europa f脙录hrt, gesprochen. Ich sage das deswegen so deutlich, weil mir viele Menschen in 脙聳sterreich sagen: Wir sind neutral; wenn wir uns nur ruhig verhalten, dann kann uns nichts passieren.

Die heutige Debatte hat gezeigt, dass auch allen Menschen in 脙聳sterreich, die in ein Flugzeug einsteigen, nat脙录rlich etwas passieren kann, weil diese Beeinflussung der Flugdaten jederzeit so gef脙陇hrlich werden kann, dass es zu einem Flugzeugabsturz kommen kann脗聽芒聙聯 und ich m脙露chte, dass das auch meine Landsleute verstehen. Und das in der Kombination: Das Flugzeug von Frau von der Leyen wurde gest脙露rt, und nachher ging auch die Desinformationskampagne los, dass alle, die das sagen, dass das nat脙录rlich 脙录berhaupt nicht stimmen kann.

Umso wichtiger脗聽芒聙聯 dar脙录ber bin ich sehr froh脗聽芒聙聯, dass wir einen Verteidigungs-Kommissar haben脗聽芒聙聯脗聽Herr Kommissar Kubilius, danke sch脙露n脗聽芒聙聯 und dass wir jetzt und dass Sie auch daf脙录r sorgen, dass dieses Galileo-Navigationssystem so sicher wird, dass wir die Menschen in Europa besch脙录tzen k脙露nnen脗聽芒聙聯 auch die Menschen in 脙聳sterreich.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Magdalena Adamowicz (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Dzi脜聸 w cieniu naruszenia polskiej granicy, naszej europejskiej granicy przez dziesi脛聟tki rosyjskich dron脙鲁w zajmujemy si脛聶 kolejn脛聟 ods脜聜on脛聟 wojny hybrydowej, jak脛聟 Rosja toczy przeciwko Europie. Globalny system nawigacji satelitarnej jest kr脛聶gos脜聜upem naszej logistyki, naszego lotnictwa i naszej 脜录eglugi. Bez niego b脛聶dziemy 脜聸lepi. Zag脜聜uszanie GPS-u przez Rosj脛聶 to agresja w czystej postaci, zagra脜录aj脛聟ca 脜录yciu milion脙鲁w Europejczyk脙鲁w, kt脙鲁rych bezpiecze脜聞stwo w ka脜录dym momencie zale脜录y od tego systemu. Zastan脙鲁wmy si脛聶, co jeszcze bandyta z Kremla ma zrobi脛聡, zanim wszyscy jednoznacznie zrozumiemy, 脜录e tylko wybicie mu z脛聶b脙鲁w i totalne zbankrutowanie tego terrorystycznego re脜录imu przywr脙鲁ci nam spok脙鲁j i bezpiecze脜聞stwo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andrius Kubilius, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, colleagues, again, thanks a lot for this debate, and thank you very much for your determination, and unity in that determination, to defend our security. Today's debate and your contributions are an encouragement to take forward our EU response to the problems and challenges and threats of jamming and spoofing.

Our exchange of views calls for implementing operational and contingency measures in the short term and developing a strong and ambitious space programme to enhance the resilience of our space systems, the protection of transport services in the Member States, and help to face interference to our space systems, also at the International Civil Aviation Organization.

We really have solid EU space systems. The Commission will continue to deploy and operate them, for the benefit of all Member States and our economic operators. We shall adapt them to new threats and risks affecting the EU economy and the security and safety of our citizens. I count on your continued support.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Die Aussprache ist geschlossen.

17. Rule of law and EU funds management in Slovakia (debate)

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber die Erkl脙陇rungen des Rates und der Kommission zu der Rechtsstaatlichkeit und der Verwaltung von EU-Mitteln in der Slowakei (2025/2863(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, Commissioner, let me stress that our Union is a community of values with the rule of law at its heart. The respect for the rule of law is vital when implementing the EU budgets, and when respecting the principle of sound financial management.

The defence of our common values and the rule of law go hand in hand with the good management of EU funds. The Rule of Law Conditionality Regulation protects the EU budget: it allows us to take measures if the EU's financial interests are put at risk by a Member State.

We welcome the dialogue between the Commission and Slovakia. We hope these exchanges will soon bear fruit, together with the reforms already undertaken by Slovakia. Should the Commission decide to propose further action, the Council will carefully examine them.

At the same time, the Council is working to address challenges before they become actual problems. This takes place in the framework of the Council's rule of law dialogue, a key preventive tool.脗聽Let me give you concrete examples of upcoming discussions: next week, ministers will meet at the General Affairs Council for discussion on the general trends of the rule of law in the EU. The discussion will be based on the Commission report from July. Then, in November, we will continue with our review of the rule of law situation in the individual Member States, focusing this time on Bulgaria, Czechia, Ireland and Germany.

Let me assure you that the Council remains committed to the promotion of the rule of law. We will continue to pay close attention to the developments in this area.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael McGrath, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Minister, honourable Members, thank you very much for this evening's debate in relation to Slovakia. Let me start off by saying that, in the last two years, Slovakia has engaged in an intensive dialogue with the Commission to resolve issues identified in the 2024 Rule of Law report, and important efforts continue in this regard.

Engagement and constructive dialogue have allowed us to engage with Slovakia on significant reforms where important issues of compliance with EU law are discussed 芒聙聯 from criminal law reforms to current draft constitutional amendments, to name just a few. As one result of this dialogue, and as noted in this year's Rule of Law report, Slovakia realigned its 2024 criminal law reform to ensure consistency with the EU's legal framework and the protection of the Union's financial interests. This is a positive step.

However, the 2025 report also flags concerns regarding the reform's implications on addressing high-level corruption. Regarding Slovakia's institutional set-up to fight corruption, for instance, the closure of its specialised anti-corruption entities, the National Crime Agency (NACA) and the Special Prosecutor's Office (SPO), resulted in delays in investigations transferred to other bodies and in a drop in the number of corruption cases. For this reason, in July this year, the Commission concluded that no progress was made in the past year on its recommendation to ensure effective and independent investigation and prosecution of high-level corruption cases made in previous Rule of Law reports.

In relation to the prevention of corruption, the Commission considered there has been some progress to strengthen the legislation on conflicts of interest. At the same time, we also noted that lobbying and asset declarations have yet to be regulated in Slovakia.

As concerns the Recovery and Resilience Plan, Slovakia continues its implementation as planned. The country has so far received 61脗聽% of its envelope. With less than one year and a half left to bring the Recovery and Resilience Facility to a successful conclusion, it is important for Slovakia 芒聙聯 as it is for all Member States 芒聙聯 to deliver the remaining reforms and investments that have been agreed under that facility.

In relation to cohesion funds, programme implementation has gained speed while gradually remedying deficiencies that were originally identified. At the same time, some challenges in the area of the partnership principle 芒聙聯 one of the cornerstones of the cohesion policy 芒聙聯 do still remain. For the Commission, it is important that essential guarantees for civil society organisations are not weakened in the implementation of EU funds, both at national and regional levels. The Commission remains in contact with the Slovak authorities in this regard.

Concerning the implementation of EU funds in agriculture and rural development, the Commission is aware of recent media reports about alleged irregularities in the way common agricultural policy funds have been disbursed in Slovakia. The Commission is in contact with the Slovak authorities that put in place actions to address any irregularities.

The Commission has performed several audits in relation to the agricultural funds, resulting in an overall financial correction of EUR脗聽27.7脗聽million for the detected irregularities, which was accepted by Slovakia while collaborating effectively during the process. Any allegations of fraud or other irregularities of a criminal nature are in the hands of OLAF and the EPPO as independent bodies. The Commission continues to monitor issues reported by the certification body.

Finally, as regards the general regime of conditionality, the Commission continuously monitors the situation in all Member States, including Slovakia. The Commission assesses every situation on an individual basis and will act if the criteria set out in the Conditionality Regulation weren't to be met.

Honourable Members, our commitment is clear: to support Slovakia in upholding the rule of law, to safeguard public trust and to protect the Union's financial interests at all times. Our democracy, security and economy rely on the rule of law, and respect for the rule of law will continue to be a must for EU funds.

I know that this commitment is also widely shared in this House, as demonstrated by the fact芒聙聭finding mission of the Democracy, Rule of Law and Fundamental Rights Monitoring Group of the Committee on Civil Liberties that took place on 2 and 3脗聽June in Bratislava. I know we have quite a number of MEPs, and indeed members of that delegation, here this evening. I look forward now to hearing your contributions to this debate.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Javier Zarzalejos, en nombre del Grupo PPE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, el Estado de Derecho no es un asunto interno. En la Uni脙鲁n Europea el Estado de Derecho puede y debe ser objeto de preocupaci脙鲁n com脙潞n y de actuaci脙鲁n correctiva.

Por eso, al afirmar la legitimidad de la acci脙鲁n del Parlamento, quiero empezar condenando el acoso sufrido por nuestro colega Tom脙隆脜隆 Zdechovsk脙陆 durante su misi脙鲁n en Bratislava como miembro de la Comisi脙鲁n de Control Presupuestario.

Proteger la integridad del Estado de Derecho no es una injerencia, es una obligaci脙鲁n. Y los Tratados nos habilitan y nos obligan a ello. El Parlamento est脙隆 cumpliendo con su deber.

Se脙卤ora presidenta, estamos hablando de deslegitimaci脙鲁n de los jueces, de ataques a periodistas y medios de comunicaci脙鲁n, de presi脙鲁n sobre las organizaciones de la sociedad civil. Estamos hablando del desmantelamiento de unidades especializadas en la lucha contra la corrupci脙鲁n. Estamos hablando de reformas legislativas para favorecer a allegados pol脙颅ticos y personales, especialmente en el terreno de la corrupci脙鲁n. Estamos hablando del recurso sistem脙隆tico a procedimientos parlamentarios de urgencia, sin participaci脙鲁n de los sectores y sujetos interesados.

S脙颅, tal vez a alguno todo esto le suene familiar. No, estamos hablando, en este caso, de Eslovaquia, pero no creamos que esos son los 脙潞nicos desaf脙颅os y que ese cat脙隆logo de amenazas se restringe a un solo pa脙颅s. Por eso, tenemos que ampliar nuestro radar y elevar nuestras exigencias para que otras violaciones, otras amenazas y otros riesgos no escapen a los imperativos democr脙隆ticos que nos obligan a todos.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, este Parlamento, lo saben bien, ha hecho cuanto ha estado en su mano para establecer un ciclo pol脙颅tico con un marco vinculante de Estado de Derecho, democracia y derechos fundamentales que prevenga 芒聙聰y no solamente corrija cuando ya es tarde芒聙聰 las desviaciones con respecto del est脙隆ndar europeo, que es vinculante para todos los Estados miembros.

La Uni脙鲁n Europea no es solamente un mercado ni un club de intereses, sino tambi脙漏n valores y un modelo europeo de Estado de Derecho y de democracia que todos los Estados miembros tienen la obligaci脙鲁n de cumplir.

En un momento dado tuvimos que enfrentarnos con la deriva autocr脙隆tica del Gobierno de Orb脙隆n en Hungr脙颅a y luego vinieron los 脗芦orbanitas脗禄, que es como llamamos a los imitadores de Orb脙隆n: durante un tiempo tuvimos la cuesti脙鲁n polaca 芒聙聰tambi脙漏n sujeta al procedimiento del art脙颅culo 7 del Tratado de la Uni脙鲁n Europea芒聙聰 y ahora tenemos un desaf脙颅o con Eslovaquia.

El Gobierno de Eslovaquia se desv脙颅a notoriamente del est脙隆ndar europeo y lo desaf脙颅a, con un primer ministro que estrecha la mano de Putin y acude sol脙颅cito a entrevistarse con 脙漏l y con Xi, sin ninguna cobertura europea, sin representar ni los valores ni el est脙隆ndar europeo: desmantela la Fiscal脙颅a Anticorrupci脙鲁n, ataca a la prensa independiente, hace retroceder pavorosamente las libertades en Eslovaquia...

Es una cuesti脙鲁n en la que no solo tenemos la obligaci脙鲁n de velar, de vigilar, sino tambi脙漏n de advertir de que la condicionalidad del Estado de Derecho se ide脙鲁 exactamente para prevenir y bloquear el acceso a los fondos europeos y al presupuesto europeo a los Gobiernos que no cumplan con sus obligaciones, con los valores consagrados en el art脙颅culo 2 del Tratado de la Uni脙鲁n Europea.

Este es el momento de fijar la vista en Eslovaquia y de apoyar la misi脙鲁n que llev脙鲁 a cabo en ese pa脙颅s la Comisi脙鲁n de Libertades, Justicia e Interior, que sufri脙鲁, una vez m脙隆s 芒聙聰lo recuerdo bien porque yo fui parte de esas misiones en m脙隆s de una ocasi脙鲁n芒聙聰 un trato indigno para el Parlamento Europeo, que no es un ovni ni una injerencia exterior en un Estado miembro, sino una representaci脙鲁n democr脙隆tica directa de la ciudadan脙颅a eslovaca en la medida en que esa ciudadan脙颅a脗聽es tambi脙漏n europea.

脗聽脗聽Thierry Mariani, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, le respect de l'脙漏tat de droit, c'est g脙漏nial, franchement, parce que 脙搂a permet, d脙篓s qu'il y a un probl脙篓me avec un gouvernement qui d脙漏pla脙庐t 脙聽 l'Union europ脙漏enne, de s'ing脙漏rer dans ses affaires. 脙聡a a 脙漏t脙漏 le cas de la Pologne, 脙搂a a 脙漏t脙漏 le cas de la Hongrie, et c'est maintenant le tour de la Slovaquie d'脙陋tre le souffre-douleur de Bruxelles.

En r脙漏alit脙漏, la Commission harc脙篓le ce pays, car ce gouvernement vous d脙漏pla脙庐t. Selon toute logique, il faudrait, pour b脙漏n脙漏ficier des passe-droits europ脙漏ens, que les Slovaques fassent un peu comme les Polonais qui, depuis l'arriv脙漏e des lib脙漏raux au pouvoir, d脙漏tournent all脙篓grement, comme on l'apprend dans le journal Politico, les fonds europ脙漏ens consacr脙漏s 脙聽 la relance post-Covid pour acheter des yachts, des voitures de luxe et m脙陋me un club 脙漏changiste, sans aucune remarque de Bruxelles pour le moment.

Que dire de ce parlement qui a lui-m脙陋me 脙漏t脙漏 脙漏clabouss脙漏 par le scandale du Qatargate en mati脙篓re de corruption? O脙鹿 en est l'enqu脙陋te? O脙鹿 en sont les condamnations? N'en d脙漏plaise 脙聽 l'Union europ脙漏enne, le social-d脙漏mocrate Robert脗聽Fico est un Premier ministre d脙漏mocratiquement 脙漏lu. Son parti a remport脙漏 les 脙漏lections en 2023 et il conduit dans son pays une politique souverainiste.

Que Bruxelles remette de l'ordre dans ses propres d脙漏rives avant de donner des le脙搂ons aux 脙聣tats.

脗聽脗聽Paolo Inselvini, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, siamo consapevoli quanto sia importante lo Stato di diritto e il rispetto dei nostri valori fondamentali, ma questo solo quando non viene utilizzato come clava politica per colpire governi indigesti alla sinistra e al pensiero unico.

Questo 脙篓 accaduto prima con la Polonia, poi con l'Ungheria, a volte addirittura con la mia Italia, perch脙漏 spesso vengono criticati coloro che non si allineano al pensiero unico.

Noi, invece, vogliamo un'Europa di popoli liberi, che si possano autodeterminare; vogliamo un'Europa nel quale i popoli e i governi siano liberi di difendere valori tradizionali e normali senza essere ricattati.

Perch脙漏 noi vogliamo davvero un'Europa nel quale i governi siano eletti dalle persone, a differenza di una sinistra che spesso si riempie la bocca di democrazia ma non la rispetta per nulla.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michal Wiezik, za skupinu Renew. 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, dva pr脙颅klady vyu脜戮itia eurofondov na Slovensku:

N脙隆rodn脙陆 脙潞stav srdcov脙陆ch a cievnych ochoren脙颅 za 1,6 mili脙鲁na eur z pl脙隆nu obnovy vybavil dve opera脛聧n脙漏 s脙隆ly. Skr脙隆til t脙陆m z脙隆sadne d脛潞脜戮ku oper脙隆cie fibril脙隆cie srdcov脙陆ch chor脙麓b. M脙隆me 脜隆pi脛聧kovo vybaven脙漏 pracovisko, ktor脙漏 zachra脜聢uje desiatky a stovky 脛戮udsk脙陆ch 脜戮ivotov.

Na druhej strane, za t脙潞 ist脙潞 sumu si 脛戮udia spriaznen脙颅 s vl脙隆dnou stranou Smer postavili opulentn脙陆 z脙隆mo脛聧ek, v ktorom nikdy nikoho neubytovali a za p脙隆r rokov sa stal ich s脙潞kromn脙陆m majetkom.

C脙颅tite ten rozdiel? Je z脙隆sadn脙陆. Ten prv脙陆 pr脙颅stup je prospe脜隆n脙陆, pos脙潞va Slovensko vpred a pom脙隆ha 脛戮u脛聫om zlep脜隆i脜楼 脜戮ivot na Slovensku. Ten druh脙陆 je zneu脜戮it脙颅m eur脙鲁pskych financi脙颅. Ober脙隆 n脙隆s o pr脙颅le脜戮itos脜楼 napredova脜楼.

Slovensko m脙隆 by脜楼 脙潞spe脜隆nou krajinou. Eur脙鲁pska 脙潞nia n脙隆m v tom pom脙隆ha a 脜戮iadna vl脙隆da, 脜戮iadna vl脙隆da nem脙隆 pr脙隆vo n脙隆s o t脙潞to pr脙颅le脜戮itos脜楼 obera脜楼.

Preto je dne脜隆n脙隆 debata, preto bola kontroln脙隆 misia, preto tu stoj脙颅me, kri脛聧脙颅me a upozor脜聢ujeme na tento probl脙漏m. Rob脙颅me tak v najlep脜隆om z脙隆ujme Slovenska. Bojujeme a budeme bojova脜楼 za to, aby sa peniaze dostali k 脛戮u脛聫om a do regi脙鲁nov na Slovensku, aby Slovensko mohlo 脛聫alej napredova脜楼.

脗聽脗聽Daniel Freund, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, what a picture: the dictators of the world together in China. You have Putin, you have Xi Jinping, you have Kim Jong Un, and you have Robert Fico hanging out with them, and I think it tells you something about where he wants to go with Slovakia. And indeed, we have seen the attacks on media freedom, on the rule of law, the corruption. I was part of both missions that went to Slovakia in June. We learned that the anti-corruption prosecutor abolished the national crime agency 芒聙聯 abolished. We heard on the ground what this does. Hundreds of people that have been convicted for corruption have been let out of prison, dozens of investigations have been interrupted or stopped. We have seen that since the new criminal code entered into force, the number of indictments for corruption cases has dropped by 95%.

So, Commissioner, I fail to understand how we are not using the conditionality. EU funds are clearly at risk. There are dozens upon dozens of examples of villas for Fico's friends and allies. Why are we not doing something before it becomes too late, before we see a second Hungary? Let's not make the same mistakes. Let's act on this now.

脗聽脗聽Milan Uhr脙颅k, za skupinu ESN. 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, mil脙颅 progres脙颅vci, chcete v europarlamente op脙陇脜楼 na Slovensko kyda脜楼 a rozpr脙隆va脜楼 sa o poru脜隆ovan脙颅 pr脙隆vneho 脜隆t脙隆tu? Dobre, po脛聫me sa na to pozrie脜楼.

V prvom rade sa nie je s k脙陆m rozpr脙隆va脜楼, lebo tu nikto nesed脙颅 a tieto va脜隆e vymyslen脙漏 t脙漏my nikoho v Eur脙鲁pskom parlamente re脙隆lne nezauj脙颅maj脙潞. Je to tu pr脙隆zdne.

Po druh脙漏, povedzte mi, kde ste boli, ke脛聫 sa za minulej vl脙隆dy poru脜隆ovali na Slovensku 脛戮udsk脙漏 pr脙隆va, ke脛聫 sa strie脛戮alo po 脛戮u脛聫och s gumov脙陆mi projektilmi? Povedzte mi, kde ste boli, ke脛聫 sa zakazovali m脙漏di脙隆? Nikoho to nezauj脙颅ma. Samozrejme, 脜戮e tu nikto nesed脙颅, lebo je to hl脙潞pos脜楼 a nezmysel.

Ke脛聫 sa zakazovali m脙漏di脙隆 a ke脛聫 sa vyp脙颅nali webstr脙隆nky, vtedy ste boli ticho. Vtedy v脙隆m nevadilo ani rozkr脙隆danie eurofondov, ani to, 脜戮e 脜聽ime脛聧ka tuneluje s celou rodinou dot脙隆cie. Vtedy ste boli ticho.脗聽Zrazu ste sa prebudili a hovor脙颅te, 脜戮e na Slovensku je poru脜隆ovan脙陆 pr脙隆vny 脜隆t脙隆t len preto, 脜戮e vy nie ste na Slovensku pri moci.

Ja by som si 脜戮elal len dve veci: keby ste kone脛聧ne prestali na Slovensko v Eur脙鲁pe kyda脜楼 a keby sa do slovensk脙陆ch z脙隆le脜戮itost脙颅 prestali stara脜楼 脛戮udia ako p脙隆n Zdechovsk脙陆.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I will be a bit stricter now, after the round of speakers for the groups.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tom脙隆脜隆 Zdechovsk脙陆 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, I will be also strict.

Pan脙颅 p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, dovolte mi za脛聧脙颅t trochu osobn脛聸. Moje korene ved脙潞 aj na Slovensko. Star脙陆 otec poch脙隆zel z Tren脛聧脙颅na a 脛聧脙隆st moj脙颅 rodiny dodnes 脜戮ije na Slovensku. Ka脜戮d脙陆 rok se sem vrac脙颅m a c脙颅t脙颅m se tady doma. Proto to, co v脙隆m chci dnes 脜聶脙颅ci, nen脙颅 jen politick脙陆 projev poslance EP, ale vyzn脙隆n脙颅 脛聧lov脛聸ka, kter脙漏mu na Slovensku z脙隆le脜戮脙颅. Pamatuji si dobu, kdy my 脛聦e脜隆i jsme vzhl脙颅脜戮eli ke Slovensku s obdivem, kdy脜戮 jste p脜聶ijali euro, kdy脜戮 va脜隆e ekonomika rostla a kdy脜戮 jste ukazovali, 脜戮e i men脜隆脙颅 zem脛聸 m脜炉脜戮e b脙陆t p脜聶脙颅kladem modern脙颅 a ekonomicky vysp脛聸l脙漏 otev脜聶en脙漏 spole脛聧nosti. O to bolestn脛聸j脜隆脙颅 je dnes sledovat, kam se Slovensko posouv脙隆.

Miliardy z evropsk脙陆ch fond脜炉 miz脙颅 v projektech, kter脙漏 neslou脜戮脙颅 slovensk脙陆m lidem, ale nezodpov脛聸dn脙陆m politik脜炉m a jejich k脙隆mo脜隆脜炉m. Kontroln脙颅 org脙隆ny, kter脙漏 maj脙颅 st脙隆t na stran脛聸 poctiv脙陆ch, jsou paralyzov脙隆ny. Tam, kde kdysi padaly stovky 脜戮alob, dnes padaj脙颅 sotva dv脛聸 ro脛聧n脛聸. B脛聸hem kontroln脙颅 mise jsme si vyslechli p脜聶脙颅b脛聸hy farm脙隆脜聶脜炉, kte脜聶脙颅 byli vyd脙颅r脙隆ni, oznamovatel脜炉, kte脜聶脙颅 脛聧el脙颅 zastra脜隆ov脙隆n脙颅, i novin脙隆脜聶脜炉, kte脜聶脙颅 riskuj脙颅 脜戮ivot, proto脜戮e p脙颅脜隆脙颅 pravdu. I j脙隆 osobn脛聸 jsem 脛聧elil zastra脜隆ov脙隆n脙颅 a moje rodina v脙陆hr脜炉脜戮k脙隆m smrt脙颅. To jen potvrzuje, 脜戮e probl脙漏m je skute脛聧n脛聸 v脙隆脜戮n脙陆 a syst脙漏mov脙陆. Po n脙隆v脜隆t脛聸v脛聸 jsme p脜聶edali p脜聶es t脜聶i sta podn脛聸t脜炉 Evropsk脙漏 komisi a pevn脛聸 v脛聸脜聶脙颅m, 脜戮e tyto podn脛聸ty budou vy脜隆et脜聶eny.

脗聽脗聽Erik Kali脜聢脙隆k (NI), ot脙隆zka polo脜戮en脙隆 zdvihnut脙颅m modrej karty. 芒聙聯 P脙隆n Zdechovsk脙陆, boli ste s脙潞脛聧as脜楼ou tzv. fact-finding mission, preto sa v脙隆s mus脙颅m op脙陆ta脜楼: pre脛聧o va脜隆a spr脙隆va je pln脙隆 l脜戮脙颅?

A vyberiem si jednu konkr脙漏tnu, ktor脙隆 je za m脜聢a absol脙潞tne z脙隆va脜戮n脙隆: pre脛聧o tvrd脙颅te, 脜戮e Slovensko nevyu脜戮脙颅va syst脙漏m kontroly Arachne, napriek tomu, 脜戮e ve脛戮mi dobre viete, 脜戮e Slovensko je jedn脙陆m z prv脙陆ch 脜隆t脙隆tov, ktor脙陆 t脙潞to slu脜戮bu vyu脜戮脙颅val naplno? 脗聽脗聽Tom脙隆脜隆 Zdechovsk脙陆 (PPE), odpov脛聸脛聫 na ot脙隆zku polo脜戮enou zvednut脙颅m modr脙漏 karty. 芒聙聯 Pane Kali脜聢脙隆ku, d脛聸kuji za ot脙隆zku. Tuto ot脙隆zku byste m脛聸l sm脛聸脜聶ovat sekretari脙隆tu v脙陆boru CONT, kter脙陆 tu zpr脙隆vu zpracov脙隆val. Jinak, mysl脙颅m si, 脜戮e vy jste zm脙颅n脛聸n docela 脛聧astou u n脙隆s v bezpe脛聧nostn脙颅ch slu脜戮b脙隆ch. Mysl脙颅m si, 脜戮e va脜隆e n脙隆v脜隆t脛聸vy rusk脙漏 ambas脙隆dy a ut脙颅k脙隆n脙颅 zadn脙颅m vchodem nebo va脜隆e spolupr脙隆ce s Voice of Europe, kter脙陆 pat脜聶脙颅 k prorusk脙漏 dezinforma脛聧n脙颅 sc脙漏n脛聸, a mnoz脙颅 politici za tuto spolupr脙隆ci byli placeni, jsou velmi dob脜聶e zn脙隆my a ukazuje to na 脙潞padek ur脛聧it脙漏 demokracie a kultury u 脛聧lov脛聸ka, kter脙陆 by m脛聸l radit premi脙漏rovi.
