脗聽脗聽Moritz K脙露rner (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Der politischen Rechten gehen in diesen Rechtsstaatsdiskussionen die Argumente aus. Jetzt musste man sogar zu dem Argument greifen, dass niemand da ist脗聽芒聙聯 wenn man der einzige seiner Fraktion ist, der 脙录berhaupt da ist, aber hier Leute sitzen; das ist wirklich ganz interessant.

Dann kommt immer das Argument in diesen Rechtsstaatsdiskussionen, wir reden immer nur 脙录ber Ungarn. Jetzt reden wir nicht 脙录ber Ungarn, das zeigt ja eigentlich, dass wir uns um alle Mitgliedstaaten k脙录mmern脗聽芒聙聯 jetzt kann man das Argument auch nicht benutzen.

Und dann kommt das dritte Argument, was immer kommt in diesen Rechtsstaatsdiskussionen: Br脙录ssel will eigentlich nur Regierungen bestrafen, die ihnen politisch nicht gefallen, rechte Regierungen. Ja, aber Fico脗聽芒聙聯 als ich das letzte Mal geguckt habe, sie sitzen jetzt bei den fraktionslosen Mitgliedern, aber eigentlich ist das eine sozialdemokratische Partei! Alle Argumente, die sie immer haben, fallen in Luft zusammen.

Worum geht es? Es geht uns darum, den Rechtsstaat zu sch脙录tzen, weil es uns darum geht, dass Steuergelder, unsere Steuerzahler脗聽芒聙聯 deutsche Steuerzahler, slowakische Steuerzahler, ungarische Steuerzahler: Alle haben verdient, dass anst脙陇ndig mit dem Geld umgegangen wird. Und das kann nur ein Rechtsstaat sicherstellen, und deswegen schauen wir 脙录berall in der Europ脙陇ischen Union genau darauf hin.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tineke Strik (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, Council 芒聙聯 Putin, Orb脙隆n, Fico. Sadly, Slovakia is following in the footsteps of Hungary and Russia. Prime Minister Fico is cracking down at the moment on fundamental rights, NGOs and free media in a rapid rule of law 'race to the bottom'. A new constitution would deny gender diversity and restrict rainbow families and sex education. This is yet another blow for the Slovakian LGBTI+ community and women whose hopes for future progress are being squashed.

Once again, the pretence of protecting national sovereignty is used to oppress citizens and negate the primacy of EU law. Fico's anti-NGO law uses allegations of foreign influence to silence critical voices. Council, Commissioner 芒聙聯 in Hungary we have seen that the typical EU strategy of acting too little, too late fails to protect citizens. The impunity of Orb脙隆n has now emboldened Fico to follow suit. So Commission, Council 芒聙聯 do not make the same mistake again.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Katar脙颅na Roth Neve脛聫alov脙隆 (NI), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Ms Strik, thank you very much for your words. I don't know how many times you have visited Slovakia or how much do you know about Slovakia, but I don't want to be disrespectful about this. But I would like to ask, you very much mentioned several times the rule of law. So can you please quote or tell me where the rule of law definition is in the European law? If you are so much sure about that, thank you very much.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tineke Strik (Verts/ALE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Well, if you don't know, it's good that you ask the question, of course. And I think if you read the Rule of Law reports of the Commission, you can see very clearly that it's a constellation of different values and guarantees that all Member States need to ensure to protect citizens. And maybe the most important rule of law principle you can find in Article 2 of our Treaty on European Union, where our values are anchored and where every Member State needs to obey this principle.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hanna Gedin (The Left). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! I Slovakien f脙露rs脙露ker man nu kringg脙楼 EU-lag, Europakonventionen, barnkonventionen och internationella f脙露rdrag. L脙楼t oss se till att de inte kommer undan med det.

R脙陇tten att best脙陇mma 脙露ver sin egen kropp 脙陇r inte f脙露rhandlingsbar. Kvinnors r脙陇tt till abort m脙楼ste vara en grundsten i varje demokrati. Att f脙露rs脙陇mra f脙露r HBTQ-personer, att v脙陇gra samk脙露nade par r脙陇tten att bilda familj 芒聙聯 det 脙陇r institutionaliserad diskriminering, och det f脙楼r aldrig normaliseras. Att skapa ett samh脙陇llsklimat som misst脙陇nkligg脙露r p脙楼 grund av k脙露n, sexuell l脙陇ggning eller bakgrund 脙陇r farligt. Att vika sig f脙露r reaktion脙陇ra krafter som g脙露r skillnad p脙楼 m脙陇nniskor och m脙陇nniskor 脙陇r farligt. Det 脙陇r riktigt farligt.

Till Slovakiens folk vill jag s脙陇ga: Er kamp f脙露r frihet och v脙陇rdighet 脙陇r ocks脙楼 v脙楼r kamp. Vi ser er, vi h脙露r er och vi 脙陇r med er.

(Talaren godtog en fr脙楼ga ("bl脙楼tt kort").)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脛陆ubo脜隆 Blaha (NI), ot脙隆zka polo脜戮en脙隆 zdvihnut脙颅m modrej karty. 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani kolegy脜聢a, vy nen脙隆vid脙颅te Roberta Fica, preto脜戮e on chce mier a vy chcete vojnu. Nen脙隆vid脙颅te Roberta Fica, lebo odmieta t脙潞 va脜隆u extr脙漏mistick脙潞 progres脙颅vnu ideol脙鲁giu.

Ale mo脜戮no neviete, 脜戮e cel脙隆 t脙隆to zbyto脛聧n脙隆 a 脙潞脛聧elov脙隆 diskusia je tu len preto, lebo to chce opozi脛聧n脙隆 strana na Slovensku Progres脙颅vne Slovensko, ktorej europoslancov vypl脙隆ca britsk脙隆 vl脙隆da, aby zasahovala do demokratick脙陆ch volieb na Slovensku.

Na Slovensku to pova脜戮ujeme za vlastizradu a ja som presved脛聧en脙陆, 脜戮e aj vy by ste sa mali sp脙陆ta脜楼 va脜隆ich kolegov z Renew, 脛聧i p脙隆chaj脙潞 vlastizradu alebo 脛聧i s脙潞 len tak presved脛聧en脙颅 o svojej nen脙隆visti vo脛聧i vlastnej vlasti. Je to hanba.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hanna Gedin (The Left), svar ("bl脙楼tt kort"). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Jag uppfattar att kollegan h脙陇r p脙楼st脙楼r att jag 脙陇r fr脙楼n Renew, men det 脙陇r jag inte. Jag 脙陇r fr脙楼n The Left. Jag 脙陇r ocks脙楼 jurist i grund och botten, och det inneb脙陇r att jag 脙陇r extremt bekymrad. Jag 脙陇r extremt bekymrad 脙露ver alla f脙露rs脙露k som jag ser n脙陇r man f脙露rs脙露ker kringg脙楼 grundl脙陇ggande m脙陇nskliga r脙陇ttigheter, n脙陇r man genom att f脙露resl脙楼 den h脙陇r nya grundlagen och f脙露r脙陇ndringen, genom det f脙露rs脙露ker kringg脙楼 inte bara EU-lag utan Europakonventionen, barnkonventionen och internationella f脙露rdrag. Det tycker jag borde oroa alla i denna kammaren, oavsett vilken partigrupp man tillh脙露r.

脗聽脗聽Monika Be脜聢ov脙隆 (NI). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, p脙隆n komis脙隆r, chcem sa po脛聫akova脜楼 za va脜隆e vyst脙潞penie a som ve脛戮mi rada, 脜戮e ste na脜隆ej slovenskej opoz脙颅cii zd脙麓raznili, 脜戮e dial脙鲁g medzi Eur脙鲁pskou komisiou a vl脙隆dou Slovenskej republiky prebieha ve脛戮mi dobre, 脜戮e nerob脙颅 vl脙隆da Slovenskej republiky v脙麓bec 脜戮iadne komplik脙隆cie a 脜戮e h脛戮ad脙隆me rie脜隆enia pre probl脙漏my, ktor脙漏 e脜隆te s脙潞 otvoren脙漏.

Takisto chcem zd脙麓razni脜楼, a to zaznelo aj na misi脙隆ch a mrz脙颅 ma, 脜戮e to tu neodznelo od p脙隆nov spravodajcov z t脙陆chto misi脙颅, 脜戮e vl脙隆da Slovenskej republiky dopredu avizovala, 脜戮e nako脛戮ko ani slovensk脙漏, ani eur脙鲁pske mechanizmy nezaregistrovali nejak脙漏 nezrovnalosti, m脙隆me tu OLAF a m脙隆me tu Eur脙鲁psku prokurat脙潞ru, ktor脙隆 tak脙漏to nezrovnalosti zaeviduje. Vl脙隆da je pripraven脙隆 spolupracova脜楼.

S p脙隆nom Freundom tu dlh脙漏 roky vedieme diskusiu o Robertovi Ficovi a v脜隆etci dobre vieme, 脜戮e nie je priate脛戮om Roberta Fica. Re脜隆pektujem to. 脛聦o sa t脙陆ka, a teraz si po脜戮i脛聧iam v脙陆raz p脙隆na Webera, 芒聙聻crazy芒聙聹 p脙隆na Zdechovsk脙漏ho, to sn脙隆脛聫 netreba ani komentova脜楼.

Ale budem teraz hovori脜楼 tuto ku kolegom z Progres脙颅vneho Slovenska. Ja neviem, kolegyne a kolegovia, kde vy beriete v sebe t脙潞 ni脛聧脙颅m neopodstatnen脙潞 a bezbreh脙潞 p脙陆chu, ktorou tu st脙隆le 脙潞to脛聧脙颅te na vl脙隆du Slovenskej republiky. Chcem v脙隆m ale poradi脜楼. Reprezentujete tu Slovensk脙潞 republiku a m脙隆te st脙隆脜楼 za z脙隆ujmami Slovenskej republiky. Nemyslite si, 脜戮e oni v脜隆etci v脙隆s bud脙潞 ma脜楼 rad脜隆ej, ke脛聫 vy tu budete na Slovensko nad脙隆va脜楼 len preto, 脜戮e nem脙隆te 脜戮iadnu in脙潞 agendu, ktor脙潞 by ste pon脙潞kli.

(Re脛聧n脙颅脛聧ka odmietla ot脙隆zku, ktor脙潞 zdvihnut脙颅m modrej karty polo脜戮ila Lucia Yar)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Niclas Herbst (PPE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Ich sage gleich vorweg: Ich akzeptiere die blaue Karte, die gleich kommt, das ist in Ordnung.

Der Haushaltskontrollausschuss und seine Mission wurden schon mehrfach erw脙陇hnt. Wir waren in der Slowakei. Sie haben den Bericht vielleicht gar nicht gelesen, aber er ist durchaus ausgewogen. Die Position der slowakischen Beh脙露rden wird dort, glaube ich, v脙露llig korrekt wiedergegeben. Wir gehen auch gar nicht ein auf die aktuelle Skandal-Berichterstattung 脙录ber die G脙陇steh脙陇user und andere Dinge, sondern es geht um strukturelle Probleme; das ist die Aufgabe des Haushaltskontrollausschusses脗聽芒聙聯 dem sind wir nachgegangen.

Es gibt in der Tat Probleme. Die Sorgen nach dieser Fact-Finding-Mission sind nicht geringer geworden. Es geht insbesondere auch um die Ausstattung der slowakischen Beh脙露rden. Wenn dort die personelle Ausstattung und auch die Befugnisse nicht ausreichend gegeben sind, dann ist das ein Problem f脙录r uns, weil wir dann nur noch die EUStA haben, und die EUStA will das auch gar nicht alleine machen. Wir brauchen die slowakischen Beh脙露rden vor Ort, und wenn die nicht unabh脙陇ngig sind und nicht ausreichend ausgestattet, dann haben wir ein strukturelles Problem. Das muss angesprochen werden.

Es geht um zwei Dinge: Es geht darum, dass die Slowakische Republik ein ganz wichtiger Bestandteil unserer Europ脙陇ischen Union ist. Und das Geld, das zur Verf脙录gung gestellt wird, geh脙露rt den Menschen dort, und nicht einigen wenigen, sondern allen脗聽芒聙聯 auch im Bereich der Agrarpolitik. Darum geht es. Und das ist eine strukturelle Aufgabe, der wir als Parlament nachgehen m脙录ssen, und zwar in jedem europ脙陇ischen Mitgliedstaat.

Und das Zweite ist nat脙录rlich auch: Wir m脙录ssen die finanziellen Interessen der Europ脙陇ischen Union wahren, die Interessen der steuerzahlenden Menschen in Europa. Und es verwundert mich, dass gerade selbsternannte Patrioten sich nicht sozusagen daf脙录r engagieren, dass diese Interessen der hart arbeitenden Menschen wahrgenommen werden, dass das Geld nicht an Oligarchen oder Einzelne geht, sondern an die Menschen, die es wirklich verdient haben.

(Der Redner ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 zu antworten.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Vielen Dank, Herr Kollege, dass Sie die blaue Karte akzeptiert haben. Sie haben in meinen Augen relativ gut ausgef脙录hrt, warum eben das, was hier gefordert wird, nicht eine Einschr脙陇nkung der Souver脙陇nit脙陇t der Mitgliedstaaten bedeutet, sondern ein Ausschluss dieser Souver脙陇nit脙陇t der Mitgliedstaaten ist. K脙露nnten Sie f脙录r mich und vielleicht f脙录r die B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger der Europ脙陇ischen Union noch einmal erl脙陇utern, warum gerade deshalb die Argumentation der selbsternannten Patrioten keinen Sinn macht?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Niclas Herbst (PPE), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Sie haben ja die Antwort im Grunde schon selber gegeben: weil es um die Menschen geht und weil wir Standards haben, die f脙录r alle gelten sollen; das gilt im Auditbereich, das gilt bei der Rechnungspr脙录fung. Und wenn wir die Regeln f脙录r alle haben, dann ist das nicht nur gerecht, sondern es dient auch dem Ziel. Wir wollen etwas erreichen, zum Beispiel mit der Agrarpolitik, mit der Koh脙陇sionspolitik; und diese europ脙陇ischen Ziele zu erreichen, das ist im Interesse der Menschen.

Und deshalb ist gerade die Argumentationsweise der selbsternannten Patrioten auch nicht richtig, weil wir ja zum Beispiel im Bereich der Koh脙陇sionspolitik soziale Unterschiede abbauen wollen脗聽芒聙聯 das ist gut f脙录r die Menschen. Das schafft nicht nur Sicherheit f脙录r die Menschen, sondern das ist das, was wir europ脙陇isch erreichen wollen. Wir unterst脙录tzen脗聽芒聙聯 das haben Sie richtig gesagt脗聽芒聙聯 die Souver脙陇nit脙陇t der Menschen dort vor Ort und eigentlich auch der Nationalstaaten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Abir Al-Sahlani (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Robert Fico 芒聙聯 you are following the schoolbook of the crackdown on democracy and human rights. We've seen it before; you go after the LGBTQI+ persons, you go after the right of safe, legal abortion. You then go after the next group and the next group. You incite corruption.

The freedom that you are enjoying, the democracy that brought you to power, the rule of law that is protecting your rights as much as anyone else's rights; these are principles and values that were fought for by the Slovak people long before you came to power. They were fought for with sweat, tears and blood. These are not for you to be used for your own agenda.

You are turning your own country into Putin's Trojan horse, inside the very Union that contributed to the prosperity of your country. Not as a favour to you, but because the Slovak people belong in Europe. I am convinced that with the Slovak people, and because of our colleagues in progressive Slovakia, democracy will prevail and your attempt to turn your country into an autocracy will fail.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Krzysztof Brejza (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo! Dzi脜聸 na m脙鲁j kraj, na Polsk脛聶, spad脜聜y drony rosyjskie. My jeste脜聸my we wsp脙鲁lnocie europejskiej, w Unii Europejskiej. Tworzona by脜聜a wsp脙鲁lnie przez pa脜聞stwa mi脜聜uj脛聟ce warto脜聸ci i zasady. To wsp脙鲁lnota demokratycznych pa脜聞stw. Dzi脜聸 widzimy, jak przyw脙鲁dca S脜聜owacji, pan Fico, leci na Kreml, by odda脛聡 ho脜聜d Putinowi na Kremlu. Drodzy S脜聜owacy, my razem przez 45 lat byli脜聸my pod butem rosyjskim, ruskim. To jest znak dla nas wszystkich, 脜录e przyw脙鲁dca pa脜聞stwa europejskiego sprzeniewierza si脛聶 warto脜聸ciom europejskim, demokracji, praworz脛聟dno脜聸ci, atakuj脛聟c wolne media, oligarchizuj脛聟c pa脜聞stwo, niszcz脛聟c wolne s脛聟dy, woln脛聟 prokuratur脛聶. Robi to po to, by trafi脛聡 do orbity Kremla, by znale脜潞脛聡 si脛聶 w strefie Kremla. Ale praworz脛聟dno脜聸脛聡 to co脜聸 wi脛聶cej. Praworz脛聟dno脜聸脛聡 jest oznak脛聟 wolno脜聸ci. I czym si脛聶 r脙鲁脜录ni kraj z praworz脛聟dno脜聸ci脛聟 od kraju bez praworz脛聟dno脜聸ci? R脙鲁脜录ni si脛聶 tym, 脜录e kraj z praworz脛聟dno脜聸ci脛聟 jest krajem wolnych obywateli. A tam, gdzie nie ma praworz脛聟dno脜聸ci, tak jak w tej Rosji, tak jak na Kremlu, jak na Bia脜聜orusi, tam nie ma wolno脜聸ci. Dlatego drodzy bracia S脜聜owacy, walczcie o swoj脛聟 praworz脛聟dno脜聸脛聡, walczcie o warto脜聸ci europejskie. S.

Bojujte za pr脙隆vny 脜隆t脙隆t. Sme s vami.

脗聽脗聽Helmut Brandst脙陇tter (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, one of the first memories of my life was the Iron Curtain, you know, as a little boy, and I saw the Iron Curtain to Hungary and to Czechoslovakia, as it was called, and I couldn't believe it, you know 芒聙聯 the towers with the soldiers and their guns. And then I was so happy, like all the others, when it was open, and we had our friends and we could live together. And it's still, by the way, a beautiful trip from Vienna to Bratislava on the Danube. And now we're working together, and we have the same rules. Together, we live with the rule of law, and that's really good.

But then I hear the recent findings of the EP Budgetary Control Committee's findings, and they reveal troubling cracks in Slovakia's management of EU funds, especially the misappropriation by the agricultural payments agency. And I really have to tell you: it's the money of the Europeans. We live together. We should have the same rights and the same duties and our money for everybody, but not for the corrupt people.

脗聽脗聽Branislav Ondru脜隆 (NI). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, kolegyne a kolegovia, vyst脙潞penia, ktor脙漏 sme si tu vypo脛聧uli, s脙潞 najlep脜隆脙颅m d脙麓kazom, 脜戮e tu nejde a od za脛聧iatku ne脜隆lo o pr脙隆vny 脜隆t脙隆t na Slovensku, preto脜戮e neexistuj脙潞 脜戮iadne argumenty, 脜戮e situ脙隆cia u n脙隆s je hor脜隆ia ne脜戮 inde. V skuto脛聧nosti je toto fanatick脙陆 a bezoh脛戮adn脙陆 脙潞tok proti Slovensku opakovan脙陆m zneu脜戮脙颅van脙颅m eur脙鲁pskych in脜隆tit脙潞ci脙颅 zo strany Progres脙颅vneho Slovenska.

Spome脜聢te si, pros脙颅m, ako sa po eurovo脛戮b脙隆ch stran脙颅cky kolega Progres脙颅vneho Slovenska, komis脙隆r Didier Reynders opakovane pok脙潞脜隆al dosta脜楼 Slovensko na s脙潞d kv脙麓li pr脙隆vnemu 脜隆t脙隆tu. Ten Didier Reynders, ktor脙陆 vz脙隆p脙陇t脙颅 po skon脛聧en脙颅 v脙陆konu svojej funkcie sa stal predmetom vy脜隆etrovania belgickej pol脙颅cie pre pranie 脜隆pinav脙陆ch pe脜聢az脙颅 a vtrhla mu pol脙颅cia domov aj do jeho kancel脙隆rie, aby zaistila d脙麓kazy. Tak脜戮e e脜隆te vy sa tu oh脙隆脜聢ate pr脙隆vnym 脜隆t脙隆tom?

D脙隆my a p脙隆ni, ale mne ste neumo脜戮nili z脙潞脛聧astni脜楼 sa na kontrolnej misii, hoci to bolo poru脜隆enie, hrub脙漏 poru脜隆enie ofici脙隆lnych parlamentn脙陆ch pravidiel, a vyl脙潞脛聧ili ste jedin脙漏ho slovensk脙漏ho poslanca, ktor脙陆 mal z脙隆ujem z脙潞脛聧astni脜楼 sa na tejto kontrolnej komisii a poru脜隆ili ste rokovac脙颅 poriadok tohto Eur脙鲁pskeho parlamentu. Hanba! Preto脜戮e v skuto脛聧nosti to, 脛聧o rob脙颅te, je pln脙漏 netransparentnosti, zatajovania, vylu脛聧ovania europoslanca, ktor脙陆 m脙隆 legit脙颅mny mand脙隆t, z toho, aby sa z脙潞脛聧astnil na kontrolnej 脛聧innosti tohto Eur脙鲁pskeho parlamentu.

Toto nie je kontroln脙隆 脛聧innos脜楼 Eur脙鲁pskeho parlamentu. Toto je hrub脙漏 zneu脜戮脙颅vanie Eur脙鲁pskeho parlamentu na vn脙潞tropolitick脙陆 boj frustrovanej slovenskej opoz脙颅cie, ktor脙隆 nie je schopn脙隆 v legit脙颅mnej politickej s脙潞脜楼a脜戮i na Slovensku uspie脜楼.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Raquel Garc脙颅a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, we have heard a lot of good things tonight, and the rest, because it will be about the people, I will say it in Dutch for also the people at home.

Want ik wil het hebben over de mensen, de mensen over wie dit gaat. Dat zijn de Slowaakse mensen. Ik was daar, in Slowakije, deze zomer nog. Ik sprak daar met mensen die het corrupte wanbeleid van premier Fico helemaal zat zijn. Helemaal zat. Net als andere Europeanen. Die hebben er ook genoeg van dat hun belastinggeld naar zo芒聙聶n corrupte bende gaat.

Daarom moeten we naast het Slowaakse volk staan. Daarom moeten we de mensen daar steunen. De mensen willen ziekenhuizen. Ze willen wegen. Ze willen scholen. De mensen willen een einde maken aan de corruptie van deze regering.

Er is hoop, want in Slowakije hoorde ik dat mensen iets anders willen. Een regering die corruptie aanpakt en het anders doet. Een regering die de rechten van lhbtiq+芒聙聭mensen respecteert. Dat hebben we gezien vanavond. Het is mogelijk. Er is hoop.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael McGrath, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, thank you, and thank you, Commissioner McGrath. Thanks also to all of the honourable Members who contributed to the debate this evening.

Let me recall that the Commission welcomes Slovakia's engagement in intensive dialogues 芒聙聯 this openness to dialogue is valued, and it has brought tangible results. At the same time, concerns do remain. The 2025 Rule of Law report provides the Commission's latest assessment of the situation. It showed the progress made on last year's recommendations and issued new ones.

I want to assure everyone in the House, irrespective of their view, that the Rule of Law report represents the Commission's objective assessment of the situation in every Member State, based on the facts. Indeed, as a Commissioner, I would have taken an oath to the effect that I would act independently, and that is given effect in the Rule of Law report, which the Commission has adopted.

Let me also underline a simple but fundamental truth. The effective management of EU funds depends on the strength of the rule of law. Without independent institutions, without accountability and without public trust, no funding system can function properly. EU funds must serve their purpose to support growth, resilience and cohesion, and not be diverted or undermined by corruption or weak oversight.

This is why the Commission continuously monitors the use of EU funds in all Member States, and why we insist that sound financial management and the rule of law go hand in hand. There was a question raised over the course of the debate about the rule of law. What is it? Does it have any legal basis? As was correctly pointed out, it is set out in Article 2 of the Treaty. I would also make the point that the Court of Justice, in its case law, recognises the rule of law as one of the fundamental values of the EU, alongside democracy and fundamental rights, and the rule of law is legally binding on all Member States of the European Union. I would also draw attention to the definition in the Conditionality Regulation, which is the law of the European Union. This defines the rule of law. It refers to the Union value enshrined in Article 2 of the Treaty on European Union. It includes the principles of legality, implying a transparent, accountable, democratic and pluralistic law-making process, legal certainty, prohibition of脗聽arbitrariness of the executive powers, effective judicial protection 芒聙聯 including access to justice by independent and impartial courts. Also, as regards fundamental rights, separation of powers, and non-discrimination and equality before the law. The rule of law shall be understood, having regard to the other Union values and principles enshrined in Article 2 of the TEU. So any Member is entitled to their own opinion, their own view, but they are not entitled to their own facts. The rule of law is clearly set out in the context of the legal code of the European Union, as interpreted and applied by the Court of Justice as well.

The Commission will continuously monitor the use of all EU funds, as I said. in all Member States, because protecting the Union's financial interests is not only a legal obligation, but also a moral duty towards European citizens and taxpayers.

As evidenced in July's Rule of Law report, more work is necessary in Slovakia to address the report's recommendations. Dialogue with the Commission is a very important part of the process, but it must be matched by concrete results on the ground. Reforms in Member States should strengthen, not weaken, the ability of institutions to fight corruption and to uphold the rule of law. The Commission will continue to follow developments closely in the spirit of cooperation, but also with the necessary rigour and the necessary vigilance and readiness to make use of our instruments as appropriate.

Let me conclude by assuring you that the Commission shares the commitment of this House to uphold the rule of law and the sound financial management of EU funds in all EU Member States, including Slovakia.

I would remind the House that, in the context of the MFF proposal that the Commission has brought forward, we are proposing to strengthen that link between rule of law and access to EU funds.

The Conditionality Regulation, which I have quoted, will remain in place if our proposals are adopted alongside in the national and regional partnership plans. There will be a direct link to the rule of law, respect for the rule of law and also respect for the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights as well.

So thanks to all of the colleagues for their attention and for their contribution this evening. All of the contributions will be carefully reflected on, and I will continue to give these issues my close personal attention.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, Commissioner, thank you for your comments and remarks. We all 芒聙聯 EU institutions and Member States together 芒聙聯 share a common responsibility to protect the rule of law and to address problematic situations that may emerge on the ground. I want to say thank you very much once again.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

PRESIDE: ESTEBAN GONZ脙聛LEZ PONS
Vicepresidente

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The debate is closed.

Declaraciones por escrito (art脙颅culo 178 del Reglamento interno)

脗聽脗聽Katar脙颅na Roth Neve脛聫alov脙隆 (NI), p脙颅somne. 芒聙聯 Cel脙隆 t脙隆to debata je op脙陇脜楼 zbyto脛聧n脙隆, pln脙隆 klamstiev a je zneu脜戮脙颅van脙隆 na vn脙潞tropolitick脙陆 boj na Slovensku zo strany na脜隆ej opoz脙颅cie. Aj z vyst脙潞penia komis脙隆ra je jasn脙漏, 脜戮e slovensk脙隆 vl脙隆da plne spolupracuje s Eur脙鲁pskou komisiou, odpoved脙隆 na ot脙隆zky o r脙麓znych typoch prij脙颅manej legislat脙颅vy, ktor脙潞 na脜隆a opoz脙颅cia neust脙隆le vy脜楼ahuje a potvrdzuje, 脜戮e t脙隆to spolupr脙隆ca komisie a vl脙隆dy je na 脜隆tandardne vysokej 脙潞rovni.

Kolegovia z in脙陆ch 脜隆t脙隆tov, ktor脙颅 vystupuj脙潞 v tejto debate vlastne ani netu脜隆ia, 脜戮e iba sl脙潞脜戮ia protivl脙隆dnym politikom z Progres脙颅vneho Slovenska ako u脜戮ito脛聧n脙颅 bl脙隆zni. Napr脙颅klad tak脙漏 obvinenie, 脜戮e deleg脙隆cie v脙陆borov Europarlamentu, ktor脙漏 boli na Slovensku v lete, sa stretli s neadekv脙隆tnymi 脙潞rov脜聢ami z脙隆stupcov slovensk脙陆ch in脜隆tit脙潞ci脙颅 s脙潞 tot脙隆lny nezmysel. Deleg脙隆cie boli prijat脙漏 na najvy脜隆脜隆ej 脙潞rovni, ministrami, ved脙潞cimi 脙潞radov, ale aj premi脙漏rom Slovenska. Tak脙隆to 脙潞rove脜聢 je vysoko nad脜隆tandardn脙隆. Aj z toho vypl脙陆va, 脜戮e v脙麓bec netu脜隆ia, do akej debaty sa zapojili a vlastne o situ脙隆cii ni脛聧 nevedia, iba naleteli narat脙颅vom na脜隆ich kolegov z opoz脙颅cie, ktor脙陆ch 脙潞lohou je iba 脜隆kodi脜楼 Slovensku.

脗聽

18. Taxation of large digital platforms in the light of international developments (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item on the agenda is the Oral Question to the Commission on Taxation of large digital platforms in the light of international developments (B10-0008/2025) (2025/2756(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Regina Doherty, author. 芒聙聯 Mr President, thank you Commissioner for joining us here this evening. I think we can all recall the scene from Scotland during the summer, where President von der Leyen painfully shook the hand of President Trump on a deal that only makes things harder and trade harder between the EU and the US. And I think it's in that context that we are discussing the principles that should govern global digitalised economy, particularly the taxation of digital services, this evening.

Taxation is all about fairness, dynamism, efficiency. It's how we raise the revenue to fund goods and services. It's about investing in the future, creating opportunities for growth and for security. Taxation should support competitiveness, investment and job creation. And if it does not do this, then it's not serving the common good. When it comes to the taxation of large digital platforms, as with all elements of tax policy, it's all about making sure that neither goal is undermined. And that's why we seek to discuss this matter today.

When capital is mobile and crosses international borders, how do we uphold the common good and also promote opportunity and investment? I think it's clear that global challenges like these require global solutions. And that's why it's right and proper that Europe has been such a leader in the negotiations of the OECD Pillar Two agreement. The agreement is a compromise that meets the realities of a digitalised economy. First, it sets out a minimum level of taxation on large companies, second, it introduces new rules for taxing the profits of large companies where they don't have that physical presence.

Our commitment to multilateralism is unwavering. At a time when international institutions are being undermined as never before, it is right that the EU continues to strongly support the OECD process, but clearly we can't make any progress if everybody is not at the table. Regions of the world that act on their own or depart from global standards create fragmentation and perverse incentives.

However, the return of Donald Trump as US President saw an executive order to withdraw his country from the agreement. That agreement was the result of so many years of hard work, so it's vital, I think, that that work and this agreement be protected by us as an absolute priority. We have seen the change of approach and temperament from the US President, and it sees the agreement as an attack on American businesses, and he is loudly promising retaliatory actions. However, that's not a reason for us to give up on the agreement. Walking away would not help to resolve them, indeed, it probably would exacerbate the problem.

So we ask, where to now for the OECD negotiations, particularly in respect of Pillar One? How can the EU show leadership to keep this agreement on the table? We've seen proposals from the G7 for US-specific exemptions and allowances and they do require careful scrutiny, and I know the Members of this House will do so. But in the midst of this geopolitical discussion, we've also asked the Commission's views of calls to take unilateral action. I know that the question of new own resources is a separate matter, but it does remain part of the discussion. Does the Commission foresee unilateral action as a serious probability, and what would the threshold of this be?

Multinational investment is an important part of the European economy. It provides jobs, opportunities, vital government revenues across the EU, both directly and indirectly. And millions of our citizens rely on this investment to earn their livelihoods. That's why departing from multilateral processes presents huge risks and will not yield the benefits that some people say it will.

It's not about threats of retaliation, it's about showing that Europe is the place to do business and not creating imbalances that will drive our investment away. Is the Commission still committed to international engagement? Does it still recognise the economic hurdles that would exist if we departed from that engagement?

I think we are living currently in a period of huge economic uncertainty. There is regular turmoil on international markets and jobs announcements are being reassessed. So when it comes to tax policies, Europe does face big challenges. And while tax is rightly a Member State competence, the growth of divergent sectoral taxes creates internal trade barriers and undermines our EU economy. So instead of calling for new taxes, we should be looking at ways to better calibrate the ones that we already have 芒聙聯 and we have plenty 芒聙聯 so that they do not create additional burdens or barriers for investment.

Finally, Commissioner, specifically in relation to the digital services tax, I think it's crucial that we take an evidence-based approach. If the Commission is looking at this option, will it commit to a comprehensive cost-benefit analysis to reduce unforeseen consequences? Everyday, people, as normal, are using digital tools and services. The digital economy is at the heart of every single one of our lives, and there is almost no way that a tax does not ultimately impact consumers in some way. It's extremely unlikely that any DST would similarly be passed on in higher charges of subscriptions to the people that were served.

It's commonly said that there are only two certainties in life: death and taxes. And I think when it comes to the latter, we need to be responsible, forward-thinking and results orientated.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael McGrath, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, good evening honourable Members, I would like to thank Regina Doherty MEP for this oral question. Fair taxation of the digital economy has been a top priority for the European Commission for many years. Digitalisation of our economy challenges one of the fundamental principles of traditional tax systems: that profits should follow presence. This is why we have been leading efforts to redesign our tax framework. We need to ensure everyone pays their fair share; this is essential for good public finances.

We have been proactive in different ways. In 2018, for instance, we put forward two significant proposals: a digital services tax and a framework for a significant digital presence. Our leadership has driven global engagement and led to groundwork for the OECD's ambitious two-pillar discussion. While these discussions initially gained traction, achieving consensus has been challenging due to sensitivities with the United States. Currently, OECD discussions on Pillar脗聽1 are on hold. The United States has expressed readiness to resume discussions on Pillar脗聽1 once a solution for Pillar脗聽2 is found. We saw recent progress with the G7 statement, which suggests possible coexistence between Pillar脗聽2 and a US minimum tax regime. This is a major step forward 芒聙聯 it shows a shared commitment to tackling multinational tax avoidance and building a more stable international tax landscape. This alignment contributed to the USA's decision to remove Section脗聽899 from the One Big Beautiful Bill. It also paved the way for a more stable environment for international tax discussions.

Honourable Members, the OECD/G20 Inclusive Framework continues its work to develop a system acceptable and implementable for all. The Commission expects that discussions on Pillar脗聽1 could resume later this year. A multilateral solution remains the Commission's preferred approach to taxing the digital economy. Specific outcomes are yet uncertain, but all parties have shown willingness to engage constructively, and this is important.

While work on Pillar脗聽1 is ongoing, it is too early to comment on possible alternatives to the OECD-led process. For now, our focus is on giving the OECD process sufficient space and time to deliver. Pillar脗聽1 means the most substantial change in taxing rights in over a century. Careful discussions and deliberations are therefore crucial. Considering these ongoing discussions, we do not intend to present a new proposal for an EU digital tax at this stage, so no impact assessment has been prepared for a proposal that does not exist. Assessing the impact on consumers, for instance, would be impossible to predict in advance and in abstract.

As for the latest investigations under Section脗聽301 of the US Trade Act, we will continue to monitor its conclusions. The EU is unwavering in its commitment to multilateralism. This is a principle upon which it was founded. But multilateralism cannot be one sided 芒聙聯 we will continue to engage constructively with third countries to foster a multilateral solution. This also includes the United States, but if such a solution cannot be reached the Commission will remain in close dialogue with the European Parliament and Member States to chart the best course forward. The EU and its Member States have the sovereign right to put in place tax policies that ensure fair and equitable taxation of all economic players, including in the area of digital taxation. And again, I thank Regina Doherty MEP for the question and I look forward to the contributions of the Members of the House.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fernando Navarrete Rojas, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, President von der Leyen said this very morning that our imperative is for Europe to 'step up where others have stepped away'.

Well, this imperative applies particularly to international taxation: resisting the temptation of unilateral action is not a sign of weakness. It is responsibility and leadership. But what should we do if others do act unilaterally? Some may argue that it gives us free rein to retaliate. I disagree: unilateral action, either by them or by us, is always an invitation to conflict.

If Europe truly wants to be a global standard-setter in the new world order, we need to rethink our strategy. Sovereignty is not about the power to do everything we can do, it is about the responsibility to do what we should do. The world is watching and Europe must lead by example. Europe must remain open to the world and abide to globally agreed rules to be credible partners. We must first do our homework, simplify, innovate and create the conditions for businesses to grow here in Europe.

A unilateral EU digital service tax will not get us there. Digital service companies are currently taxed according to the same principles to any other sector, based on profits and on the location where these services are provided. Some may consider this is flawed, but that only calls for a stronger global engagement for an alternative, not for unilateral shortcuts.

Therefore, I'm really glad the Commission has decided to put aside the digital service tax as own resources. It is an important step in showing real leadership in a contested world.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bruno Gon脙搂alves, on behalf of the S&D Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, big tech companies are not paying their fair share of taxes today. And we know that this is no longer a controversial stance, only a fact. All evidence points to workers and small businesses paying higher rates than Meta, Amazon and other digital giants. Unfortunately, when we were close to an international deal with more than 130 countries, President Trump stepped in to protect the interests of his corporate friends. With the threat of tariffs and other retaliation, this US administration is now trying to prevent us from reaching a European solution, and it is very curious that the far right that claims to be sovereigntist didn't even show up to the debate today.

But colleagues, this is not the time to backtrack. The debate today is not only about tax justice, but also about our sovereignty. We must remain and stand united and remind everyone that the EU remains a democracy. The question is therefore simple. Are you siding with the President Trump and forfeiting the mandate given by your citizens, by our citizens, to legislate on their behalf, or are you ready to stand for our values and to ensure that big tech cannot avoid their fair share of taxes and their fair share of responsibilities? We chose the second option. The S&D Group chose tax justice and, more importantly, we choose democracy.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Francesco Torselli, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, possiamo dire di essere tutti d'accordo sul fatto di dover regolamentare l'economia digitale e la sua fiscalit脙聽.

Permettetemi per脙虏 di sottolineare come, di pari passo, vada affrontato il tema del脗聽base erosion and profit shifting, ovvero del fenomeno per le quali le grandi multinazionali sfruttano le disparit脙聽 fiscali dei singoli Stati nazionali per poter spostare i propri profitti laddove vi sia una tassazione a loro pi脙鹿 favorevole.

Detto questo, non 脙篓 una tassazione unilaterale europea sui servizi digitali la scelta ottimale da fare, a nostro modo di vedere.

Spettabile Commissione, a voi il compito di perseguire la via del multilateralismo e della giustizia fiscale ma, vi prego, evitiamo le scelte autolesioniste come quelle che abbiamo visto in altri settori 芒聙聯 penso al manifatturiero, all'industria dell'auto, all'agroalimentare 芒聙聯 perch脙漏 finiremo col danneggiare nuovamente i consumatori, anzich脙漏 percorrere la via dell'equit脙聽 fiscale e della giustizia fiscale che, ogni tanto, sarebbe bene scegliere.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Katri Kulmuni, Renew-ryhm脙陇n puolesta. 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, suurten digialustojen verotusta kehitet脙陇脙陇n kansainv脙陇lisesti vastaamaan my脙露s suuren digitalouden kasvua. T脙陇ss脙陇 on pyrkimyksen脙陇 l脙露yt脙陇脙陇 tapoja verottaa sellaisten yritysten toimintaa, jotka sijoittuisivat maihin, joissa niill脙陇 ei ole fyysist脙陇 l脙陇sn脙陇oloa.脗聽OECD ja G20-maat pyrkiv脙陇t luomaan verotusnormeja digitaalisille yrityksille ja varmistamaan, ett脙陇 ne maksaisivat asianmukaiset verot maissa, joissa niiden tulot syntyv脙陇t, sen sijaan, ett脙陇 tuloja脗聽siirrett脙陇isiin veroparatiiseihin.

My脙露s EU:n t脙陇ytyy edist脙陇脙陇 suurten digitaalisten alustojen verotusta monin tavoin, totta kai globaaleilla sopimuksilla verotuksesta, mutta my脙露s kansallisilla verotusk脙陇yt脙陇nn脙露ill脙陇 digitaalisten palveluiden verotukseen, sek脙陇 edist脙陇脙陇 digitalisaatiota yleisemmin vahvistaen samalla digitaalista itsen脙陇isyytt脙陇 ja my脙露s normittamalla t脙陇t脙陇 alaa. Koska t脙陇m脙陇 on rajatylitt脙陇v脙陇脙陇, my脙露s ratkaisujen t脙陇ytyy olla verojen osalta kansainv脙陇lisi脙陇. On itsest脙陇脙陇n selv脙陇脙陇, ett脙陇 pit脙陇脙陇 toimia siell脙陇, miss脙陇 k脙陇ytt脙陇j脙陇t ja kuluttajat sijaitsevat riippumatta yrityksen fyysisest脙陇 l脙陇sn脙陇olosta. T脙陇m脙陇 on my脙露s itse asiassa yksi harvoja keinoja, joilla EU voi t脙陇ll脙陇 hetkell脙陇 pelata kovaa USA:ta vastaan, kuten my脙露s he tekev脙陇t meille.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasmus Andresen, im Namen der Verts/ALE-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! 脙聹ber 90脗聽Milliarden脗聽Euro脗聽芒聙聯 so viele Gewinne haben Meta, Amazon, Alphabet und Microsoft allein in den letzten drei Monaten gemacht. Sie verschieben die Gewinne so, dass sie eigentlich nirgendwo richtig Steuern zahlen und bei uns in Europa schon gar nicht. W脙陇hrend kleine Betriebe in der EU ehrlich Steuern zahlen, tricksen die Tech-Milliard脙陇re, wo sie nur k脙露nnen. Niemand in der Bev脙露lkerung findet das gut, und auch viele Abgeordnete hier im Haus wehren sich dagegen, aber trotzdem handelt die EU-Kommission nicht. Sie handelt nicht, weil sie Angst vor Trump hat, weil sie Angst davor hat, dass Trump Z脙露lle erh脙露ht oder Sicherheitsgarantien aufk脙录ndigt.

Aber ich sage Ihnen, im Umgang mit Donald Trump und den Tech-Konzernen ist Angst kein guter Ratgeber. Im Gegenteil: Mit Trump kann man keine verl脙陇sslichen Deals machen. Deshalb m脙录ssen wir uns unabh脙陇ngig von den USA aufstellen und daf脙录r sorgen, dass wir den Einfluss der gro脙聼en Tech-Konzerne beschr脙陇nken, und dazu geh脙露rt aus unserer Sicht auch eine faire Besteuerung der gro脙聼en Digitalkonzerne. Sie machen Milliardengewinne脗聽芒聙聯 jetzt ist Zeit, dass sie daf脙录r Steuern zahlen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jussi Saramo, The Left-ryhm脙陇n puolesta. 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, meid脙陇n veroj脙陇rjestelm脙陇mme ei vastaa 2000-luvun todellisuutta. Se on rakennettu fyysiselle l脙陇sn脙陇ololle, jolla on yh脙陇 pienenev脙陇 merkitys taloudessa. Kaikkein suurimmat ja rikkaimmat yritykset eiv脙陇t usein maksa lainkaan veroja maihin, joissa ne tekev脙陇t voittonsa. T脙陇m脙陇n seurauksena rahaa ei riit脙陇 kouluille eik脙陇 sairaaloille, valtiot velkaantuvat, eriarvoisuus kasvaa ja markkinoilla voittavat ep脙陇reilusti kilpailevat j脙陇ttiyritykset.脗聽Siksi jo seitsem脙陇n vuotta sitten alettiin rakentaa digij脙陇ttien verotusta Euroopan komission toimesta, mutta t脙陇m脙陇 digivero j脙陇脙陇dytettiin, kun alettiin rakentaa viel脙陇 parempaa OECD:n sopimusta kaikkien suuryritysten tasapuolisesta verottamisesta.

Sitten tuli Trump. Ensi t脙露ikseen h脙陇n heitti uudet verosopimukset roskakoriin. Trumpin miljard脙露脙露rikaverit naureskelevat nyt tyytyv脙陇isen脙陇 芒聙聯 ja nyt piti palata alkuper脙陇iseen suunnitelmaan, josta voimme p脙陇脙陇tt脙陇脙陇 itse, mutta oikeistolainen komissio totteleekin n脙露yr脙陇sti Trumpin k脙陇skyj脙陇. Aikana, jolloin digiverojen tulisi olla Trumpin meille asettamien tullien vastapainona viel脙陇 suunniteltujakin isompia, on korkea aika laittaa Muskit, Zuckerbergit ja heid脙陇n kaverinsa tasapuolisesti verolle 芒聙聯 samalle viivalle eurooppalaisten yritysten kanssa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rada Laykova, on behalf of the ESN Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, today we are discussing the taxation of large digital platforms. But actually, no one in this Chamber addressed the elephant in the room: why does the EU so desperately need new fiscal resources?

The answer is very simple: the common NextGenerationEU debt. Yes, this is in fact forbidden by the Treaties, and Brussels assumed that low interest rates would go on forever, which was wrong.脗聽Now, 15脗聽% and up to 20脗聽% of the annual EU budget will be consumed just to pay interest. Not to pay back the debt but, every single year, only to refinance it again and again.

This means the EU is building a second household that exists only to serve big investors. Here we are not discussing fairness or innovation, but addressing this trap, this debt trap, imposed on today's and tomorrow's Europeans. NextGeneration EU is in fact in the debt of the next generation.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Markus Ferber (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen, liebe Kollegen! Unsere Steuerregeln stammen aus einer Zeit, in der wirtschaftliche Aktivit脙陇t an Fabrikhallen, an Lagern, an B脙录rogeb脙陇uden festzumachen war. Heute jedoch entstehen Werte in Datenstr脙露men, auf Plattformen, in digitalen Gesch脙陇ftsmodellen, und das muss sich nat脙录rlich auch in unserem Steuerrecht widerspiegeln. Wir brauchen eine zeitgem脙陇脙聼e Definition der steuerlichen Betriebsst脙陇tte, die nicht mehr nur aus Geb脙陇uden, aus Backstein oder M脙露rtel bestehen kann.

Das Prinzip ist klar und脗聽芒聙聯 ich glaube脗聽芒聙聯 auch gerecht. Gewinne m脙录ssen dort besteuert werden, wo die Wertsch脙露pfung stattfindet. Digitale Konzerne d脙录rfen sich nicht l脙陇nger der Verantwortung entziehen, indem sie Gewinne in Niedrigsteuerl脙陇nder verschieben, w脙陇hrend sie ihre Ums脙陇tze mitten im europ脙陇ischen Binnenmarkt erzielen.

Und der Binnenmarkt ist auch das entscheidende Stichwort. Ein Flickenteppich nationaler Digitalsteuern wird uns nicht weiterbringen. Nur ein einheitlicher europ脙陇ischer Ansatz kann Fairness und Rechtssicherheit im Binnenmarkt gew脙陇hrleisten und zugleich daf脙录r sorgen, dass alle Unternehmen nach den gleichen Regeln zu spielen haben.

Dabei sollten wir uns nicht von den Widerst脙陇nden aus den USA abhalten lassen. Es ist eine Frage unserer eigenen Souver脙陇nit脙陇t, dass Europa seine eigenen Entscheidungen trifft, wenn es um die Steuergerechtigkeit im Bereich der digitalen Welt geht. Unsere B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger erwarten zu Recht, dass auch die digitalen Giganten ihren fairen Beitrag leisten. Deswegen, Herr Kommissar, brauchen wir einen neuen Anlauf f脙录r eine europ脙陇ische Digitalsteuer.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Aurore Lalucq (S&D). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, le d脙漏bat d'aujourd'hui ne porte pas tant sur la fiscalit脙漏 que sur notre courage, notre souverainet脙漏 et notre capacit脙漏 脙聽 admettre la r脙漏alit脙漏. Quelle est-elle aujourd'hui?

Nous avons accept脙漏 que Trump se retire de l'accord sur la taxation mondiale, sur les multinationales, pensant ainsi le calmer pour obtenir un bon deal avec les 脙聣tats-Unis. Est-ce que notre accord commercial est bon? Est-ce qu'il s'est calm脙漏? La r脙漏ponse est non. Mais on a vendu 脙搂a en se disant que 脙搂a a apport脙漏 de la certitude, un horizon stable, aux entreprises. Est-ce que leur horizon est stable? La r脙漏ponse est non, puisque le lendemain m脙陋me il attaquait 脙聵rsted, puis Novo脗聽Nordisk, puis il nous mettait au d脙漏fi d'oser mettre en place une taxation sur le num脙漏rique. Qu'avons-nous fait alors? Toujours rien, pensant l脙聽 aussi le calmer. Et maintenant c'est son soutien m脙陋me 脙聽 l'OCDE qu'il est en train de remettre en cause.

Quand allons-nous donc comprendre? Quand allons-nous comprendre que Donald脗聽Trump ne s'arr脙陋tera jamais et que, oui, 脙漏videmment, il faut mettre en place une taxation sur le num脙漏rique puisque les GAFAM, les grandes entreprises technologiques am脙漏ricaines, ne vivent pas sur le march脙漏 europ脙漏en? Mais il faut m脙陋me aller plus loin. Il faut d脙漏fendre nos entreprises en d脙漏fendant notre r脙漏glementation, en mettant en place une politique de souverainet脙漏 num脙漏rique, une vraie. Pas uniquement des mots, comme on l'a dit aujourd'hui d'Ursula脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, car ce qu'elle a dit, c'脙漏tait des mots jusqu'脙聽 pr脙漏sent. Il nous faut privil脙漏gier, 脙聽 travers des achats, nos acteurs du num脙漏rique europ脙漏en. Et cela commence aujourd'hui.

Donc l'amour c'est bien, les preuves d'amour c'est mieux. On va maintenant demander 脙聽 la Commission europ脙漏enne des preuves d'amour, et notamment pour les acteurs du num脙漏rique qui se sentent aujourd'hui, au niveau europ脙漏en, totalement d脙漏laiss脙漏s. Et un conseil 脙聽 Mme脗聽Ursula脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen: relire le rapport Draghi, parce qu'elle l'a trahi aujourd'hui, et plus d'une fois.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽St脙漏phanie Yon-Courtin (Renew). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire脗聽McGrath, chers coll脙篓gues, le dernier deal de l'OCDE 脙漏tait clair, pourtant: les multinationales doivent payer l脙聽 o脙鹿 elles font leurs profits. C'est une question de justice fiscale. Mais, depuis le retour de Donald脗聽Trump, l'脙漏quilibre mondial vacille et les n脙漏gociations multilat脙漏rales sont 脙聽 l'arr脙陋t. L'Europe ne peut pas continuer 脙聽 baisser la t脙陋te chaque fois que Trump hausse le ton.

Le temps n'est plus 脙聽 la peur, mais au courage et 脙聽 l'action. Taxer les g脙漏ants du num脙漏rique, ce n'est pas juste collecter de l'argent, c'est aussi une question de souverainet脙漏 num脙漏rique. Les coll脙篓gues l'ont d脙漏j脙聽 rappel脙漏, mais il faut le redire. Mais cela ne s'arr脙陋te pas 脙聽 la taxation des grandes entreprises technologiques.

Trump d脙漏fie toutes nos r脙篓gles num脙漏riques. Des r脙篓gles qui prot脙篓gent 450脗聽millions d'Europ脙漏ens, et toutes les entreprises, tous les citoyens, sans exception. Donc, les 脙聣tats-Unis de Donald脗聽Trump voient nos r脙漏glementations et nos valeurs comme des attaques et r脙漏pondent par le chantage tarifaire.

Cela ne peut plus durer. Les grandes entreprises technologiques ne peuvent pas continuer 脙聽 d脙漏fier nos lois, 脙聽 fragiliser nos entreprises et 脙聽 mettre nos citoyens en danger. Si Trump veut jouer au cowboy, l'Europe doit rappeler qu'ici c'est elle qui est le sh脙漏rif.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Toussaint (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, nous devons faire payer les g脙漏ants du num脙漏rique: Google, Amazon, TikTok, Meta et d'autres. Ces entreprises pillent nos donn脙漏es, nous espionnent, pi脙漏tinent la nature et les droits humains et sociaux, puis partent se cacher dans des paradis fiscaux, parfois m脙陋me au c脜聯ur de l'Europe, Monsieur le Commissaire.

Pendant que les Europ脙漏ens peinent 脙聽 rassembler de quoi vivre dignement 脙聽 la sueur de leur front, les 脗芦tech bros脗禄, Musk, Bezos ou Zuckerberg, continuent 脙聽 engranger des milliards, et cela en ne payant quasiment aucun imp脙麓t. Cela est-il juste? Non. Et cela doit cesser.

Nous, Parlement europ脙漏en, avons, de fa脙搂on r脙漏p脙漏t脙漏e, demand脙漏 脙聽 ce que ces g脙漏ants du num脙漏rique soient tax脙漏s au niveau international, c'est vrai, mais d脙漏j脙聽, oui, d脙漏j脙聽 au niveau europ脙漏en. Et le tout sans plus tarder. Une taxe europ脙漏enne 脙聽 5脗聽% sur les services num脙漏riques, c'est simple, ce serait 37脗聽milliards par an d脙篓s 2026. Pouvons-nous s脙漏rieusement, raisonnablement nous permettre de nous passer de cet argent pr脙漏cieux?

Mais voil脙聽 qu'appara脙庐t M.脗聽Trump, leur chevalier servant. Et quand M.脗聽Trump aboie, la Commission s'efface. Notre question aujourd'hui est donc plus urgente que jamais: comptez-vous, Monsieur le Commissaire, tenir t脙陋te 脙聽 Donald脗聽Trump? Comptez-vous d脙漏fendre la souverainet脙漏 europ脙漏enne face aux 脙聣tats-Unis? Que proposez-vous de concret pour tenir t脙陋te face aux g脙漏ants du num脙漏rique?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Danu脜隆e Nerudov脙隆 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, for too long, digital giants have profited from our European market without contributing fairly to our public finances or moderating hateful content.

'You deserve to die 芒聙聯 you and your son.' This is what I sometimes receive on my social media. I use social platforms every day. My children do too. I always believed that they should connect people and inspire creativity, but today, too often, they do the opposite. Instead of dialogue, we see hate; instead of facts, we see disinformation, Russian trolls, anti-Semitism, homophobia. This is not the digital European space we stand for. We must recreate this space 芒聙聯 a space for people with values, a space for respect, a space for democracy. And platforms must take responsibility for moderation, while at the same time paying their fair share in taxes. These are the sides of the same coin. Social platforms must go back to what they promised us: to connect people, not divide them; to inform, not to poison; to serve democracy, not to undermine it. Let's make sure they do.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matthias Ecke (S&D). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Unser Steuersystem funktioniert nicht mehr. Fr脙录her galt: Je erfolgreicher ein Unternehmen, desto mehr Steuern zahlt es. Aber heute entziehen sich gerade die erfolgreichsten ihrer Verantwortung. 2021 war der Tiefpunkt: Amazon, damals das zweitgr脙露脙聼te Unternehmen der Welt, bekam in der EU-Geld zur脙录ck, statt Steuern zu zahlen. Das versteht niemand mehr.

Die effektive Besteuerung digitaler Plattformen in Europa liegt heute bei unter 10脗聽Prozent, bei klassischen Unternehmen bei rund 20脗聽Prozent;脗聽vor 30脗聽Jahren haben die gr脙露脙聼ten Konzerne noch etwa 30脗聽Prozent Steuern gezahlt. Das ist nichts anderes als ein Schlag ins Gesicht der Menschen hier in Europa. Wenn Amazon, Google, Meta kaum Steuern zahlen, dann verlieren wir alle.

Und dass wir heute 脙录berhaupt 脙录ber die Digitalsteuern der EU sprechen m脙录ssen, liegt ja daran, dass Donald Trump und die US-Administration das OECD-Abkommen boykottieren. Klar, eine globale L脙露sung w脙陇re viel besser gegen diese Art der Gewinnverschiebung, aber wir d脙录rfen uns in der EU auch nicht von Trump abh脙陇ngig machen; wir m脙录ssen auch selber agieren. Und deswegen ist es richtig, dass wir sagen: Ja, wir m脙录ssen einen eigenen Vorschlag vorlegen, wenn es nicht weitergeht mit der OECD. Und nein, wir lassen uns das nicht von Donald Trump und den Tech-Oligarchen, die aufs Engste zusammenarbeiten, verbieten.

Deswegen: Europ脙陇ische Steuern werden hier in Europa entschieden, und wir wollen, dass digitale Plattformen endlich ihren fairen Anteil beitragen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Damian Boeselager (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Dear Commissioner, you should not have caved to Trump's bullying. You should not have stripped the digital tax just because of the looming US-UK trade deal. Let's be clear: a fair tax on big tech should not be a negotiation chip in any trade negotiation.

Nobody likes to pay taxes, but employees pay them and small business owners pay their fair share as well. Why? Because they understand that we need them to afford our pensions, subsidise trains and for high-quality health care systems.

But tech giants don't pay their fair share. In general, they pay around 9脗聽% of taxes in Europe, while traditional businesses pay 23脗聽%. So, since they are creative, we should be creative as well. First, how about we introduce an Amazon tax, a tax that is basically put on every product sold on large online platforms to equal the competition with local shops?

Second, how about a social media tax? We could make Meta, YouTube and TikTok pay for every user that they have in Europe, for example EUR 1 per user.

Third, how about a network usage tax, so that big tech actually pay for the networks and the infrastructure that they use?

Commissioner, every company that profits from Europe should also profit Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Angelika Winzig (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Herr Kommissar! Ja, es ist h脙露chst an der Zeit f脙录r eine europ脙陇ische Digitalsteuer. Ich bin eine 脙露sterreichische Unternehmerin und zahle wie viele Klein- und Mittelbetriebe und auch Gro脙聼betriebe meine Steuern dort, wo auch mein Gewinn anf脙陇llt. Ich kann mir genauso wie diese Unternehmer auch nicht aussuchen, wo ich meinen Gewinn hin verschieben m脙露chte.

Digitale Gro脙聼konzerne haben aber da ganz andere M脙露glichkeiten脗聽芒聙聯 und vor allem auch in unseren Mitgliedstaaten脗聽芒聙聯, n脙陇mlich von Steuerbeg脙录nstigungen. Das Ziel muss sein, Gewinne dort zu versteuern, wo sie auch anfallen.

Ja, und wie gesagt: Zu Tode gef脙录rchtet ist auch gestorben. Wir sollten uns nicht an Trump orientieren, sondern an einer richtig guten europ脙陇ischen Digitalsteuer.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Eero Hein脙陇luoma (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, with an effective paid tax much below 15脗聽%, digital platforms do not pay their fair share in the European Union, despite the profits being made here. This is totally unacceptable.脗聽Try to explain this to your local bakery, entrepreneur or teacher. People and companies who pay much more than the big technical giants.

We have tried to tackle this multilaterally. However, multilateral solutions seem to be only dreams these days. Therefore, in today's new geopolitical reality, the European Commission should step up and push again for a European Union digital service tax. In a world where our main trade partner threatens the Union, its citizens, its farmers, its workers and its factories with 50脗聽% tariffs on goods, this is the only fair response from our side. The Union and its citizens should stop being the financial milk cow of foreign digital platforms. It's time to act.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandra G脙鲁mez L脙鲁pez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, ayer Trump volvi脙鲁 a amenazar a Europa con reabrir la guerra comercial, porque sancionamos a Google, una gran empresa tecnol脙鲁gica norteamericana que bloquea la leg脙颅tima competencia de nuestros buscadores europeos.

Y todo eso, adem脙隆s, en el marco de una cena, donde los due脙卤os de las grandes plataformas cenaban con Trump mientras se jactaban de que aprietan, subyugan y someten a Europa a sus propios intereses.

Por eso, comisario, la tasa digital no solo es justicia fiscal, es tambi脙漏n soberan脙颅a europea. Es dignidad democr脙隆tica y es decirle a Trump que 脙漏l puede ponerle el mantel y servirle la cena a una mesa de millonarios, pero que a los europeos lo 脙潞nico que nos importa es que, cuando se levanten de la mesa, antes paguen la cuenta.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Claire Fita (S&D). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, plus de 90脗聽milliards d'euros: ce sont les b脙漏n脙漏fices de Google en 2024. En seulement seize secondes, Google encaisse ce que la moyenne des m脙漏nages europ脙漏ens met une ann脙漏e 脙聽 gagner. Les profits des g脙漏ants du num脙漏rique s'envolent tandis que les revenus des citoyens stagnent. Ce n'est pas un simple d脙漏s脙漏quilibre, c'est une injustice fiscale 脙聽 corriger absolument. Alors allons-y et allons-y maintenant.

Les 脙聣tats-Unis se retirent de l'accord fiscal de l'OCDE? Eh bien, l'Europe doit montrer l'exemple. Elle doit faire payer aux multinationales ce qu'elles doivent l脙聽 o脙鹿 elles font leurs profits. Oui 脙聽 une taxe europ脙漏enne sur les services num脙漏riques. Il s'agit de justice. Parce que chaque euro que Google, Amazon ou Meta ne paient pas, ce sont nos PME, nos agriculteurs, nos citoyens, nos services publics qui en supportent le co脙禄t.

La justice fiscale, c'est choisir une Europe souveraine contre l'Europe de l'impuissance.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Solicitudes incidentales de uso de la palabra (脗芦catch the eye脗禄)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, comisario McGrath, 脗驴se da usted cuenta de cu脙隆ntas veces se ha hablado en este Pleno del Parlamento Europeo 芒聙聰tambi脙漏n en el debate del Estado de la Uni脙鲁n芒聙聰 de las plataformas digitales y su modelo de negocio como problema europeo? Porque solo desde Europa puede hinc脙隆rsele el diente a esa oligarqu脙颅a que ha recibido el nombre de 脗芦tecnofeudalismo脗禄, que escapa al Derecho europeo si puede.

Por tanto, hay un problema no solamente de equidad fiscal, como se ha dicho, de justicia tributaria, que paguen la parte que les corresponda y lo hagan como pagamos todos los dem脙隆s contribuyentes: donde generan sus beneficios. No, hay un problema tambi脙漏n de lo que se ha denominado 芒聙聰no s脙漏 si con un l脙漏xico muy acertado芒聙聰 脗芦soberan脙颅a europea脗禄, el 脗芦Brussels Effect脗禄, la capacidad de la Uni脙鲁n Europea de imponer sus derechos a los gigantes de la red.

Pero, en tercer lugar, es un problema de credibilidad de la Uni脙鲁n Europea, m脙隆xime cuando despu脙漏s de haber hecho el rid脙颅culo en la cita del campo de golf escoc脙漏s, estrechando la mano de Trump para intentar aplacarlo, amenaza todav脙颅a con sanciones a quien se atreva a imponer fiscalidad justa a los gigantes tecnol脙鲁gicos de Silicon Valley.

Es completamente intolerable: la fiscalidad justa es una necesidad europea.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Fin de las intervenciones con arreglo al procedimiento de solicitud incidental de uso de la palabra (脗芦catch the eye脗禄))

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael McGrath, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, I would like to thank all of the honourable Members for their contribution to the debate this evening. It covered a number of issues that I am very familiar with, given my previous role as a finance minister in a Member State, Ireland, where I brought through the legislation to give effect to the EU Minimum Tax Directive, in effect giving expression to pillar two. So it is very interesting to hear all of the contributions this evening and I want to reiterate that the Commission remains a strong supporter of multilateralism. This is not a slogan, but a strategic imperative, and that is why we will continue to work constructively through the OECD and other fora to reach a balanced global agreement.

Our goal is to successfully conclude the pillar one discussions, and we are fully committed to making this happen. And as I said in my introductory remarks, the Commission expects that discussions on pillar one could resume later this year. If global consensus remains out of reach, we will collaborate closely with the European Parliament and Member States to determine next steps, and while work on pillar one is ongoing, it is too early to comment on possible alternatives to the OECD-led process. Our focus, as I said, is on giving that process sufficient space and time to deliver. The digital economy is a very important part of our economy, and we will ensure that the principles of fair taxation prevail here as well, and thank you all for your contributions to this important debate this evening.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much, Commissioner. The debate is closed.

Declaraciones por escrito (art脙颅culo 178 del Reglamento interno)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kinga Koll脙隆r (PPE), 脙颅r脙隆sban. 芒聙聯 A glob脙隆lis minimumad脙鲁r脙鲁l sz脙鲁l脙鲁 mai vita lehet脜聭s脙漏get ny脙潞jt arra, hogy konkr脙漏t p脙漏ld脙隆n kereszt脙录l mutassam meg, hogyan lett a FIDESZ az ad脙鲁emel脙漏sek korm脙隆nya Magyarorsz脙隆gon az elm脙潞lt 15 脙漏vben.

A glob脙隆lis nagyv脙隆llalkoz脙隆sokra kivetett minimumad脙鲁t az Orb脙隆n-korm脙隆ny minden eszk脙露zzel h脙隆tr脙隆ltatni pr脙鲁b脙隆lta, 脙隆m v脙漏g脙录l k脙漏t sz脙漏k k脙露z脙露tt a pad al脙隆 estek.

2022-ben ugyanis 脙漏ppen amiatt mondta fel az amerikai vezet脙漏s a Magyarorsz脙隆g 脙漏s az USA k脙露z脙露tti kett脜聭s ad脙鲁ztat脙隆s elker脙录l脙漏s脙漏t c脙漏lz脙鲁 egyezm脙漏nyt, mert Magyarorsz脙隆g akad脙隆lyozta a glob脙隆lis minimumad脙鲁 bevezet脙漏s脙漏t. 脙聧gy van ma minimum 15%-os ad脙鲁 a v脙隆llalkoz脙隆soknak 脙聣S 30%-os ad脙鲁 a befektet脜聭knek.

Az ad脙鲁egyezm脙漏ny hi脙隆nya rendk脙颅v脙录l k脙隆ros a magyar 脙漏s az amerikai befektet脜聭k sz脙隆m脙隆ra a nagyobb ad脙鲁teher miatt, mindez pedig versenyh脙隆tr脙隆nyt jelent a magyar v脙隆llalkoz脙隆soknak. Eml脙漏keztet脜聭脙录l: Magyarorsz脙隆gon az elm脙潞lt h脙隆rom 脙漏vben nem n脜聭tt a gazdas脙隆g - az ipar 12%-kal esett vissza, a beruh脙隆z脙隆sok t脙露bb mint 30%-kal. Vagyis Orb脙隆n ut脙鲁lag mindenk脙漏ppen feleslegesnek tekinthet脜聭 harca jelent脜聭s gazdas脙隆gi h脙隆tr脙隆nyt okozott haz脙隆nknak.

A gazdas脙隆g n脙露veked脙漏s脙漏nek egyik kulcsa a kisz脙隆m脙颅that脙鲁 脙漏s v脙隆llalkoz脙隆sokat t脙隆mogat脙鲁 ad脙鲁z脙隆si k脙露rnyezet. Ez脙漏rt a TISZA le fogja cs脙露kkenteni az ad脙鲁k sz脙隆m脙隆t 脙漏s ennek r脙漏sze lesz a magyar-amerikai kett脜聭s ad脙鲁ztat脙隆st elker脙录l脜聭 egyezm脙漏ny helyre脙隆ll脙颅t脙隆s脙隆hoz sz脙录ks脙漏ges t脙隆rgyal脙隆sok miel脜聭bbi megkezd脙漏se is.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Costas Mavrides, author. 芒聙聯 Mr President, this resolution is about five European citizens, Cypriot nationals that, while visiting their properties in the occupied area of Cyprus, were illegally detained and subjected to inhumane treatment by the occupying regime established and ruled by Turkey. I would like to thank my colleagues for their support in drafting this strong resolution, particularly with two references specifically.

First, in condemning the deliberate intimidation tactics by the occupying regime, such as this illegal detention aiming to terminate the criminal action by the Republic of Cyprus against usurpers of occupied Greek-Cypriot properties. We extend our full support to the Republic of Cyprus to enforce the EU rule of law. Second, we urge the European Commission and the External Service to take all necessary measures, including punitive ones, against Turkey, in order to secure their release. This morning, the Commission President talked about EU defence and security, but said nothing about Turkey and the occupation of EU territory in Cyprus. The credibility of EU defence and security is tested in Cyprus, and the Commission President failed in the eyes of many European citizens, including myself.

脗聽

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandro Gozi, author. 芒聙聯 Mr President, five脗聽EU脗聽citizens visited their own properties and, just for that, they were detained without credible evidence, denied medical care and even their lawyer was arrested. Three of them now have been released, but two are still held in unacceptable conditions, and we demand their immediate and unconditional release.

And where is this happening? In the territory of our Union, in northern Cyprus. T脙录rkiye still owns 37脗聽% of Cyprus under military occupation and has sole responsibility for the situation in the occupied areas.

As Renew Europe, we strongly condemn these arbitrary detentions and the appalling conditions imposed on our citizens. Fifty-one years after the Turkish invasion, intimidation and injustice continue and, the more democracy declines in T脙录rkiye 芒聙聯 where elected mayors are jailed 芒聙聯 the worse the situation becomes in Cyprus too. This is a blatant violation of international law and EU principles. We have a duty to protect our citizens and uphold our values.

We must therefore be absolutely clear: we will never accept intimidation and injustice or European soil.
